entities.name: Adler Theatre
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: Closed | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
A restored Art Deco jewel hosting Broadway tours, comedians, and live music.

The Adler's lobby stops you cold—soaring ceilings, geometric tile work, and that particular hush that old theaters hold. The main stage books the touring shows that skip smaller markets: Broadway productions, stand-up headliners, touring bands. It's the kind of venue where the bones of the building matter as much as what's on stage; you're sitting in 1927 craftsmanship while watching something made last year.

The Adler anchors Downtown Davenport's riverfront corridor, steps from the Mississippi and a natural pairing with dinner before or drinks after. It's one of those institutions that gets better the more you use it—locals know the sightlines from certain sections, which restaurants are worth the pre-show timing, how to actually get parking. Once you've caught a show here, it becomes part of how you think about the QC.

entities.name: Float Quad Cities
entities.hours: Monday: 9:45 AM – 7:35 PM | Tuesday: 9:45 AM – 7:35 PM | Wednesday: Closed | Thursday: Closed | Friday: 9:45 AM – 7:35 PM | Saturday: 9:45 AM – 7:35 PM | Sunday: 9:45 AM – 7:35 PM
entities.description:
Sensory deprivation tanks where an hour feels like a reset button.

Float Quad Cities sits quiet and intentional in Moline, offering what sounds abstract until you're actually floating—ninety minutes in body-temperature saltwater, no light, no sound, just you and the drift. The tanks themselves are clean and private, the staff unfussy about the whole experience, and regulars come back because something genuinely shifts after an hour alone with your thoughts. First-timers often emerge a little dazed, talking about how their shoulders didn't know they could relax like that.

Float sits in Moline, just far enough from downtown to feel like its own destination—the kind of place you go on purpose, not by accident. It's become a quiet cornerstone for anyone serious about stress relief or recovery, and the ratings speak to real, consistent care. Once you've floated here, you understand why it keeps showing up on the radar of people looking for something genuinely restorative in the QC.

entities.name: Quad City Botanical Center
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 5:00 PM
entities.description:
Forty acres of green space where the Mississippi Valley grows things year-round.

The Botanical Center sprawls across Rock Island like an open secret—indoor tropical rooms that smell like soil and humidity, outdoor gardens that shift with the seasons, and in winter, a miniature train that loops through poinsettias and twinkling lights. It's the kind of place where you can spend an hour wandering through a teaching greenhouse or sit on a bench and watch families hunt for hidden corners. The gift shop and library add layers; locals come back because there's always something blooming, even in February.

The Quad City Botanical Center sits on the Illinois side of the river in Rock Island, anchoring a quieter neighborhood that feels separate from downtown bustle but still accessible. It's the rare destination in the QC that works for everyone—a peaceful solo afternoon, a family outing, a place to reset when you need green and growing things around you. Once you know it's there, you'll find yourself returning with visiting friends.

entities.name: 11th Hour Bar & Grill
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM
entities.description:
A Silvis mainstay where the wings are crispy and everybody knows your name.

11th Hour has the comfortable, lived-in feel of a place that's been doing the same thing well for years — wood-paneled walls, a bar that runs the length of the room, and a kitchen that doesn't overcomplicate things. The wings are the draw; regulars will argue their preferred sauce while the bartender pours another round. Weekends fill up fast with a mix of locals, families on Saturday afternoons, and the after-work crowd that's been coming back since before anyone could check in on their phone.

11th Hour sits in Silvis, just off the main drag — the kind of neighborhood spot that anchors a community without needing to hustle for attention. With a 4.5-star rating across over 400 reviews, it's proven itself as one of those places where consistency matters more than trends. It's worth the short drive if you're looking for honest food, a strong pour, and the kind of unpretentious hospitality that keeps people coming back.

entities.name: 129 Coffee and Wine Bar
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 3:00 – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 3:00 – 10:00 PM | Friday: 3:00 – 11:00 PM | Saturday: 3:00 – 11:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Wine and coffee in Le Claire, where evenings stretch longer than you planned.

129 opens at 3 PM on weekdays—that sweet spot between afternoon and night—and the vibe shifts with the light. You can start with a careful pour-over and a book, then order a glass of something red as the sun drops and the room fills with people who clearly know each other. It's small and unhurried, the kind of place where the bartender remembers what you drank last time.

Le Claire's Main Street has quietly become one of the QC's most underrated stretches—a few blocks of genuine character just north of the riverfront, easy to reach but still feels slightly removed from the noise. 129 is exactly the kind of anchor spot that makes a neighborhood worth driving to: local, unpretentious, and uninterested in fitting into anyone's brand but its own.

entities.name: 392Caffe
entities.hours: Monday: 6:30 AM – 3:00 PM | Tuesday: 6:30 AM – 3:00 PM | Wednesday: 6:30 AM – 3:00 PM | Thursday: 6:30 AM – 3:00 PM | Friday: 6:30 AM – 3:00 PM | Saturday: 7:00 AM – 3:00 PM | Sunday: 8:00 AM – 3:00 PM
entities.description:
Italian café culture transplanted to downtown Davenport's corner row.

392Caffe feels like you've stepped into a Roman neighborhood spot—all espresso-stained counters, Italian pastries in the case, and a rhythm that rewards regulars. The morning crowd is serious about their coffee; the lunch crew comes for panini and sandwiches that taste like someone actually knows what they're doing. The hours are honest (closing at 3 PM most days), which means you're eating breakfast or lunch here, not scrolling on your laptop at 4 PM.

Sitting on Downtown Davenport's main stretch, 392Caffe anchors the neighborhood's slow shift toward places that care about craft and execution. The 4.7-star rating across 700+ reviews isn't accident—it's the kind of consistent, word-of-mouth success that tells you locals have figured out what the rest of us are just discovering. Once you've had a proper espresso here, it changes your mornings.

entities.name: 7 Brew Coffee
entities.hours: Monday: 5:30 AM – 10:00 PM | Tuesday: 5:30 AM – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 5:30 AM – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 5:30 AM – 10:00 PM | Friday: 5:30 AM – 11:00 PM | Saturday: 5:30 AM – 11:00 PM | Sunday: 5:30 AM – 10:00 PM
entities.description:
Quick specialty coffee and cold brew that opens before dawn.

7 Brew opens at 5:30 AM on weekdays — early enough to catch you before your commute or your run. The ordering is fast and the drinks are built with care: cold brew that's smooth and balanced, espresso drinks that don't taste rushed. It's the kind of place where you're in and out in five minutes, but the coffee tastes like someone actually paid attention.

Tucked on Middle Road in Bettendorf, 7 Brew fits the rhythm of the east side — convenient, reliable, and open late enough (11 PM on weekends) to swing by after dinner or work. It's a chain, yes, but it's one that's earned its loyal following across the Quad Cities, and for good reason: consistency matters when you're grabbing your fourth coffee of the week.

entities.name: 9Round Kickboxing Fitness
entities.hours: Monday: Open 24 hours | Tuesday: Open 24 hours | Wednesday: Open 24 hours | Thursday: Open 24 hours | Friday: Open 24 hours | Saturday: Open 24 hours | Sunday: Open 24 hours
entities.description:
Nine stations, nine minutes per round, pure adrenaline.

9Round is built on a simple premise: punch, kick, and move through nine different stations in rapid succession, no class schedule required. Walk in whenever it fits your day — 3 a.m. or 3 p.m. — and a trainer will pair you with a heavy bag, speed bag, or focus mitts. It's the kind of workout that feels less like fitness and more like learning to fight, which is probably why people keep coming back.

Tucked into Bettendorf's Middle Road corridor, 9Round fills a real gap in the QC's fitness scene. It's open around the clock, which means whether you're an early riser, night shift worker, or just restless at midnight, there's no excuse to skip it. The gym shows up consistently on our guides because it's one of those places where locals are genuinely invested — the kind of spot that builds a real community among its members.

entities.name: Abernathy's
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 3:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Downtown's best-curated gallery shop for art, design, and purposeful objects.

Abernathy's feels like walking into someone's very good taste — the kind of person who knows the difference between a print and a piece, and isn't afraid to stock both. The space is thoughtfully arranged without feeling precious: local art on the walls, carefully chosen books and design objects on shelves, the kind of things that make you stop and actually look. It's a place where you can spend twenty minutes or two hours, and either way you'll leave with something that matters.

Abernathy's anchors a stretch of Downtown Davenport that's quietly become the QC's creative heart. It sits near galleries, studios, and other independent shops — the kind of block worth a whole afternoon of wandering. It's exactly the place we keep coming back to when we're looking for somewhere that feels genuinely, locally made: no corporate polish, just real curatorial work.

entities.name: Airbrushed Sunless LLC
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 5:00 PM
entities.description:
Custom airbrush tans that look like you actually spent time outside.

Airbrushed Sunless has built a loyal following on one thing: making spray tans look natural, not orange. The technicians here take time to match your skin tone and customize coverage — you're not getting the one-size-fits-all spray. Walk in pale in February, walk out looking like you spent a weekend at the river without the UV damage or the commitment of a tanning bed.

Airbrushed Sunless sits right downtown, in the same neighborhood as the coffee shops and restaurants worth your time. It's the kind of place that accumulates five-star reviews because people actually come back, season after season, which says everything about the care and consistency happening in that chair.

entities.name: AJ Nails & Spa
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Moline's go-to nail spot with a loyal following and a relaxed vibe.

AJ Nails & Spa does the fundamentals well — clean stations, steady hands, and the kind of consistency that keeps people coming back. The space has that comfortable, lived-in feel of a neighborhood favorite, not a sterile chain, and the team works unhurried even when the chairs are full. Whether you're in for a quick gel or a full spa afternoon, there's no pressure and no upsell — just good work at a fair price.

AJ sits in Moline, where the best local businesses tend to thrive quietly without fanfare. It's exactly the kind of place worth knowing about — the salon your coworker has been going to for three years, the spot you'll keep returning to once you've found it. That 4.6 Google rating isn't accident; it's the mark of a place that shows up consistently for its community.

entities.description: Storied family-owned Italian parlor with an array of pizza toppings, crusts & styles, plus calzones.

entities.name: AL’s Burgers
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM
entities.description:
Griddle-pressed burgers and a counter where regulars know their order by heart.

Al's does the fundamentals right — beef that's seasoned proper, buns that are toasted, toppings that don't slide around. The small counter feels like it's been in the same spot for decades (because it has), and there's a rhythm to the place: the sizzle of the griddle, the quick banter with the crew, the satisfaction of a burger that tastes exactly like it should. Lunch crowds pile in, but the pace never feels rushed.

Al's sits in Rock Island, one of the QC's most grounded neighborhoods — the kind of place where the good food spots stick around because people actually eat there, not because of hype. It's the burger standard that locals measure other burgers against, and it shows up again and again in conversations about what makes the QC's food scene honest and real. Once you've been, you'll understand why.

entities.name: Anna's Nail Salon
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Davenport nail salon where regulars return for careful work and honest service.

Anna's keeps a calm, focused room—no rushed assembly line feel here. The technicians take their time with each person, and you'll notice the same faces coming back week after week, which says everything about consistency in a place where that matters. Walk in on a Saturday afternoon and you'll see chairs full of people who know exactly what they're getting.

Anna's sits in Davenport, accessible and unpretentious—the kind of neighborhood spot that builds trust slowly and keeps it by doing the fundamentals right. It's the sort of place that fills our guides because it's where real people actually go, not because of marketing, but because the work holds up and the experience feels genuine.

entities.name: Ansan Nails & Spa
entities.hours: Monday: 12:00 – 7:00 PM | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Moline's go-to for careful manicures and a calm afternoon escape.

Ansan has built a steady reputation on consistency—the kind of place where regulars book months ahead and newcomers leave impressed. The room is quiet and focused, without the chaos of bigger chains; you'll see the same technicians working methodically, attention on detail. Whether you're after a simple polish or an intricate design, the work holds up.

Ansan sits in Moline, just far enough from downtown to feel like a neighborhood spot rather than a destination—but worth the short drive from anywhere in the QC. It's the kind of reliable, unpretentious place that shows up again and again in locals' rotation, and once you've experienced the care they take, you'll understand why.

entities.name: Antique & Treasure Trove
entities.description:
A sprawling Moline antique shop where the hunt matters more than the haul.

Antique & Treasure Trove is the kind of place where you lose track of time — narrow aisles lined with mismatched furniture, glass cases crowded with vintage jewelry and collectibles, and shelves that go deeper than they look from the front. You might find a mid-century lamp next to Depression glass, or a stack of old vinyl beside hand-painted cast iron. There's no particular logic to the layout, which is exactly the point — the real pleasure is wandering and discovering.

Sitting in Moline, Antique & Treasure Trove appeals to the QC browsers and collectors who enjoy the tactile experience of digging through actual inventory rather than scrolling through a screen. It's the kind of shop that rewards repeat visits, since stock shifts and new finds keep appearing in corners you could've sworn you'd checked. If you're the type who enjoys a good treasure hunt, this belongs on your route.

entities.name: Antique Archaeology Le Claire
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 4:00 PM
entities.description:
Vintage Americana and salvage treasures in a Le Claire landmark.

Walking into Antique Archaeology feels like stepping into someone's carefully curated obsession — the kind of place where a 1940s neon sign hangs next to reclaimed barn wood next to a stack of old cast-iron skillets. You'll lose time here, moving between rooms of industrial finds, vintage signage, and oddball collectibles that somehow all make sense together. Whether you're hunting for a specific piece or just wandering, there's real treasure mixed in with the browsing.

Antique Archaeology sits right in Le Claire's walkable stretch — the kind of town where you can spend an afternoon moving between shops without ever feeling rushed. It's become a pillar of the area's character, drawing regulars who know exactly what they're after and first-timers who just want to see what catches their eye. Once you've been, it's easy to see why it keeps showing up on lists of places worth the drive.

entities.name: Antojitos Mexican Restaurant
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 1:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:30 AM – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 10:30 AM – 8:00 PM | Friday: 10:30 AM – 8:00 PM | Saturday: 10:30 AM – 8:00 PM | Sunday: 10:30 AM – 8:00 PM
entities.description:
Rock Island's best-kept spot for handmade Mexican comfort food.

Antojitos does the fundamentals right — fresh tortillas, properly seasoned beans, the kind of chile relleno that tastes like someone's abuela is in the kitchen. The room is unpretentious; you're here for the food, not the ambiance. Orders arrive hot and generous, and regulars come back because consistency matters, especially with something as personal as home cooking.

Antojitos sits in Rock Island, a neighborhood with real neighborhood bones — the kind of place where a restaurant's reputation spreads by word of mouth, not Instagram. With a 4.8 rating built on 51 careful reviews, it's exactly the kind of locally-loved spot we keep coming back to. Once you've had it, you'll understand why it stays full on weekends.

entities.name: Argrow's House Cafe and Gift Shop
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
A historic home-turned-cafe serving lunch and local gifts, Wednesday through Saturday.

Argrow's House feels like visiting a friend's living room — because it literally is one. The cafe operates out of a genuine Davenport home, where you can settle into vintage furniture with a sandwich and coffee while browsing handpicked gifts in the next room. The limited hours (Wednesday through Saturday, 9 to 2) mean it rewards the locals who know when to show up, creating an intimate atmosphere that can't be rushed or scaled up.

Argrow's House sits in Davenport's quieter residential pockets, away from the riverfront hustle — the kind of place you find by word-of-mouth or stumbling around a neighborhood you thought you knew. It's exactly the sort of unexpected gem that makes a community guide useful: a spot with real character that doesn't announce itself, where the experience of simply being there matters more than what's on the menu.

entities.name: Armored Gardens
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM
entities.description:
A sprawling beer garden where the whole QC comes to unwind.

Armored Gardens is built for lingering — long picnic tables, a rotating tap list that takes beer seriously, and enough room that you're never fighting for elbows. Friday and Saturday nights stretch past midnight, and the crowd shifts from after-work regulars to late-night groups looking for somewhere louder than a bar, more social than a brewery. The space has that rare quality of feeling both energetic and relaxed, the kind of place where you can catch up with friends or watch the night happen around you.

Armored Gardens sits in Downtown Davenport, the neighborhood that's become the QC's gathering place — steps from the riverfront and surrounded by the restaurants and galleries that have revitalized this part of the city. It's a cornerstone spot that shows up on every local's shortlist of where to spend a Friday night, and once you've posted up at one of those tables, you'll understand why it keeps people coming back.

entities.name: Aroma Coffee & Cafe
entities.hours: Monday: 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Tuesday: 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Thursday: 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Friday: 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Saturday: 7:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Sunday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM
entities.description:
Davenport spot where coffee quality and genuine hospitality actually match.

Aroma feels like the kind of place that's been doing the same thing well for long enough that they've stopped needing to prove anything. The espresso is solid, the pastries rotate with care, and the people behind the counter remember your order. Mornings fill with the expected regulars and the occasional newcomer drawn in by word-of-mouth; afternoons slow into a quieter rhythm where you can actually think.

Aroma sits in Davenport proper, anchoring a corner of the city that rewards the kind of wandering that leads to real discoveries. It's the sort of reliable, unpretentious spot that locals return to week after week — not because it's Instagram-worthy, but because consistency and kindness still matter. Once you've found it, you'll understand why it keeps showing up in conversations about where to actually spend your morning in the QC.
