entities.name: Atomic Coffee Bar
entities.hours: Monday: 5:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Tuesday: 5:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Wednesday: 5:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 5:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Friday: 5:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Saturday: 6:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Sunday: 6:00 AM – 8:00 PM
entities.description:
A tiny counter pulling dialed espresso shots before most of Davenport wakes up.

Atomic opens at 5 a.m. on weekdays — earlier than almost anywhere else in the QC — and the regulars know it. The space is small enough that you're watching the barista work, and they're watching you decide between a cortado and a flat white. There's a hum of purpose here: people grabbing their shot before the commute, remote workers settling in with a pour-over, the kind of quiet focus you only find in places that take coffee seriously.

Atomic sits in Davenport's District, steps from the riverfront and the cluster of bars and restaurants that define the neighborhood's evening character. It's the rare QC spot that anchors both the early-morning crowd and the afternoon laptop brigade, which is why it keeps showing up on our guides — places that feel essential to how locals actually move through their days.

entities.name: Augustana's Fryxell Geology Museum
entities.description:
A quiet geology museum where rocks tell the story of the Mississippi Valley.

Fryxell sits on Augustana's campus like a secret—the kind of place locals forget about until they remember it matters. Inside, you're surrounded by specimens that actually speak to where you live: fossils pulled from Illinois bedrock, minerals that glint under careful museum lighting, and displays that explain why the river carved this valley the way it did. It's small enough to explore in an hour, thoughtful enough to keep you there longer.

Fryxell Geology Museum lives on Augustana's Rock Island campus, a pocket of the QC that rewards wandering. It's the kind of place that doesn't announce itself loudly—no marquee, no heavy foot traffic—but once you know it's there, it becomes one of those essential stops that rounds out an afternoon. It belongs in our guides because it's genuine: a working museum that serves students and curious locals alike, built on real expertise and the kind of specificity that makes the Quad Cities feel less like any town and more like *your* town.

entities.name: Aunt Bea's Cafe
entities.hours: Monday: 8:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Tuesday: 8:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Wednesday: 8:00 AM – 3:00 PM | Thursday: 8:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Friday: 8:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Saturday: 8:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Home cooking that tastes like someone actually cares about lunch.

Aunt Bea's keeps the kind of hours that suggest this isn't a concept — it's a neighborhood fixture. The menu reads like a conversation with someone's grandmother: sandwiches built on real bread, soups that change with what's available, the kind of sides that make you realize most places skip the small details. The 4.6-star consistency across 230+ reviews isn't accident; it's what happens when a place stays focused on doing one thing — feeding people well — and doesn't get distracted.

Aunt Bea's anchors Rock Island's quieter corner, the kind of spot locals already know and visitors stumble onto with genuine luck. It's close enough to the riverfront and island attractions to slide naturally into a day exploring, but far enough removed that it feels like a real discovery rather than another tourist stop. Once you've been, you'll understand why it keeps showing up in the routines of people who live here.

entities.name: Awake Coffee Company
entities.hours: Monday: 6:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Tuesday: 6:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Wednesday: 6:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Thursday: 6:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Friday: 6:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Saturday: 6:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Sunday: 6:00 AM – 6:00 PM
entities.description:
Early-opening Davenport spot keeping the neighborhood caffeinated since dawn.

Awake opens at 6 AM every day, which means you can count on it before most of the QC is awake. The space has that lived-in feel of a place that's become a reliable anchor for the morning crowd—regulars know their order, the staff knows their faces. It's straightforward coffee done right, the kind of spot where consistency matters more than flash.

Awake sits in Davenport's working rhythm, the kind of neighborhood coffee shop that serves the people who actually live nearby rather than chasing Instagram moments. It's the place you return to because it's there, it's open, and it gets the job done—exactly the kind of local fixture we look for when building our guides to the real QC.

entities.name: Awaken Beauty
entities.description:
A Moline wellness studio where beauty treatments feel like self-care rituals.

Awaken Beauty treats facials and skincare like they're meant to be—unhurried and intentional. The space has that rare quality of feeling both calm and energizing, the kind of place where you actually turn your phone off without thinking about it. Whether you're in for a facial, waxing, or something more specialized, the attention to detail suggests the staff genuinely cares whether you leave feeling better than when you walked in.

Awaken Beauty sits in Moline, a neighborhood that's quietly becoming a destination for the QC's wellness-minded folks. It's the kind of spot that earns a perfect rating and keeps it—word travels fast when a place does one thing really well. Once you've experienced it, you'll understand why it shows up on our radar as a place worth your time.

entities.name: Azteca 1 Mexican Restaurant
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM
entities.description:
Family-run cantina where margaritas flow and the murals are almost as colorful as the crowd.

Azteca 1 has the kind of energy that builds as the sun sets — it starts as a lunch spot for regulars and transforms into the place where Davenport comes to unwind with strong drinks and real food. The murals wrap around the dining room in bright blues and golds, and there's always someone at the bar ordering another round. The margaritas are generous, the carne asada doesn't cut corners, and the room has that lived-in warmth that comes from decades of the same family running the same restaurant.

Azteca 1 sits in Davenport and has earned its reputation the slow way — 951 reviews on Google, a 4.4 on Yelp, and a loyal base that keeps coming back. It's the kind of reliable, unpretentious gathering spot that anchors a neighborhood, and if you're looking for where actual Quad Citizens spend their Friday and Saturday nights, you'll find them here.

entities.name: Backwater Gamblers Ski Site
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 6:00 – 8:30 PM | Thursday: Closed | Friday: Closed | Saturday: Closed | Sunday: 6:00 – 8:30 PM
entities.description:
Local ski club keeping River City's summer traditions alive on the water.

Wednesday and Sunday evenings, Backwater Gamblers fires up the tow line on the Rock Island riverfront — a small, devoted crew of water skiers and wakeboarders who've been at it for decades. It's not fancy; it's intentional. You'll see the same faces season after season, the kind of people who'd rather spend a July evening getting pulled behind a boat than anywhere else. The club has the feel of something genuinely local, built by people who love the sport more than they care about turning a profit.

Backwater Gamblers sits on the Rock Island side of the Mississippi, part of the QC's quieter recreational backbone — the kind of place that doesn't make it into glossy tourism guides but absolutely should. It's worth seeking out if you're a skier yourself or just curious about how the Quad Cities' most dedicated enthusiasts spend their summers. Once you know it exists, you realize it's been here all along, doing its thing.

entities.name: Bakehouse
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 6:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Wednesday: 6:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Thursday: 6:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Friday: 6:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Saturday: 8:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Sunday: 8:00 AM – 2:00 PM
entities.description:
Serious baked goods and coffee in a stripped-down East Moline corner.

Bakehouse opens early — 6 a.m. on weekdays — and the smell hits you before you're through the door. The cases hold croissants that actually shatter, sourdough that's been allowed to proof properly, and the kind of pastries that make you understand why people plan their mornings around a bakery. It's minimal and no-frills, which means the focus lands entirely on what's behind the counter: flour, butter, time, and skill.

Bakehouse sits in East Moline, just far enough from downtown to feel like a neighborhood secret, but worth the short drive from anywhere in the QC. It's the kind of place that builds a devoted following quietly — people who know that real bread and pastry work don't announce themselves with marketing, just with results. Once you've had their croissant, you'll understand why it keeps showing up on lists of the best things to eat in the region.

entities.name: Ballet Quad Cities
entities.description:
Professional ballet on the Rock Island stage, teaching and performing year-round.

Ballet Quad Cities brings the discipline and beauty of classical dance to the riverfront. Whether you're catching a mainstage production or watching technique unfold in one of their studios, there's a real sense of craft here — dancers who've trained seriously, choreography that matters, and a company committed to keeping ballet alive in the QC rather than letting it drift to larger cities. The school feeds the company; the company inspires the students. It's a small ecosystem, but it's functional and honest.

Rock Island's arts scene runs deeper than most people realize, and Ballet Quad Cities is proof of that. It sits alongside other cultural anchors in a neighborhood that's quietly become one of the QC's most interesting pockets — walkable, historic, and genuinely invested in arts and culture. If you care about what your community makes and believes in, this is a place worth supporting and experiencing.

entities.name: Bally's Quad Cities Casino & Hotel
entities.hours: Monday: Open 24 hours | Tuesday: Open 24 hours | Wednesday: Open 24 hours | Thursday: Open 24 hours | Friday: Open 24 hours | Saturday: Open 24 hours | Sunday: Open 24 hours
entities.description:
A full-throttle casino hotel where the energy never stops, day or night.

Bally's sits right on the Rock Island waterfront, all glass and bright lights—the kind of place that hums 24/7 whether you're there at noon or 3 a.m. The casino floor is thick with slot machines and table games, the restaurants run the gamut from quick bites to sit-down dinners, and if you need to stay over, the modern rooms and indoor pool mean you can make an actual weekend of it. It's unapologetically a destination unto itself, not a quiet corner—but that's exactly the point.

Bally's anchors Rock Island's riverfront, a major draw that brings visitors from across the region and beyond. It's the kind of anchor institution that shows up in any serious conversation about QC nightlife and entertainment, and whether you're a casual player, a diner, or someone looking for a full getaway without leaving town, it belongs in the mix.

entities.name: Barrel House
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM
entities.description:
A straightforward pub where Davenport comes to eat, drink, and stay awhile.

Barrel House operates with the confidence of a place that doesn't need to try too hard — wood-heavy interior, a solid bar program, and food that leans comfort without pretension. The crowd shifts with the clock: lunch is local business types and regulars on stools, evening brings families, and weekends get properly lively. It's the kind of establishment where the bartenders know what you drink by your third visit.

Barrel House anchors uptown Davenport, that stretch of the city that's quietly become one of the QC's most reliable neighborhoods for a night out. It sits within easy reach of other spots worth your time, making it natural to build an evening around — grab dinner here, walk next door for a drink somewhere else, or stay put if the vibe's right. It's a place that shows up in locals' regular rotation for good reason: consistent, unpretentious, and genuinely welcoming.

entities.name: Bass Street Chop House
entities.hours: Monday: 4:00 – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 4:00 – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 4:00 – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 4:00 – 9:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Sunday: 4:00 – 9:00 PM
entities.description:
White-tablecloth steaks and bourbon in the heart of Moline.

Bass Street Chop House does the traditional steakhouse thing—the kind of place where the bartender knows their whiskey collection better than most people know their own kitchen. Dark wood, leather booths, and that specific hum of conversation that only happens when people are eating expensive beef. Friday and Saturday lunches bring the business crowd; weeknight dinners feel more intentional, like you've planned something.

Bass Street sits in Moline, a neighborhood with real dining depth that doesn't always get the attention it deserves. It's the kind of established restaurant that shows up again and again on our guides because locals actually go there—not for Instagram moments, but for a solid prime rib and a pour of something good. Once you've been, you'll understand why it's become a fixture.

entities.name: Beef + Bourbon Chophouse
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 4:00 – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 4:00 – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 4:00 – 9:00 PM | Friday: 4:00 – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 4:00 – 9:00 PM | Sunday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM
entities.description:
High-end steakhouse where the bourbon list rivals the dry-aged beef.

Beef + Bourbon hits that sweet spot between special-occasion serious and approachable — dark wood and leather booths that feel built for long dinners, a bourbon selection that goes deep (not just the obvious names), and steaks that arrive properly seared and rested. The kitchen knows what it's doing with a ribeye, and the sides — creamed corn, loaded potatoes — don't pretend to be anything fancier than perfect versions of themselves. It's the kind of place where you linger over dessert and your second pour without feeling rushed.

Beef + Bourbon sits in Bettendorf, where solid dining options cluster near the I-74 corridor but this one stands apart for its genuine craft and polish. It's become a reliable choice for the QC when you want to mark an occasion — anniversary, promotion, client dinner — without leaving town, and the consistent high ratings speak to a kitchen that takes pride in execution. Once you've had dinner here, it gets added to your mental rotation of places that remind you the Quad Cities has real depth when you're looking for it.

entities.name: Bella Salon
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 8:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
A Davenport salon where the stylists actually listen and the results show.

Bella's got the kind of reputation that follows you — walk in and you'll see why regulars book months out. The stylists here treat your hair like it matters, whether you're coming in for a trim, color, or a complete refresh. Saturday mornings fill up fast, so plan ahead, but the wait is worth it; people come back because they trust what happens in the chair.

Bella sits in Davenport, accessible and reliable in a way that makes it easy to make it part of your routine. With a 4.9 rating across nearly 650 reviews, this is the kind of neighborhood spot that builds its reputation quietly, one good haircut at a time — the places we keep coming back to are the ones that show up on our guides.

entities.name: Ben Butterworth Parkway West
entities.hours: Monday: 6:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Tuesday: 6:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Wednesday: 6:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Thursday: 6:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Friday: 6:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Saturday: 6:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Sunday: 6:00 AM – 11:00 PM
entities.description:
Fifty acres along the river where Moline slows down and breathes.

Ben Butterworth Parkway West stretches generously along the water's edge, with a fishing pier that extends into the Mississippi like an invitation. The paved trails are wide enough for walkers, runners, and families on bikes without anyone feeling crowded—even on Saturday mornings. There's a boat launch if you've got one ready, picnic shelters if you're planning ahead, and a playground where kids actually want to spend time, but the real draw is the simple fact of space: open green, river views, and the kind of quiet that feels rare in a city.

Ben Butterworth sits on Moline's riverfront, part of the QC's best-kept secret—that the Mississippi is genuinely walkable and beautiful here, not just a backdrop. It's the kind of park that anchors a neighborhood, where regulars know the best benches and which trails catch the light at golden hour. Once you've spent an afternoon here, you'll understand why it shows up on our guides again and again.

entities.name: Bend XPO
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 6:00 AM – 3:00 AM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Bend XPO is a modern, customizable event space in the heart of East Moline's Bend Campus — a 132-acre
    neighborhood with hotels, restaurants, and entertainment all within walking distance. 100,000 square feet of
  flexible indoor and outdoor space for conferences, concerts, weddings, and community events.

entities.name: Bent River Brewing Company
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 3:00 – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 3:00 – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 3:00 – 9:00 PM | Friday: 3:00 – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 3:00 – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 9:00 PM
entities.description:
Local brewery where Rock Island gathers for honest beer and live music.

Bent River's tasting room has the feel of a place that grew from genuine craft rather than a business plan — concrete floors, modest lighting, and a bar built for conversation. The beers are clean and approachable, the kind you can taste through, and on weekends when live music spills into the space, it becomes the neighborhood's living room. Come early on a Saturday, grab a stool, and you'll likely find the people who actually built this place pouring your beer.

Bent River sits in Rock Island, a neighborhood that's quietly becoming the QC's brewing anchor — walkable, unpretentious, and serious about beer without the performance. It's the kind of local spot that rewards repeat visits; once you've found your way there on a Friday night, you'll keep coming back.

entities.name: Bespoke Beauty and Body
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: Closed | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Custom skincare and wellness treatments built around your actual skin.

Bespoke Beauty and Body takes the guesswork out of skincare — the kind of place where they ask real questions before recommending anything. The space feels intentional and calm, the kind of quiet corner where you actually have time to talk through what your skin needs instead of being rushed through a transaction. Whether you're dealing with chronic dryness, sensitivity, or just want facials that actually work, the team here treats it like a puzzle worth solving.

Tucked into the Village of East Davenport, Bespoke Beauty and Body is one of those locally-loved spots that builds its reputation through word-of-mouth and consistent results — 139 five-star reviews tell you something. It's the kind of place that rewards exploring the quieter neighborhoods of Davenport, where you find real expertise instead of chain-store generics. Once you've been, you'll likely be back.

entities.name: Bettendorf Family YMCA
entities.hours: Monday: 4:30 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 4:30 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 4:30 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 4:30 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 4:30 AM – 8:00 PM | Saturday: 6:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Sunday: 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM
entities.description:
A serious gym with actual community roots, open before dawn.

The Bettendorf Y opens at 4:30 AM on weekdays — early enough for the pre-work crowd that fills the cardio floor while it's still dark outside. The equipment is well-maintained and the pool draws families on weekends, but what sets it apart is the lack of pretense: this is where neighbors actually know each other's names, where the front desk staff remember if you've been gone a week, where fitness feels like an obligation to yourself rather than a performance for Instagram.

The Bettendorf Family YMCA sits in Bettendorf proper, anchoring the community's east side with the kind of staying power that matters in a town that values consistency. Unlike the boutique gyms and flashy fitness chains that come and go, this is the place families have cycled through for years — kids learning to swim, parents finding their routine, older members keeping their strength. It belongs in our guides because it's genuinely local in a way that feels increasingly rare.

entities.name: Beyond Genetics
entities.hours: Monday: 6:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Tuesday: 6:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Wednesday: 6:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 6:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Friday: 6:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Saturday: 6:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Personal training that actually listens to what your body needs.

Beyond Genetics opens early — 6 a.m. early — which means you can build a real routine before work. The trainers here aren't running you through a template; they're reading your movement, asking questions about old injuries, and adjusting on the fly. It's the kind of place where people stay for years because they're actually getting stronger, not just sore.

Beyond Genetics sits in Park Hill, a neighborhood that's becoming one of Moline's most active pockets — close enough to downtown to grab coffee after a session, but far enough to feel like a real community space. It's the kind of gym that shows up on local recommendation lists again and again because the people who train there genuinely want to send their friends, and that word-of-mouth loyalty says everything.

entities.name: BiBimBap Korean and Thai cuisine
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 2:30 PM, 5:00 – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 2:30 PM, 5:00 – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 2:30 PM, 5:00 – 9:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 2:30 PM, 5:00 – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM
entities.description:
Korean and Thai cooking that rewards the adventurous eater without pretense.

BiBimBap does what it says — the bibimbap arrives in a sizzling stone bowl, rice crackling against the sides while you stir in egg, vegetables, and your choice of protein. But the menu doesn't stop there: the pad thai carries real heat, the Korean fried chicken arrives properly crunchy, and the banchan (side dishes) keep appearing without fanfare. It's the kind of place where regulars know to order the lunch specials and off-menu items, and newcomers leave planning their next visit.

Sitting in Bettendorf, BiBimBap fills a real gap in the QC's dining landscape — accessible, honest food that doesn't require a trip to the Des Moines or Chicago suburbs. The lunch crowds and strong Google ratings speak to how thoroughly locals have embraced it. It's become one of those restaurants that shows up again and again on our guides because it actually delivers on what it promises.

entities.name: Bierstube Moline
entities.description:
A waterfront beer hall where German tradition meets live music and river views.

Bierstube feels like the kind of place that's been doing the same thing well for decades — because it has. The beer list runs deep (German imports share shelf space with local craft), and the kitchen leans into hearty schnitzel and bratwurst that tastes like someone's grandmother had a say. Most nights there's live music drifting through the wood-paneled room, and the crowd is mixed: families early, regulars by the bar, couples claiming the window tables overlooking the Mississippi.

Bierstube sits right on the water in Moline, part of a riverfront strip that's become one of the QC's most underrated stretches. It's the kind of establishment that anchors a neighborhood — unpretentious, consistent, genuinely good at what it does — and it shows up on every serious local's list of places that actually matter.

entities.name: Bix Beiderbecke Memorial Society
entities.description:
A quiet shrine to jazz history and one of Davenport's greatest sons.

The Bix Beiderbecke Memorial Society sits like a keeper of secrets in downtown Davenport — a small, focused space devoted to the cornet player who changed jazz forever before dying at 28. Walking in, you're surrounded by artifacts, photographs, and recordings that tell the story of a local kid who became a legend: Bix's handwritten notes, period instruments, the details of a life cut short. It's the kind of place that rewards slow looking and deeper listening.

The society anchors a growing appreciation for Davenport's musical legacy, sitting within walking distance of the riverfront and other cultural stops downtown. If you're serious about jazz, or curious about what made this corner of Iowa matter to American music, this is essential — a small room that holds an outsized story.

entities.name: Bix Beiderbecke Museum and Archives
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
The shrine to Davenport's jazz prodigy and the sound that defined an era.

Walk into the Bix Beiderbecke Museum and you're stepping into the life of a cornet player who burned bright and brief — a Davenport kid who became a legend before he turned 28. The archive holds his instruments, recordings, photographs, and the kind of granular details that obsessive jazz fans live for: you can hear the particular ache in his tone, see the sheet music he marked up, understand why musicians still argue about whether anyone's ever played it better. It's a small, focused museum, but it doesn't feel cramped — it feels like a conversation with someone who knew exactly what he wanted to say.

The Bix Museum sits in Downtown Davenport, steps from the riverfront and the other cultural anchors that make this neighborhood worth an afternoon. Bix Beiderbecke is Davenport's most famous son, and this museum is where his story lives — not as a tourist attraction, but as a genuine archive tended by people who care deeply. If you're into jazz, American music history, or just the kind of hyper-local cultural institutions that reveal what a place actually values, this one belongs on your list.

entities.name: Bix Bistro
entities.hours: Monday: 7:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 7:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 7:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 7:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 7:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 7:00 AM – 9:00 PM
entities.description: Stylish, contemporary restaurant with modern American cuisine, plus a cocktail lounge.

entities.name: Black Hawk Forest
entities.hours: Monday: 7:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Tuesday: 7:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 7:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Friday: 7:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 7:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 7:00 AM – 10:00 PM
entities.description:
Thick woods and quiet trails where Rock Island's wild side still shows.

Black Hawk Forest sprawls across Rock Island like a secret the city keeps to itself — dense enough that you forget the roads are close by. The trails wind through mature hardwoods and past small clearings, and on a weekday morning you might have the whole place to yourself. It's the kind of green space that locals actually use, not just pass by, and the 7 AM opening means you can catch the woods before anyone else arrives.

Black Hawk Forest sits in Rock Island, a neighborhood with real character and genuine outdoor bones — close enough to downtown to reach in minutes, but far enough away to feel like actual escape. It's the kind of place that belongs on our guides because it's where the QC shows you what it was before the strip malls and highways, and reminds you why people chose to build a city here in the first place.

entities.name: Black Hawk State Historic Site
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 12:00 PM, 1:00 – 4:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 12:00 PM, 1:00 – 4:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 12:00 PM, 1:00 – 4:00 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 12:00 PM, 1:00 – 4:00 PM | Sunday: 9:00 AM – 12:00 PM, 1:00 – 4:00 PM
entities.description:
Sacred ground and prairie where Sauk history runs deep.

Black Hawk State Historic Site sits on land the Sauk people called home for centuries—you can feel that continuity walking the rolling trails and open meadows. The visitor center holds artifacts and stories that ground you in the region's indigenous past, and the landscape itself is the real teacher: native prairie, oak savanna, and views that haven't changed in the ways that matter. It's quiet most weekdays, which means you might have the place almost to yourself.

Black Hawk is perched on Rock Island's eastern edge, far enough from downtown to feel like a real retreat but close enough for a morning or afternoon visit. It's one of those places the Quad Cities often overlooks—no crowds, no gift shop upsell—but it's exactly where locals take visiting friends who want to understand what this land actually is, and was.

entities.name: Boba Tea House
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 6:30 PM
entities.description:
Handmade boba and bright teas in a Davenport spot that feels like a real discovery.

Boba Tea House makes their tapioca pearls in-house—you can watch the small batches roll—and it changes everything about the drink. The tea selection leans classic and clean (no syrupy shortcuts), and regulars swap recommendations the way coffee people do about roasts. The room itself is modest and unpretentious, the kind of place where you're as likely to see high schoolers after school as you are couples killing an afternoon.

Boba Tea House sits in Davenport proper, part of a growing constellation of drink-focused spots that take their craft seriously. It's the kind of place that rewards a detour—once you've found it, you'll keep coming back, and you'll start bringing friends who are tired of the usual chain options.

entities.name: Books-A-Million
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 7:00 PM
entities.description:
A sprawling bookstore where browsers can lose hours in the stacks.

Books-A-Million anchors a solid stretch of retail in Davenport with the kind of inventory that rewards wandering — deep sections on fiction and nonfiction, a kids' corner that actually entertains, and tables stocked with games and toys that make gift shopping less of a chore. The staff knows their sections and the space is big enough that you won't feel rushed, whether you're hunting a specific title or just seeing what catches your eye on a Saturday afternoon.

Books-A-Million sits in Davenport's commercial heart, convenient for a browse between errands or a dedicated trip for readers who stock their shelves regularly. It's the kind of destination bookstore the QC needs — accessible, reliable, and honest about what it offers without pretense.

entities.name: Boot Barn
entities.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 7:00 PM
entities.description:
Western wear, work boots, and the real deal—no costume shop vibes.

Boot Barn stocks the kind of boots you actually need: Carhartt work gear, serious Stetson hats, and real leather that'll last. The selection runs deep enough that whether you're outfitting for a job site or genuinely living the Western lifestyle, you're not wading through tourist trinkets. Staff know the difference between a work boot and fashion, and it shows.

Boot Barn sits on Davenport's commercial strip, easy to reach and impossible to miss if you need actual workwear or authentic Western gear. It's the kind of practical, no-nonsense stop that locals rely on—the place you go when you need something real, not when you're browsing. Once you've found your fit here, you'll keep coming back.

entities.name: Bootleg Hill Honey Meads
entities.hours: Monday: 3:00 – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 3:00 – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 12:00 – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM | Friday: 12:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Saturday: 12:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Sunday: 12:00 – 9:00 PM
entities.description:
Honey mead bar where craft cocktails meet medieval tradition.

Bootleg Hill takes the ancient art of mead seriously — but not solemnly. The space hums with an unpretentious energy, the kind of place where you can order a honey-forward cocktail without irony, sit at the bar and watch the bartenders work, and actually enjoy yourself. Their house meads are the draw, but they know how to build a cocktail around them, and the food lands better than you'd expect from a mead bar.

Located in Downtown Davenport, Bootleg Hill represents the neighborhood's slow shift toward destinations worth the drive. It's a place that doesn't try to be anything other than what it is — a genuinely unusual spot in a river town — and that's exactly why it keeps appearing on our guides. If you've been curious about mead but didn't know where to start, or you just want to sit somewhere that feels a little different from the usual QC bar, this is the place.

entities.name: Boozie's Bar & Grill
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM
entities.description:
Old-school bar where the burgers are honest and the crowd is real.

Boozie's feels like the kind of place that's been pouring the same drinks and flipping the same burgers since the '80s — and that's exactly the point. The space has that worn-in comfort of a true neighborhood bar: wood-dark interiors, no pretense, and a kitchen that knows how to nail the fundamentals. Come Friday or Saturday night and you'll find the bar three-deep with locals who've claimed their spots; come weekday lunch and you'll find a quieter crowd of regulars who nod at each other over burgers and cold beers.

Boozie's sits right in Downtown Davenport, anchoring the kind of unpretentious nightlife block that gives the area its soul. It's the bar that shows up in conversations about "where to actually go" in the QC — not because it's trendy, but because it's reliable, familiar, and full of the people who make downtown worth visiting on a Friday night.

entities.name: Brews Energy
entities.hours: Monday: 5:30 AM – 6:30 PM | Tuesday: 5:30 AM – 6:30 PM | Wednesday: 5:30 AM – 6:30 PM | Thursday: 5:30 AM – 6:30 PM | Friday: 5:30 AM – 6:30 PM | Saturday: 7:00 AM – 6:30 PM | Sunday: 7:00 AM – 6:30 PM
entities.description:
Serious smoothie bowls and cold brew for the early-morning crowd.

Brews Energy opens at 5:30 on weekdays—earlier than most of the QC—and it shows: the counter moves with purpose, regulars know their order by heart, and the acai bowls arrive with granola still crackling. The cold brew is consistently smooth, the kind you can drink black without wincing. It's a no-nonsense spot where people fuel up before their day actually starts.

Brews Energy sits in Rock Island, a neighborhood that's quietly becoming a destination for people looking past the usual downtown strips. The early hours and focused menu make it the kind of place that builds a loyal following fast—exactly the type of local favorite we keep coming back to for our guides.

entities.name: Bruegger's Bagels
entities.hours: Monday: 5:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Tuesday: 5:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Wednesday: 5:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Thursday: 5:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Friday: 5:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Saturday: 5:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Sunday: 5:00 AM – 2:00 PM
entities.description:
New York bagels boiled fresh, ready before sunrise in Bettendorf.

Bruegger's opens at 5 a.m. — earlier than most of the QC is awake — which means you can catch the bagels still warm from the oven. The formula is straightforward: boil, bake, build a sandwich. The Everything bagel holds up under cream cheese and lox; the breakfast sandwiches (egg, cheese, meat) are uncomplicated and honest. It's the kind of place where regulars know the order by heart, and the line moves fast even when it's crowded.

Located on Kimberly Road in Bettendorf, Bruegger's sits in the rhythm of the east side's morning commute — easy to grab before work or school. It's a reliable breakfast stop that does one thing well, the kind of consistent anchor that makes a neighborhood feel cared for. Once you've found your bagel order here, you'll keep coming back.

entities.name: Bucktown Social
entities.description:
Hotel restaurant doing breakfast right and dinner with real ambition.

Bucktown Social opens early for the people who actually need it—travelers catching flights, early risers who want eggs done well—and the morning light through those DoubleTree windows feels earned somehow. By evening it transforms into something more deliberate, the kind of place where you can tell the kitchen cares about what's leaving the line. It's a hotel restaurant that doesn't feel like one, which in the QC is rarer than it should be.

Bucktown Social sits in Downtown Davenport, steps from the riverfront and the heart of what's happening in the QC right now. It's the kind of restaurant that belongs in our guides because it's doing the work—reliable breakfast, dinner that matters, and a space that serves both the transient and the local equally well.

entities.name: Buffalo Bill Museum
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 4:00 PM
entities.description: Exhibits about Buffalo Bill's life & Mississippi River heritage, including an 1800s stern wheeler.

entities.name: Buttercupp Candles (Leclaire, Iowa)
entities.description:
Hand-poured candles that smell like someone's best memory.

Buttercupp sits on Le Claire's main drag, and stepping in feels like walking into someone's home library — warm light, wood shelves, and the kind of scents that make you stop and actually breathe. The candles are poured in-house, which means the seasonals rotate and the standards are always fresh; regulars come back for specific scents the way they'd hunt down a favorite coffee order. It's the kind of place where you go in for one candle and leave with three because the person behind the counter actually knows what they're selling.

Le Claire sits just outside Bettendorf, and Buttercupp has become a quiet anchor on the town's walkable stretch — worth the short drive from downtown or a stop on the way to the riverfront antique shops. It's the kind of local business that shows up on gift lists and in the hands of people who care about the details, and once you've been, you'll understand why it's become a regular stop for anyone who knows the area.

entities.name: Butterworth Center and Deere-Wiman House
entities.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 5:00 PM
entities.description: 1800s mansion with Victorian interiors & formal gardens, open for guided tours & events.

entities.name: California Nails
entities.hours: Monday: 9:30 AM – 7:00 PM | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 9:30 AM – 7:00 PM | Thursday: 9:30 AM – 7:00 PM | Friday: 9:30 AM – 7:00 PM | Saturday: 9:30 AM – 6:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 5:00 PM
entities.description:
Moline's reliable spot for a quick manicure and a comfortable chair.

California Nails has mastered the neighborhood salon formula—efficient, friendly, and no-nonsense. Walk in on a weekday afternoon and you'll find a calm room with plenty of stations, the kind of place where the technicians know their regulars by name and remember how you like your nails shaped. It's the spot you return to not because it's fancy, but because it's dependable.

California Nails sits in Moline, the QC's quieter, steadier side—the kind of neighborhood where people actually live their lives rather than just pass through. It's the sort of place that deserves a spot in our guides because it's genuinely useful: reliable, reasonably priced, and tucked into the everyday rhythm of the community. Once you find your nail person here, you'll keep coming back.

entities.name: Calla
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Curated home goods and design pieces in a quietly refined East Davenport shop.

Calla feels like stepping into someone's carefully considered living room — the kind where every object has a reason for being there. The selection leans toward vintage and artisan finds mixed with contemporary design, arranged with enough breathing room that you actually want to linger and look. Tuesday through Saturday afternoons, it's a low-pressure space to browse, ask questions, and maybe find exactly the accent piece or serving bowl you didn't know you needed.

Calla sits in the Village of East Davenport, a quieter pocket of the QC that's become known for independent shops and local character. It's the kind of place that rewards a deliberate visit — not a quick errand, but an afternoon detour when you're interested in discovering what locally-minded businesses are doing in the area. Once you've been, you'll understand why it keeps showing up in conversations about the QC's best-kept retail finds.

entities.name: Capitol Theatre
entities.description:
A restored 1920s palace where the QC comes to see live theater.

Walking into the Capitol is like stepping into someone's memory of what a night out should feel like — ornate plasterwork catches the light, the orchestra pit still hums with possibility, and the seats (many original) face a stage that's hosted everyone from vaudeville acts to touring Broadway productions. The QC's community theater groups perform here regularly, sharing the bill with regional acts and traveling shows, and there's something about the gilt and velvet that makes even a local production feel like an event worth dressing up for.

The Capitol Theatre anchors Downtown Davenport's entertainment corridor, a few blocks from the riverfront and the District's after-hours energy. It's been a gathering place for over a century, and it still is — the kind of institution that shapes how a community sees itself. Once you've caught a show here, the building becomes part of your QC story.

entities.name: Capriotti's Sandwich Shop
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Sunday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM
entities.description:
Towering, messy subs that live up to the hype they've earned nationwide.

Capriotti's isn't trying to be precious about sandwiches — these are built tall and loose, the kind that require both hands and a stack of napkins. The Bobbie (roasted turkey, stuffing, cranberry sauce) is the signature that built the chain, and it's worth ordering at least once; there's something about that specific combination that just works. The counter moves fast, the staff knows the menu cold, and it's the kind of place where regulars have their order memorized and newcomers leave impressed.

Capriotti's sits in Davenport proper, accessible and unpretentious — exactly the kind of sandwich shop that feeds the neighborhood without fanfare. It's been reliably good for years, which is why it keeps showing up on our radar. You go in hungry, order without overthinking it, and leave satisfied.

entities.name: Cat's Eye Distillery
entities.description:
Craft spirits and a welcoming tasting room in the heart of Bettendorf.

Cat's Eye Distillery brings a genuine spirit-making operation to Bettendorf — the kind of place where you can taste what they're actually making in the back. The tasting room has the feel of somewhere the owners actually want to spend time, with the sort of hospitality that makes you linger longer than you planned. Whether you're a spirits enthusiast or just curious, there's no pretension here, just good distilling and people who know what they've made.

Cat's Eye sits in Bettendorf, a neighborhood that's quietly building its own identity beyond the main QC drag. It's the kind of local producer worth seeking out — the sort of place that rewards curiosity and makes for a genuine afternoon outing, especially if you're pairing it with dinner or drinks elsewhere in the area. Once you've found it, you'll understand why it keeps showing up on lists of places that actually matter to people who live here.

entities.name: Celebration River Cruises
entities.hours: Monday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Tuesday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 8:00 AM – 6:30 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Paddlewheel views of the Mississippi from a real riverboat.

Celebration River Cruises operates from Moline's riverfront, where you board an honest-to-goodness paddlewheel boat — the kind that actually looks like it belongs on the Mississippi. The cruises move slowly enough that you can watch the river traffic, spot eagles if you're lucky, and feel the weight of the water beneath you. It's the kind of outing that makes the river feel less like a landmark you drive past and more like the reason the Quad Cities exists.

Celebration River Cruises sits on Moline's waterfront, steps from some of the QC's best river views and a natural fit for anyone wanting to actually experience the Mississippi rather than just admire it from shore. Whether you're marking an occasion or just looking for an afternoon that moves at water speed, it's the kind of distinctly local experience that belongs in our curated guides — the sort of thing visitors ask about, and locals sometimes forget to do.

entities.name: Centennial Park
entities.hours: Monday: 6:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 6:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 6:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 6:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 6:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 6:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Sunday: 6:00 AM – 9:00 PM
entities.description:
250 acres of river trails, courts, and open green where Davenport actually plays.

Centennial Park sprawls along the Mississippi like the QC's backyard—big enough that you can walk the riverside trails without feeling crowded, even on a Saturday morning. The dog area gets loud and happy; the skate park draws the right kind of focused energy; the gazebos dot the grass for picnics that stretch into evening. It's the kind of place where you see the same faces regularly—joggers on the paths, families on the courts, kids learning to push off a skateboard—because it actually works for how people spend their time.

Centennial Park anchors Davenport's outdoor life in a way that matters. Open from 6 AM to 9 PM daily, it's woven into the routines of anyone who lives here—a place that shows up on our guides because it's genuinely essential to understanding how the QC moves, plays, and congregates. Once you've walked those riverside trails or watched the sunset from a bench, you understand why locals keep coming back.

entities.name: Center for Belgian Culture
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 1:00 – 4:00 PM | Thursday: Closed | Friday: Closed | Saturday: 1:00 – 4:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
A quiet museum devoted entirely to Belgian heritage and craft.

The Center for Belgian Culture occupies a modest footprint in Moline, but what it holds—lace, ceramics, historical documents, family artifacts—tells the story of the Belgian families who built this corner of the Quad Cities. The museum operates on a limited schedule (Wednesday and Saturday afternoons), which means it rewards those who plan ahead; once inside, you'll find volunteer docents who actually know the families behind the pieces. It's the kind of place where a single lace doily can lead to a twenty-minute conversation about technique and immigration.

The Center sits in the Overlook neighborhood, a quieter residential pocket that many QC visitors overlook entirely. But this museum is exactly why we pay attention to the smaller, more specific corners of the region—it's a genuine record of a particular community's history and pride, run by people who care deeply about keeping those stories alive. If you're curious about the deeper roots of the QC, or you have Belgian heritage yourself, this is where you belong.

entities.name: Central Standard Craft Burgers & Beer
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM
entities.description:
Aged beef burgers and 24 craft taps in a warm, unpretentious room.

Central Standard does the fundamentals right — the beef is aged, the burgers are built with care, and the beer list is long enough to spend a whole evening working through it without repeating yourself. The patio catches the evening light, the TVs are always tuned to something worth watching, and the crowd skews local and relaxed. This is where Bettendorf comes to eat something good without the fuss.

Central Standard sits in Bettendorf, the QC's most reliable neighborhood for a solid night out — the kind of place that doesn't try too hard but gets the details right. It's become the sort of restaurant that regulars return to again and again, which is exactly what our guides are built around: places worth knowing, places that feel like they're made for you.

entities.name: Channel Cat Water Taxi
entities.description:
A vintage riverboat shuttle connecting both sides of the Mississippi in 15 minutes flat.

Channel Cat is pure Quad Cities pragmatism dressed up as leisure — a genuine paddlewheel ferry that carries you from Davenport's riverfront to Rock Island and back, diesel rumbling beneath your feet and river wind doing most of the work. The ride itself is the point: watching the current push against the hull, the skyline shrink behind you, the industrial bones of the QC laid bare from the water. It's touristy enough to feel like an occasion, but locals take it too, especially on warm evenings when the upper deck fills with people who just want to be on the river for a while.

Channel Cat operates from Davenport's riverfront, that stretch of town that's finally becoming a destination again. It's the rare QC activity that gets you actually on the water rather than just looking at it — a 15-minute escape that costs almost nothing and delivers something you can't replicate from shore. Once you've done it, you'll find reasons to do it again.

entities.name: Circa '21 Dinner Playhouse
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 4:00 PM
entities.description: Former Vaudeville theater offering comfort food & a bar, with plays, concerts & performing staff.

entities.name: Circle Tap
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM
entities.description:
A neighborhood dive where the cheap beer flows and everyone knows your name.

Circle Tap is the kind of place that opens at 11 a.m. and doesn't close until 2, which tells you everything you need to know about its purpose. The kind of bar where regulars have claimed their stools, the jukebox plays what it always plays, and the bartender pours a generous pour without asking twice. It's unpretentious in the best way — no craft cocktail menus or reclaimed wood — just a real neighborhood gathering spot where the pool table gets action and the wings are hot.

Sitting in the 5 Points area of Davenport, Circle Tap is exactly the kind of local institution that matters to the actual fabric of the Quad Cities. It's not a destination bar in the shiny sense, but rather a place that's been holding down the neighborhood for years, serving the people who live nearby and the ones who keep coming back because it feels like home. That's the kind of authenticity we chase on our guides.

entities.name: City Loafers
entities.description:
Breakfast and dinner spot where downtown Davenport actually gathers.

City Loafers opens early and stays open late, which means it catches the whole rhythm of the neighborhood—early risers fueling up before work, lunch crowds squeezing in between errands, then a second life as a dinner destination when the sun drops. The split hours (6 AM to 2 PM, then 4 to 10 PM) tell you something: this is a place built around real eating patterns, not just passing traffic. The regulars know the drill and the kitchen clearly cares about both the morning eggs and the evening plates.

City Loafers sits right in Downtown Davenport, where the QC's best neighborhood energy lives. It's the kind of reliable, unpretentious spot that shows up in locals' weekly rotations—not destination dining, but the place you find yourself recommending when someone asks where to actually eat downtown. Once you land there, you understand why.

entities.name: Clint's Draft House Pizza & Grill
entities.hours: Monday: 6:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Tuesday: 6:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Wednesday: 6:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Thursday: 6:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Friday: 6:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Saturday: 6:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM
entities.description:
Pizza and pints in Old Towne — open early enough for breakfast, late enough for last call.

Clint's does the comfortable neighborhood bar thing well: wood-fired pizza that's crispy at the edges, a draft list that rotates, and the kind of crowd that ranges from families grabbing lunch to groups settling in for the night. The space has that lived-in feeling — worn booths, sports on the screens, regulars who know the bartenders' names. It's the place you end up at more often than you planned, partly because the hours are absurd (6 a.m. opening on weekdays means breakfast pizza is actually happening here) and partly because the pizza is reliably good.

Clint's sits in Old Towne Moline, a neighborhood that's quietly become one of the QC's more reliable spots for a night out — walkable, unpretentious, and full of places that feel like they belong to locals rather than tourists. It's the kind of anchor spot that shows up in the rotation when you're looking for somewhere to eat and drink without fuss, no matter what time you're thinking about showing up.

entities.name: Club Pilates
entities.hours: Monday: 6:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Tuesday: 6:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Wednesday: 6:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 6:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Sunday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM
entities.description:
Reformer pilates classes where Bettendorf comes to get stronger.

Club Pilates fills the early mornings and after-work slots with people serious about the work — the kind of studio where you show up, clip into the reformer, and spend 50 minutes learning what your core can actually do. The instructors know their regulars by name and their form intimately; they'll cue you toward better alignment without making it feel like correction. By 6:45 AM on a weekday, the room is full and focused, the only sound the smooth glide of the carriage and the instructor's steady voice counting you through another set.

Club Pilates sits in Bettendorf, the QC's quieter, more residential east side — the kind of neighborhood where fitness routines are taken seriously and community matters. It's the sort of place that builds real loyalty; a 5-star Google rating with over 140 reviews doesn't happen by accident. If you're looking to build strength with people who've made the same commitment, this is where the Bettendorf set does it.

entities.name: Codfish Hollow Barnstormers
entities.description:
A converted barn hosting live music under exposed beams and string lights.

Codfish Hollow is exactly what its name suggests—an old barnstormers' venue where the rafters echo with live sets most weekends. The space keeps its bones (all that weathered wood, the real barn smell that lingers) while rows of picnic tables and a small bar make it feel less museum piece and more genuine gathering spot. You'll catch everything from country to indie rock to folk, and there's something about seeing a band play in front of a wall of hay bales that makes the whole night feel less polished and more real.

Codfish Hollow sits in Maquoketa, a short drive northeast of the core QC but worth the trip if you're looking for the kind of venue that doesn't pretend to be anything other than what it is. It's become a pillar of the region's live music scene—the kind of place locals plan weekends around and visitors stumble upon and never forget. Once you've sat under those lights with a drink in hand and good music filling the barn, you'll understand why it keeps landing on our lists.

entities.name: Cody Road Coffee LeClaire
entities.hours: Monday: 6:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Tuesday: 6:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Wednesday: 6:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Thursday: 6:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Friday: 6:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Saturday: 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Sunday: 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM
entities.description:
Le Claire's morning anchor—serious coffee in a space that feels like home.

Cody Road pulls espresso with the kind of consistency that keeps people coming back before work, and the room itself—warm light, real furniture, the smell of fresh beans—makes you want to linger longer than you planned. Weekday mornings fill quickly with locals who know their order by heart; weekends move slower, which is exactly when you should sit by a window with a pour-over and let an hour disappear.

Cody Road sits on Le Claire's main drag, a small river town that's become a favorite detour for anyone who knows it exists. It's the kind of reliable, unpretentious place that anchors a neighborhood—the coffee shop people actually need, not just visit—and it's exactly why we keep it on our map.

entities.name: Coffee House Cafe and Goods
entities.hours: Monday: 7:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Tuesday: 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Thursday: 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Friday: 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Saturday: 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Sunday: 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM
entities.description:
Neighborhood coffee shop where the regulars know your order by heart.

Coffee House Cafe and Goods feels like the kind of place that's been quietly doing right by East Davenport for years — the kind where Monday mornings stretch into evening hours (they're open until 8 PM) and the weekend crowd knows to come early. There's a lived-in comfort here: good coffee, a modest goods section that actually makes sense, and the unhurried feeling of a spot that isn't trying to be anywhere else. It's the rare local café that earns a 4.7 rating across hundreds of reviews without any of the polished pretense.

Coffee House Cafe and Goods sits in the Village of East Davenport, a quieter, more residential corner of the city that rewards exploring. This is the kind of neighborhood anchor that shows up in our guides because it's genuine — built for the people who actually live nearby, not for Instagram. Once you've found it, you'll understand why it keeps showing up in conversation among locals who care about where they spend their mornings.

entities.name: ComedySportz Quad Cities
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: Closed | Thursday: Closed | Friday: 6:00 – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 6:00 – 10:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Live improv comedy where the audience calls the plays and the laughs are competitive.

ComedySportz turns a Friday or Saturday night into something between a sports event and a comedy show — two teams of improvisers riff off audience suggestions while a referee keeps score on a board. The energy is infectious; people come for the laughs but stick around for the genuinely unpredictable moments when an off-the-cuff scene lands perfectly or spectacularly falls apart. It's the kind of place where you end up invested in whether the blue team or red team nails the next game.

ComedySportz sits in Moline, on the Illinois side of the river where live entertainment options are worth seeking out. It's a Friday-night ritual for plenty of QC regulars — the sort of venue that gets people off their couches and into a room with strangers who become temporary teammates in the comedy. Once you've been, you'll understand why it consistently earns high marks and keeps people coming back.

entities.name: Comfort Inn Davenport East - Bettendorf
entities.description:
Clean rooms, reliable service, and a price that won't wreck your budget.

The Comfort Inn on the Davenport-Bettendorf border does what it promises without the fuss. Rooms are straightforward and well-maintained, with the kind of bed that actually lets you sleep after a long drive. The front desk staff know the area well enough to point you toward the good breakfast spots and the quickest route to whatever brought you here — whether that's a family visit, a Rhythm City Casino run, or just a solid night's rest between destinations.

This stretch of Route 61 has all the major chain hotels, but the Comfort Inn holds its own with genuine hospitality and consistent upkeep. It's the kind of place locals recommend to visiting relatives without hesitation, and it sits close enough to downtown Davenport and Bettendorf's shopping that you're never far from what the QC has to offer.

entities.name: Common Chord | Home of the Redstone Room

entities.description: Venue for performances by touring musicians of all stripes plus a cafe, open-mike sessions & more.

entities.name: Contrary Brewing Co
entities.hours: Monday: 4:00 – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 4:00 – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 4:00 – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 4:00 – 9:00 PM | Friday: 4:00 – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 6:00 PM
entities.description:
A brewery worth the drive to Muscatine — craft beer and real hospitality.

Contrary Brewing sits in a converted space that feels lived-in rather than designed, the kind of place where the bartenders remember your name by the second visit. The beer lineup skews thoughtful — no gimmicks, just well-made IPAs, stouts, and seasonals that reward paying attention. Weekend afternoons draw a mix of locals and QC visitors willing to make the 20-minute trip, and there's enough space and good energy that you can settle in for hours without feeling rushed.

Muscatine sits east of the main Quad Cities cluster, but Contrary has become a genuine destination rather than an afterthought — the kind of place that pulls people off the interstate and into a town they might otherwise skip. It's earned a loyal following not through hype but through consistency, and it shows up in our guides because it represents what smaller QC neighborhoods can do when they get it right.

entities.name: Cookies & Dreams
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Sunday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM
entities.description:
Warm cookies and ice cream dreams in the heart of Bettendorf.

Cookies & Dreams is the kind of place that smells exactly like it should — butter and vanilla hitting you the moment you walk in. The setup is simple and intentional: choose your cookie (they're still warm, still soft), pick your ice cream flavor, and watch them sandwich it together. The regulars know to come after dinner; families with kids treat it like their third space on weekend nights.

Cookies & Dreams sits in Bettendorf, just far enough from downtown to feel like a destination but close enough to everything else that it's easy to add to your evening. It's become the kind of spot where birthdays happen and cravings get satisfied, and it shows up in our guides because the QC deserves places that do one thing well and make you want to come back.

entities.name: Cool Beanz Coffee
entities.description: Chill spot for espresso drinks, panini & pastries, whiskey & wine flights, craft beer & live music.

entities.name: Cordova Dragway
entities.description:
Quarter-mile thrills and the roar of nitro fuel on summer nights.

Cordova Dragway is where the QC goes to feel the ground shake. Friday and Saturday nights fill the bleachers with folks who've come for the full experience — the smell of racing fuel, the crack of a tree light, the howl of engines doing what they're built for. Whether you're timing bracket racers or watching the pro cars, there's a particular electricity here that doesn't exist anywhere else in the region.

Cordova Dragway sits just outside the main QC corridor, making it an easy drive when you want to escape the routine. It's become a seasonal tradition for families, gear heads, and anyone who appreciates the unfiltered spectacle of motorsports. Once you've heard a funny car hit the throttle, you'll understand why the regulars keep coming back.

entities.name: Costa Del Mar Mexican Grill & Seafood
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM
entities.description:
Fresh ceviche and grilled fish in a bright Davenport room that feels like an escape.

Costa Del Mar does the Gulf right—ceviches that taste like they arrived this morning, grilled whole fish that arrive sizzling on cast iron, shrimp that's cooked with actual restraint. The dining room is warm and unhurried, full of families and regulars who clearly know the menu by heart. Order the ceviche if you're undecided, or ask your server what came in fresh today.

Costa Del Mar sits in Davenport, anchoring a neighborhood that's quietly built itself into one of the QC's most interesting dining destinations. It's the kind of place that draws people back again and again—not because it's trendy, but because the food is honest and the welcome is genuine. Once you've had their ceviche, you'll understand why it shows up on every serious eater's rotation.

entities.name: Country Inn & Suites by Radisson, Davenport - Quad Cities
entities.description:
A solid mid-range landing spot with free breakfast and no surprises.

Country Inn & Suites delivers exactly what it promises — clean rooms, a gym if you need it, and a breakfast spread that'll get you out the door fueled. The cookies by the front desk are a nice touch, the kind of small hospitality detail that makes a chain feel slightly more personal. It's the type of place where business travelers know what they're getting, and families passing through can rest easy without overthinking it.

The hotel sits on Davenport's accessible commercial strip, convenient to I-80 and the downtown core without requiring a complicated drive. If you're visiting the Quad Cities and want a dependable base without the boutique price tag — or if you're here for work and need a reliable bed and breakfast — this is a straightforward choice that won't disappoint.

entities.name: Country Inn & Suites by Radisson, Moline Airport
entities.description:
No-frills airport hotel with a pool and a free ride to your gate.

Country Inn & Suites sits a short walk from Moline Airport—close enough that the shuttle actually makes sense, far enough that you're not hearing jets all night. The rooms are clean and straightforward, the kind of place that doesn't try to be anything fancier than it is. There's an indoor pool that's genuinely useful on a cold night, and the complimentary breakfast means you're not hunting for coffee before an early flight.

It's the practical choice for travelers passing through the Quad Cities, especially anyone flying out of Moline or arriving late. You're not here for the atmosphere—you're here because you need a bed, a shower, and a way to the airport that doesn't involve calling an Uber at 5 a.m. That simplicity, and the fact that it actually delivers on what it promises, is exactly why it lands on our guide.

entities.name: Country Style
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM
entities.description:
A nostalgic ice cream counter where the malts are thick and the lines move fast.

Country Style is the kind of place that hasn't changed much in decades, and that's entirely the point. The soft serve swirl is reliable, the sundaes arrive stacked generous, and the malts—made thick enough to actually require effort on the straw—taste like they did when your parents were kids. It's fluorescent-lit and unpretentious, the sort of spot where families pile in after baseball games and teenagers claim the corner booths on Friday nights.

Tucked into the Willard/Wharton stretch of Moline, Country Style sits on a stretch of road that feels distinctly separate from the riverfront buzz—but that's exactly what makes it valuable to know about. It's a reminder that the best QC experiences aren't always polished or Instagram-ready; sometimes they're just honest, consistent, and exactly what you need on a warm afternoon. Once you've been, you'll understand why three-hundred-plus people have left reviews.

entities.name: Country Style Ice Cream & Coffee
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM
entities.description:
A Davenport institution scooping thick malts and soft-serve since before you were born.

Country Style feels like it's been here forever—because it has. The counter is worn smooth from decades of orders, the flavors rotate with real seasons, and a malt here is properly thick, the kind that requires actual effort on the straw. On warm evenings families pile in with kids, couples squeeze into booths, and you'll hear the same regulars ordering the same thing they've ordered for years.

Country Style anchors a particular kind of Davenport evening—the kind where you end up downtown without a big plan and find yourself standing in line for ice cream anyway. It's the rare chain location that actually feels local, the sort of place that shows up in family photo albums across three generations. Once you've been, you understand why the Google ratings stay high: some places just deserve to stick around.

entities.name: Coya's Cafe
entities.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 1:00 AM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 1:00 AM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Moline's late-night anchor for Colombian coffee and unfussy, honest food.

Coya's stays open past midnight on weekends — a rarity in the QC — and the regulars know it. The coffee is strong and straightforward, the kind that doesn't apologize, and the kitchen turns out simple meals without pretension: empanadas, sandwiches, arepas. It's the place you end up at 11 p.m. when everywhere else has closed, or the spot you slip into on a Saturday morning when you want to linger without feeling rushed.

Coya's sits in Moline, a neighborhood that rewards exploration if you know where to look. It's the kind of place that builds its reputation quietly — nearly 1,000 reviews and a 4.5-star rating speak to loyalty more than hype. Once you've discovered it, you'll find yourself coming back, not because it's trying to impress you, but because it's genuine and consistent in a way that matters.

entities.name: Crabby's Bar & Grill
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 3:00 AM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 3:00 AM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 3:00 AM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 3:00 AM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 3:00 AM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 3:00 AM | Sunday: 10:00 AM – 3:00 AM
entities.description:
A Coal Valley institution where the crowd stays late and the energy never quits.

Crabby's runs from 10 a.m. to 3 a.m. every single day — the kind of stamina that only happens when a place knows exactly what its regulars want. The bar has the comfortable chaos of somewhere people actually go to, not somewhere they visit: worn leather, the smell of fried food and beer, a jukebox that gets used. Weekends swell with the kind of crowd that treats it like a second living room.

Out in Coal Valley, Crabby's sits on the edge of the QC's sprawl — far enough from downtown to feel like a neighborhood spot, close enough to matter. It's been doing this for years, which means something in a market where bars come and go. The kind of place that shows up on our radar not for being precious or new, but for being genuinely necessary to the people who go there.

entities.name: Crafted QC - Handmade, Gifts & Workshops
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 12:00 – 4:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 4:00 PM
entities.description:
A bright shop where local makers sell their work and teach you to make your own.

Crafted QC feels like stepping into someone's carefully curated studio—shelves lined with handmade goods from QC artists, the kind of pieces that actually have stories behind them. The real draw is the workshops: you can show up on a Thursday morning to learn letterpress, or a Saturday afternoon to try your hand at pottery or printmaking. It's the rare gift shop that doubles as a creative space, so you leave with something made by someone you might've just met.

Crafted QC anchors downtown Le Claire, a river town that's become a real destination for people who care about craft and local makers. The limited hours (closed Mondays and Tuesdays) mean planning ahead, but that's part of the charm—this is a place built around intention, not convenience. It's exactly the kind of spot that reminds you why the Quad Cities punches above its weight for creative community.

entities.name: Crane & Pelican Cafe
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM, 5:00 – 8:00 PM | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM, 5:00 – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM, 5:00 – 8:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM, 5:00 – 8:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM, 5:00 – 8:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description: Locally sourced American comfort food served in a 19th-century house with Mississippi River views.

entities.name: Crawford Brew Works
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM
entities.description:
Brewpub where the beer is taken seriously and the food actually matches.

Crawford Brew Works does that rare thing — makes its own beer and doesn't treat the kitchen as an afterthought. The taproom hums with the kind of energy that happens when locals know the head brewer by name, and the kitchen turns out the kind of food that makes you linger over a second pour. Grab a seat at the bar to watch the taps, or claim a table and work through their rotating specials; either way, you're tasting something made steps away from where you're sitting.

Crawford sits in Bettendorf's growing dining corridor, close enough to the river that a walk before or after feels natural. It's become one of those places the QC keeps coming back to — the kind of brewpub that actually earns its reputation rather than coasting on novelty. Once you've been, you'll understand why it shows up in conversation about where to take out-of-town guests.

entities.name: Crawford Company
entities.hours: Monday: 7:30 AM – 4:30 PM | Tuesday: 7:30 AM – 4:30 PM | Wednesday: 7:30 AM – 4:30 PM | Thursday: 7:30 AM – 4:30 PM | Friday: 7:30 AM – 4:30 PM | Saturday: Closed | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
A Rock Island institution where coffee, pastries, and craft sensibility meet weekday mornings.

Crawford Company opens early and closes by mid-afternoon — a deliberate rhythm that tells you this is a place with its own pace. The pastries are the real draw: laminated, buttery things that arrive fresh and disappear fast, paired with coffee that tastes like someone actually cares about the pour. Regulars have their orders down to a glance, and there's an easy quiet to the room that feels earned, not designed.

Crawford Company sits in Rock Island, a neighborhood with real character and fewer tourist footnotes than the bigger Quad Cities anchors. It's weekday-focused, closing Saturdays and Sundays, which means it belongs to the people who actually live here — the ones who know the difference between a place that's trying and a place that's *there*. That's exactly the kind of local fixture we chase for our guides.

entities.name: Creative Signature Cuisine
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 8:00 AM – 8:30 PM | Wednesday: 8:00 AM – 8:30 PM | Thursday: 8:00 AM – 8:30 PM | Friday: 8:00 AM – 8:30 PM | Saturday: 8:00 AM – 8:30 PM | Sunday: 8:00 AM – 2:00 PM
entities.description:
Creative takes on comfort food, drawn fresh daily in East Moline.

Creative Signature Cuisine doesn't play it safe—the menu shifts with what's in season and what the kitchen feels like executing well that week. The ratings speak for themselves (4.8 stars isn't noise), and the regulars come back because they trust the chef to surprise them. It's the kind of place where you ask your server what's good today, and they actually have a real answer.

Creative Signature Cuisine sits in East Moline, just outside the river towns but worth the short drive if you're serious about eating well. The limited hours (closed Mondays, early close on Sundays) hint at a kitchen that's not trying to be everything to everyone—they're selective about when and how they cook. Once you've had a meal here, you'll understand why it keeps showing up on our radar.
