entities.name: Crow Creek Dog Park
entities.description:
Off-leash acres where your dog actually gets tired out.

Crow Creek has the space that smaller neighborhood parks can't touch — your dog can actually run, and you can actually watch them do it without anxiety. The grounds roll enough to feel like real terrain, and there's enough shade scattered through that you're not cooking on a summer afternoon. Regulars know the rhythm: mornings are calmer, weekends bring the pack, and there's always someone who knows which dogs play well together.

Crow Creek sits in Bettendorf, just east of the river — far enough from downtown to have real elbow room, close enough that it's a natural stop if you're already exploring that side of the QC. It's the kind of place that dog owners talk about, the one they actually drive to instead of settling for the closer option. If you've got a dog and you live here, this place earns its spot on your regular rotation.

entities.name: Cruz Cutz Barbershop
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
A sharp Moline barbershop where the cuts are clean and the regulars keep coming back.

Cruz Cutz has built something real in Moline — the kind of place where you're not just getting a haircut, you're sitting in a chair that knows your name. The barbers here take their time, listening to what you actually want instead of rushing through the day. Walk in on a Saturday and you'll see the proof: a steady stream of customers, mostly the same faces, mostly satisfied.

Cruz Cutz sits in Moline, where good neighborhood businesses still matter. With a 4.7 rating across 257 reviews, it's earned the kind of trust that doesn't come from marketing — it comes from doing the work right, week after week. It's exactly the kind of local place we highlight because it's what makes the QC feel like a real community, not just a collection of chain stores.

entities.name: Culture Shock Barber Studio
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 5:30 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 5:30 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 5:30 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 5:30 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 12:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
A sharp downtown barbershop where the cuts are precise and the vibe is welcoming.

Culture Shock sits on a Downtown Davenport block that's quietly becoming somewhere people actually want to be, and the barbershop fits the moment perfectly. The barbers here know what they're doing — clean lines, careful fades, the kind of attention to detail that makes you want to go back. It's the rare neighborhood spot that's earned perfect ratings across the board, which says something about both the craft and the people wielding the clippers.

Culture Shock Barber Studio is in Downtown Davenport, one of the QC's most characterful pockets — a short walk or drive from the riverfront and easy to fold into a longer visit. It's the kind of place that shows up again and again on our curated guides: a neighborhood anchor that does one thing exceptionally well, and makes you feel like a regular the moment you walk in.

entities.name: D' Lua on the River
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 9:00 AM – 9:00 PM
entities.description:
Brazilian steakhouse where the meat keeps coming and the river views don't hurt.

D'Lua is the kind of place where servers move through the room with skewers of perfectly charred picanha and lamb, and you just keep nodding yes. The dining room opens onto the Mississippi — big windows, good sightlines — and there's something about watching the water while you're in the middle of a churrascaria experience that makes the whole evening feel a little more expansive. It's festive without being loud, generous without being pretentious, the kind of restaurant where you'll see families celebrating milestones sitting two tables over from a Friday night date.

D'Lua sits right on the Davenport riverfront, in a neighborhood that's quietly become the QC's most reliable dining destination. It's the kind of place that shows up again and again on our curated guides — because it delivers on both the food and the setting, and because it's exactly the sort of restaurant that keeps you choosing to stay local instead of driving somewhere else.

entities.name: Daiquiri Factory
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 4:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Wednesday: 4:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Thursday: 4:00 PM – 2:00 AM | Friday: 4:00 PM – 2:00 AM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Frozen daiquiris and a bartender who knows your name by round two.

Daiquiri Factory does one thing and does it well — you pick your flavor, they blend it frozen and strong, and you settle in at the bar or a high-top to watch downtown come alive around you. The crowd shifts with the hours: quieter on weekday afternoons when it's mostly regulars, louder and looser as Friday night kicks in. It's the kind of place where the bartenders remember what you ordered last time and where the line between a quick drink and the whole evening blurs easily.

Daiquiri Factory anchors a corner of Downtown Davenport's emerging nightlife row, steps from other bars and a short walk to the riverfront. It's unpretentious and genuinely local — the kind of spot that regulars fiercely defend and newcomers discover by accident and then come back to. Once you've been, you'll understand why it keeps showing up on our guides.

entities.name: Daley Tax Service
entities.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 1:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Local tax prep that actually answers your questions without the corporate runaround.

Daley Tax Service has built a 5-star reputation the old-fashioned way — by showing up, listening, and getting the job done right. The team knows the difference between a quick file and a thorough one, and they're not rushing you through either. Saturday morning appointments mean you don't have to take time off work, and the straightforward pricing means no surprises when you walk out.

Daley sits in Moline, solid ground for people who need straightforward, reliable service without the big-box tax place experience. With 214 Google reviews at 5 stars, this is the kind of place that gets passed along quietly among neighbors and coworkers — proof that sometimes the best recommendations in the QC come from word of mouth, not advertising.

entities.name: Dan Nagle Walnut Grove Pioneer Village and Olde St. Ann's Church
entities.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Sunday: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM
entities.description:
A quiet walk through 1800s frontier life, buildings and all.

Dan Nagle's Pioneer Village spreads across Long Grove like a living history lesson—actual period structures moved here and restored, not replicas. You'll wander past a one-room schoolhouse, a log cabin, a blacksmith shop, each one real enough to smell the wood and dust. It's the kind of place that hits different when you're standing in a genuine 19th-century kitchen, imagining how narrow and purposeful those lives were.

Long Grove sits on the quieter edge of Scott County, making this village feel genuinely removed from the usual QC route—which is exactly the point. It's ideal for an unhurried afternoon when you want to slow down and touch something old, or when you're introducing kids to how people actually lived here 150 years ago. The whole experience has the feel of a discovery rather than a destination, the kind of local gem that deepens your sense of place.

entities.name: Danndee Flowers
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: Closed | Thursday: Closed | Friday: Closed | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Sunday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM
entities.description:
Weekend floristry in Aledo where arrangements feel like they're made just for you.

Danndee Flowers opens only Saturday and Sunday, which means the arrangements you find here are fresh and intentional—not yesterday's inventory. The shop carries the kind of flowers that actually smell like something, and the owner knows how to build a bouquet that doesn't look like it came from a formula. Whether you're after something for a dinner table or a real occasion, there's care in every stem.

Aledo sits just beyond the river, a quiet town that most QC visitors skip—which is exactly why it's worth the drive. Danndee Flowers is the kind of neighborhood spot that reveals itself slowly: limited hours, real relationships with regulars, flowers that matter. It's a reminder that the best places in the QC aren't always on the main drag.

entities.name: Davenport Junior Theatre
entities.description:
A community stage where kids discover their voices—and audiences discover real magic.

The Davenport Junior Theatre has been the place where second-graders realize they can sing, where shy eighth-graders step into spotlights and find confidence, and where families come to watch their own kids become someone else entirely. The productions here feel urgent and alive in a way that matters—this isn't polished or distant, it's your neighbor's daughter as Dorothy, your kid's best friend stealing scenes as a comic villain. Walking in, you sense the energy of a place that takes young performers seriously, gives them real staging and real responsibility, and trusts them to carry entire shows.

Davenport Junior Theatre sits in the heart of Davenport's cultural landscape, the kind of institution that has shaped generations of QC kids and remains deeply woven into the community's identity. It belongs in our guides because it's exactly what makes a region feel like home—a place that isn't about spectacle, but about opportunity, belonging, and the particular magic of watching young people discover what they're capable of. If you have kids, or you remember what it felt like to be one, you'll understand why this place matters.

entities.name: Davenport Speedway
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: Closed | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 3:30 AM – 12:00 PM | Saturday: Closed | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Slot car racing in a devoted corner of Davenport's creative south side.

Davenport Speedway is exactly what it sounds like — a carefully maintained slot car track where the whir of miniature engines and the focus of serious hobbyists fills a quiet Thursday or Friday afternoon. The setup has the feel of a place built by someone who actually races, not a tourist attraction: meticulous track work, vintage and modern cars side by side, and a community of regulars who know every groove. If you've ever dismissed slot cars as kids' stuff, an hour here will change that.

Davenport Speedway sits on the quieter south side, away from the riverfront bustle — the kind of specific, unprompted local spot that makes a place feel real. Limited hours mean you'll need to plan ahead, but that's part of what keeps it genuine. It's the kind of place that appears on our guides because it's genuinely worth seeking out if you're curious about what Quad Citians actually do with their time.

entities.name: Davidson Jewelers
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
A third-generation jeweler where engagement rings get the attention they deserve.

Davidson Jewelers has been in East Moline long enough to know what matters — the staff can talk you through diamond cuts like they're discussing old friends, and they're not rushing you toward the biggest stone. The showroom feels unhurried, cases well-lit and organized, the kind of place where you actually want to linger and ask questions. Whether you're shopping for an heirloom remake or starting from scratch, there's a quiet competence here that shows in the 4.9-star reviews.

Davidson Jewelers sits in East Moline, just across the river from Moline's core — close enough to grab dinner nearby, far enough to feel like its own thing. It's the kind of steady, reliable local business that reminds you why the Quad Cities still has independent jewelers doing real work, the ones your parents and grandparents might've trusted too.

entities.name: Dead Poet’s Espresso Ltd
entities.hours: Monday: 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Tuesday: 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Thursday: 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Friday: 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Saturday: 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Literary vibes and serious espresso in the heart of Moline.

Dead Poet's feels like the kind of place built by people who actually care — the kind where your barista remembers how you take it, and the espresso tastes like someone dialed it in that morning, not last week. The space has that worn-in charm of a neighborhood spot that's earned its regulars: mismatched furniture, walls that have clearly hosted local art, the hum of a real community rather than foot traffic. You'll see the same faces most mornings, the same dog waiting outside, the same quiet intensity of people getting their work done.

Dead Poet's sits on Moline's quieter streets — a short hop from the river and the energy of downtown, but with its own distinct rhythm. It's become one of those places that locals actually guard a little, the kind that shows up on every "best of" list not because of Instagram appeal, but because the coffee is genuinely good and the atmosphere rewards you for showing up more than once.

entities.name: Decades Antiques Shop
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: Closed | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 4:30 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 4:30 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 4:30 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Vintage goods and homemade lunch in a Le Claire storefront worth the drive.

Decades feels like shopping in someone's carefully curated living room — the kind of person who knows the difference between mid-century and actually mid-century. You'll find glassware that catches the light right, leather-bound books that smell like old libraries, and the occasional piece of furniture that makes you do the math on your truck bed space. The real surprise is the kitchen: sandwiches and soups that change with the season, made fresh and served at a few small tables where locals linger longer than they planned.

Le Claire has become a quiet destination for people who like to move slow through a town — antique shops, river views, and the kind of Friday afternoon that doesn't feel rushed. Decades fits that rhythm perfectly, worth a dedicated trip or an easy detour if you're already exploring the riverfront. It's the kind of place that rewards showing up in person: you'll find something you didn't know you needed, and you might eat better than expected while you're there.

entities.name: Deere & Company World Headquarters
entities.description:
Industrial history made tangible, right on the riverfront.

Deere & Company's world headquarters is less a restaurant than a pilgrimage site for anyone curious about what built the Quad Cities. The building itself — all clean lines and modernist restraint — sits like a monument to American manufacturing, and the grounds offer views back toward the river that remind you why Moline became what it is. Step inside and you're walking through the company's DNA: the cafeteria hums with engineers and legacy employees, and if you're lucky, the gift shop has vintage John Deere literature that tells the real story of Iowa farm country.

Deere & Company World Headquarters anchors Moline's identity in a way few other places do — it's the reason the whole area exists. Whether you're a genuine manufacturing history buff or just someone who wants to understand the QC's backbone, this is worth a deliberate visit. It's the kind of place that makes sense alongside a walk along the riverfront and a meal downtown, a physical reminder of why the Quad Cities matter.

entities.name: Del-Rich Loan & Neat Stuff Antiques
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Vintage finds and loan shop treasures in one quirky downtown corner.

Del-Rich is the kind of place where you're never quite sure what you'll find — one visit it's vintage glassware and old pocket watches, the next it's mid-century furniture or a wall of vinyl. The shop has that lived-in feeling of a real antique store, not a staged one, with prices that won't make you wince and owners who actually know what they're selling. The loan side adds to the character; it's a working neighborhood spot, not a tourist trap, which means the inventory turns over and keeps you coming back.

Del-Rich sits in Downtown Davenport, where a handful of independent shops are quietly building something worth the drive. It's the kind of place that rewards a slow Saturday morning — park once, hit the coffee shops and galleries nearby, and let the afternoon unfold. Once you've found one good antique haunt in the QC, you'll find yourself returning, and Del-Rich has the kind of reliable charm that keeps people coming back.

entities.name: Devils Glen Park
entities.hours: Monday: 6:00 AM – 10:30 PM | Tuesday: 6:00 AM – 10:30 PM | Wednesday: 6:00 AM – 10:30 PM | Thursday: 6:00 AM – 10:30 PM | Friday: 6:00 AM – 10:30 PM | Saturday: 6:00 AM – 10:30 PM | Sunday: 6:00 AM – 10:30 PM
entities.description:
Wooded creek trails and disc golf in Bettendorf's best-kept outdoor corner.

Devils Glen feels like the QC's secret — the kind of park where you can actually hear the creek over the trees. The trails wind close enough to the water that you'll spot it through the brush, and they're worn enough to know they're used but not so packed that it feels crowded. If you're throwing disc, the course threads through the same woods, and if you're just here with kids or a dog, the playground and fire pit area give you reasons to linger past sunset.

Devils Glen sits on Bettendorf's east side, far enough from downtown to feel genuinely removed but close enough that it's a 10-minute drive from most of the city. It's the kind of place that rewards a second visit — once you know the trails, you'll find yourself coming back on weekday mornings or weekend afternoons, depending on your mood. The park opens early and stays open late, which means it works whether you're looking for a quick morning walk or an evening with a fire.

entities.name: Disc Replay Davenport
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 7:00 PM
entities.description:
The QC's go-to for used games, DVDs, and the occasional diamond in the rough.

Disc Replay is organized chaos in the best way — rows of pre-owned games sorted by console, shelves of DVDs organized well enough to actually find what you're looking for, and a steady hum of people hunting for that one title they've been chasing. The staff knows their inventory and aren't afraid to dig, and the trade-in counter moves steady, which means fresh stock keeps rolling in. Whether you're hunting retro Nintendo or just looking to offload last year's games at fair price, there's a reason locals keep coming back.

Disc Replay sits in Davenport and pulls from a wide radius — gamers, collectors, and casual browsers all make the trip. It's the kind of place that rewards repeat visits; the inventory turns over enough that you might find something unexpected every time you walk in. For anyone building a collection or just looking to spend less on entertainment, it's become a reliable corner of the Quad Cities retail landscape.

entities.name: Doland Jewelers Inc
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 3:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
A fourth-generation jeweler where they still size rings while you wait.

Doland's has the feel of a place that's earned its 4.9 rating the slow way — by doing the same thing well for decades. Walk in and you're greeted by cases of engagement rings, watches, and repairs in progress; the staff knows the difference between what looks good and what actually lasts. Whether you're after a specific stone, need a family heirloom restored, or just want a straight answer about what you're buying, there's no pressure and no pretense.

Doland Jewelers sits in Davenport, rooted in the kind of local business that anchors a downtown. It's the jeweler people return to — for engagements, anniversaries, and the small ceremonies that matter — and that loyalty shows in the reviews. Once you've trusted them with something precious, you keep coming back.

entities.name: Donut Delite
entities.hours: Monday: 5:00 AM – 3:00 PM | Tuesday: 5:00 AM – 3:00 PM | Wednesday: 5:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Thursday: 5:00 AM – 3:00 PM | Friday: 5:00 AM – 3:00 PM | Saturday: 5:00 AM – 1:00 PM | Sunday: 6:00 AM – 1:00 PM
entities.description:
Old-school donuts made fresh before dawn, gone by lunch.

Donut Delite opens at 5 a.m. for a reason — they're frying and glazing in real time, and by mid-morning the cases are picked over by people who know. The rhythm here is tight and purposeful: you order at the counter, watch them box your dozen, and you're out. It's the kind of place where regulars have standing orders and the staff knows what you want before you say it.

Tucked into Moline, Donut Delite is a neighborhood anchor that's been doing one thing well for long enough that it shows up consistently in locals' weekend routines. It's an easy stop on the way to somewhere else, or a reason to get up early on a Saturday — the kind of genuine, no-frills spot that reminds you why the QC's best places aren't always the newest ones.

entities.name: Donuts & More
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 5:30 AM – 1:00 PM | Wednesday: 5:30 AM – 1:00 PM | Thursday: 5:30 AM – 1:00 PM | Friday: 5:30 AM – 1:00 PM | Saturday: 5:30 AM – 1:00 PM | Sunday: 5:30 AM – 1:00 PM
entities.description:
Early-morning institution where the donuts are hot and the line moves fast.

Donuts & More opens at 5:30 AM—earlier than most of Davenport is awake—and by 6:15 the counter is slammed with regulars who know exactly what they want. The cases rotate through the day, so you're gambling a little on what's left by mid-morning, but that's part of the charm; come early and you'll catch the glazed still warm enough to leave fingerprints. It's a grab-and-go operation that's been doing the same thing, the same way, for long enough that it feels like an institution.

Donuts & More sits in Davenport proper, the kind of place that catches you before work, before errands, before the day really starts. It's become the sort of spot that shows up in conversations between locals—the one you recommend to friends visiting from out of town, the one you remember when you're craving something uncomplicated and done right. Once you've caught it at the right hour, it earns a permanent place in your rotation.

entities.name: Doodads Estate Sales
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 3:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Vintage treasure hunting in a downtown Davenport rabbit hole.

Doodads is the kind of place where you lose track of time — cramped aisles stacked with mid-century furniture, old glassware, framed prints you didn't know you needed, and the occasional find that makes the whole trip worth it. The inventory turns fast enough that regulars check back weekly, never sure what's landed on the shelves. It's cluttered and lived-in, the opposite of a sterile antique mall, which is exactly why people come back.

Doodads sits in Downtown Davenport, steps from the riverfront and easy to combine with a coffee run or lunch nearby. It's a place that rewards curiosity and patience — the kind of spot that shows up on local guides because the community actually uses it, trades tips about finds, and keeps the energy alive. Once you've spent an afternoon picking through the stacks, you'll understand why it's become a QC fixture.

entities.name: Dot's Pots
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
A potter's studio and shop where clay becomes dinner plates and conversation.

Dot's Pots feels like stepping into someone's working life — because you are. The studio is spare and purposeful, shelves lined with handmade vessels in earth tones and jewel glazes, each one slightly different from the last. You can watch the making happen here, and Dot herself will tell you which pieces are fresh from the kiln and which have been waiting for the right person to take them home.

Dot's Pots sits in Greater Tartan Oaks, a quieter corner of Moline where you have to know where you're going — which is exactly why it feels like finding something real. It's the kind of place that appears in our guides because it represents what the QC does best: real people making real things, and opening their doors to share it.

entities.name: Downtown Bettendorf Organization
entities.hours: Monday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Tuesday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: Closed | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
The folks actually making Downtown Bettendorf happen, day in and day out.

Downtown Bettendorf Organization is the beating heart of the neighborhood's revival — not a destination in itself, but the reason destinations exist here at all. Stop by their office during business hours and you'll find real people working real angles: tracking which storefronts are opening, connecting owners with resources, dreaming up the kind of small-scale events that turn a street into a place people actually want to be. They're the ones who know every empty corner and every new lease signing before it hits social media.

The DBO sits right in the middle of Bettendorf's downtown corridor, the nerve center of a neighborhood that's quietly become one of the QC's most interesting pockets. If you're scouting a new restaurant location, looking to understand what's really happening in the area, or just curious about where the community energy is flowing, they're worth knowing. This is the kind of local infrastructure that separates thriving neighborhoods from ones that just exist.

entities.name: Downtown Davenport
entities.description:
The QC's most walkable stretch, lined with character and real reasons to linger.

Downtown Davenport is where the river pulls closest to the streets, and you can feel the difference—there's actual foot traffic here, actual energy. The blocks between 2nd and 4th Streets hold galleries, restaurants, and bars that aren't chains, mixed in with older storefronts that haven't been smoothed over. Walk it on a Friday evening and you'll see why locals keep coming back: it's the rare pocket of a midsize river city that actually feels lived-in.

Downtown Davenport is the QC's most characterful pocket—a short walk from the riverfront, and easy to fold into a longer evening. It shows up again and again on our curated guides because it's genuinely worth your time, whether you're grabbing coffee, catching live music, or just seeing what the city looks like when it's paying attention to itself.

entities.name: Duck City Bistro
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 5:00 – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 5:00 – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 5:00 – 9:00 PM | Friday: 5:00 – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 5:00 – 10:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Candlelit dinners and a globally curious menu in downtown Davenport's most established room.

Duck City Bistro has been doing this long enough to know what it's doing — the kind of place where the bartender remembers regulars and the kitchen moves with quiet confidence. The dining room glows warm under low lights, intimate without feeling forced, and the menu pulls from everywhere: you might find yourself choosing between duck confit and something bright with Southeast Asian spice. There's a lounge that invites lingering, a full bar that takes itself seriously without pretension, and the kind of consistency that keeps people coming back for anniversaries and quiet midweek nights alike.

Duck City Bistro sits on Downtown Davenport's main stretch, where the best restaurants and bars cluster within walking distance of the riverfront. Nearly a thousand five-star reviews speak to something real — this is the kind of place that anchors a neighborhood, that shows up on locals' lists of "where to take someone special," and that proves the QC has restaurants worth the drive.

entities.name: Dunn Brothers Coffee
entities.hours: Monday: 5:30 AM – 5:00 PM | Tuesday: 5:30 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 5:30 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 5:30 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 5:30 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 6:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Sunday: 6:00 AM – 2:00 PM
entities.description:
Sustainable espresso and cold brew from a coffeehouse that shows up early and stays consistent.

Dunn Brothers opens at 5:30 on weekdays—earlier than most of Davenport—which means it's already humming when you need it. The beans are sourced with care, and whether you're ordering a pulled shot or a smooth cold brew on a humid summer morning, there's a thoughtfulness to what lands in your cup. The Birchwood Fields location has the relaxed feel of a place that knows its regulars and doesn't need to try too hard.

Dunn Brothers sits on the east side of Davenport, a bit removed from the downtown riverfront but exactly where a neighborhood coffeehouse should be—convenient, reliable, built for the people who live nearby. It's the kind of spot that doesn't make headlines but earns its loyal following by doing the fundamentals well, day after day. That consistency is what keeps it on our radar.

entities.name: D’Savor House
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM
entities.description:
Global comfort food where flavors travel further than the menu suggests.

D'Savor House trades in the kind of cooking that doesn't fit neatly into one category—you'll find Caribbean spices rubbing shoulders with classic American technique, all of it grounded in real technique and fresh ingredients. The space itself is unpretentious; you're here for the food, not the ambiance, though the Saturday morning crowds suggest plenty of locals have already figured that out. Order with curiosity and you'll understand why 81 reviews average 4.6 stars.

East Moline doesn't get as much traffic as downtown Davenport or Bettendorf's main drag, which means D'Savor House operates a little under the radar—a genuine neighborhood spot rather than a destination everyone's already heard about. It's the kind of place worth the short drive across the river, the kind that makes you want to tell someone about it.

entities.name: East Moline Coffee Company
entities.hours: Monday: 6:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Tuesday: 6:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 6:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 6:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 6:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Sunday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM
entities.description:
A neighborhood spot where East Moline gathers before work and stays for the warmth.

East Moline Coffee Company has the feel of a place that's earned its 4.7 rating through consistency and genuine care—the kind of coffee shop where regulars have their order memorized and newcomers immediately understand why. The early opens (6 a.m. weekdays) catch the commute crowd, but it's the actual space that keeps people lingering: real conversation happens here, not just caffeine transactions. By mid-morning, it's a mix of locals catching up over breakfast and remote workers who've claimed a table for the long haul.

East Moline doesn't get as much spotlight as downtown Davenport or the District, but that's partly what makes it worth knowing—it's where the QC actually lives. East Moline Coffee Company sits at the heart of that everyday rhythm, and nearly 400 reviews averaging nearly five stars suggest this is the kind of neighborhood anchor that deserves a visit if you're exploring beyond the tourist corridors.

entities.name: East Moline Public Library
entities.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 3:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
A quiet corner of East Moline where curiosity still gets a librarian's full attention.

The East Moline Public Library keeps the kind of hours that actually work — open until 8 p.m. most weeknights, which means you can stop by after work or school without rushing. The staff here remembers what libraries were supposed to be: a place where someone will help you find what you're looking for, whether that's a specific book, research help, or just a reliable table and decent light. It's the sort of place that hasn't been glossed over by trends, which means it feels genuinely yours to use.

East Moline doesn't get as much attention in the QC's community conversation as it deserves, but this library is exactly why locals know better. It's a straightforward public good — maintained, staffed, and open when people actually need it — which is increasingly rare enough to be worth knowing about. If you live or work on this side of the river, it's worth a visit.

entities.name: ECDYSIAST ARTs MUSEUM
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 7:00 – 11:00 PM | Thursday: 7:00 – 11:00 PM | Friday: 7:00 – 11:00 PM | Saturday: 7:00 – 11:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
An intimate art space that opens Wednesday through Saturday nights in downtown.

Ecdysiast Arts Museum operates on its own schedule — Wednesday through Saturday evenings only — which means showing up feels like being let in on something. The space itself is spare and intentional, built around live art and performance in a way that rewards close attention. There's no velvet rope energy here; it's the kind of place where you might end up talking to whoever's running it about what you're seeing.

Ecdysiast sits on a quiet block of downtown Davenport, part of a growing cluster of independent galleries and studios that are slowly redefining what the riverfront corridor can be. It's worth planning an evening around — check their schedule, make the trip, and expect something that won't show up in the usual tourist guides. That's exactly why it belongs in ours.

entities.name: Eddy's Snack Bar
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM
entities.description:
A neighborhood institution slinging loose burgers and validation since forever.

Eddy's looks like it stepped out of the 1970s and decided to stay put — wood paneling, a counter that's seen thousands of elbows, booths worn soft by regulars who've claimed the same seats for decades. The burgers are loose and charred at the edges, the kind that fall apart a little when you bite them, served with a handful of fries and zero pretense. Wednesday through Sunday, the place fills with families, old-timers, and anyone who knows that the best meals in the QC don't come with Instagram potential — they come with a 4.8-star rating from 700 people who'd rather eat here than anywhere else.

Eddy's sits in Glen Armil, a quieter Davenport neighborhood that doesn't get as much attention as the riverfront or downtown, but has its own slow, steady rhythm. It's exactly the kind of place — unpretentious, consistent, genuinely beloved — that makes a community guide worth reading. Once you've been, you'll understand why it keeps showing up in conversations about where to actually eat in the Quad Cities.

entities.name: Egg Parlor
entities.hours: Monday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Tuesday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Thursday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Friday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Saturday: 7:00 AM – 2:30 PM | Sunday: 7:00 AM – 2:30 PM
entities.description:
Silvis breakfast spot where the eggs are the main event, not an afterthought.

Egg Parlor does one thing and does it well — turns out omelets, scrambles, and benedicts that justify the early wake-up. The dining room fills quick on weekend mornings with a steady stream of regulars who know their usual order by heart. Yolks are cooked to spec, toast arrives properly buttered, and the coffee stays hot — the kind of place where breakfast feels like it matters.

Egg Parlor sits in Silvis, just off the main drag, easy to reach from anywhere in the QC before 2 p.m. It's unpretentious and well-loved — nearly a thousand solid reviews speak to the consistency — which is exactly why it belongs on any local's shortlist. If you're hunting for honest breakfast that doesn't try to be something it's not, this is the place.

entities.name: Elevate Trampoline Park
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM
entities.description:
A warehouse full of trampolines where kids burn energy and adults remember how to fly.

Elevate is exactly what it promises — wall-to-wall bouncing surfaces in a converted space that smells like rubber and sounds like controlled chaos in the best way. There's a dodgeball court suspended above the floor, a foam pit for the fearless, and enough springs underfoot that even a modest jump feels like defying gravity. Parents camp on the sidelines with coffee while their kids lose themselves for an hour, coming out sweaty, grinning, genuinely tired.

Elevate sits in Davenport and fills a real gap on rainy days or when the energy in your house has nowhere else to go. It's the kind of place that earns its 4.3-star rating through sheer usefulness — not Instagram-worthy, not trying to be anything fancier than what it is, just reliably good at what it does. If you've got kids and you're looking for an afternoon that actually works, this is where the QC parents know to look.

entities.name: Elm Street Apothecary
entities.description:
Apothecary goods and curiosities for people who actually care what they put on their skin.

Elm Street Apothecary feels like walking into someone's carefully edited medicine cabinet — all thoughtful brands, small-batch salves, and the kind of products that work because they're made by people who sweat the details. The space itself is unhurried; you can actually pick things up, read labels, ask questions without feeling rushed toward a register. It's the kind of place where the staff knows the difference between their soaps, and they'll tell you why one might work better for your skin than another.

Elm Street has become a quiet anchor on Davenport's east side — the kind of neighborhood shop that rewards exploration if you venture past the main drags. It's the sort of place that shows up again and again in our guides because people return to it, not out of obligation, but because they've learned to trust what's there. Once you've found it, you'll notice yourself recommending it to friends.

entities.name: Emeis Golf Course
entities.description:
Nine holes of honest golf on Davenport's south side.

Emeis is the kind of course that doesn't try to be something it's not — tight fairways, real greens, and enough trees to keep you honest without feeling punitive. It's the perfect length for a quick round before work or an evening when you've got two hours to kill, and the regulars know every slope and trouble spot. The pace moves steady here; you're not waiting on anyone.

Emeis sits on Davenport's south side, away from the riverfront tourist corridor but exactly where the locals go to play. It's a nine-holer built for people who actually golf, not just talk about it — the kind of neighborhood course that anchors a community and builds real loyalty over years. Once you've played here, you understand why it keeps showing up on the scorecard.

entities.name: Envied Vintage Clothing Etc
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 12:00 – 6:00 PM | Thursday: 12:00 – 6:00 PM | Friday: 12:00 – 6:00 PM | Saturday: 12:00 – 6:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 6:00 PM
entities.description:
Curated vintage finds in a downtown Davenport spot that rewards slow browsing.

Envied is the kind of place where you lose track of time pulling through racks of 70s blazers and faded band tees, each piece feeling like it was actually chosen rather than just accumulated. The selection tilts toward genuine vintage — not costume-party throwaway — and the prices reflect that care. Come on a weekend afternoon when you're not rushed; the real treasures are hiding in the back.

Envied sits in Downtown Davenport, steps from the riverfront and the cluster of galleries, bars, and independent shops that make this neighborhood worth circling back to. It's closed midweek but opens Wednesday through Sunday, which means planning a visit actually feels intentional — the kind of place that rewards a dedicated afternoon of exploring, not a random errand run.

entities.name: Exotic Imports
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Global oddities and home goods tucked into Moline's understated retail stretch.

Exotic Imports feels like wandering through someone's well-curated travels — the kind of shop where you'll find hand-carved wooden masks next to Moroccan tea glasses, then stumble on a shelf of Indian spices you didn't know you needed. The inventory rotates enough that regulars keep coming back, and the staff moves through the narrow aisles with the ease of people who actually know their stock. Saturday mornings draw the crowd, but weekday afternoons are quieter — better for lingering and actually thinking about whether that Balinese throw pillow fits your life.

Exotic Imports anchors a quieter corner of Moline, away from the chain-heavy stretches but close enough to downtown that it's worth the drive. It's become one of those places locals send their visiting friends when they want to show off the QC's actual character — proof that thoughtful, independent retail still thrives here if you know where to look.

entities.name: Exotic Thai
entities.description:
Traditional Thai done right, with a menu deep enough to explore for years.

Exotic Thai keeps things honest — pad thai that tastes like it should, curries built on real spice layering, and enough lesser-known dishes to reward regulars who venture past the standards. The room is unpretentious and roomy, the kind of place where families spread out on Saturday nights and you can actually hear your table talk. Orders arrive thoughtfully timed, never rushed, and the lunch specials are the kind of deal that makes you wonder why you don't go more often.

Exotic Thai sits in Davenport and has earned the kind of steady reputation that comes from doing one thing well for years — nearly 900 reviews and a 4.5 rating aren't accidents. It's the rare local restaurant that's become genuinely irreplaceable to the QC's food landscape, the place you recommend to friends visiting and the one you return to when you want Thai that tastes like Thailand.

entities.name: Family Museum
entities.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
A hands-on museum where kids actually want to spend the afternoon.

The Family Museum in Bettendorf is the rare place where your kids run toward the exhibits instead of away from them. The play-based setup means they're learning about art and science without realizing it — building, experimenting, dancing through the galleries instead of standing still in front of glass cases. The energy is purposeful but not chaotic; you can actually breathe while your kids are engaged.

The Family Museum sits in Bettendorf, across the river and worth the drive when you need a morning or afternoon that doesn't involve a screen. It's the kind of cultural anchor that makes the QC feel like a place where families can actually root themselves — the sort of spot that turns a Saturday into something memorable instead of just something to survive.

entities.name: Famous Dave's Bar-B-Que
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM
entities.description:
Wood-smoked ribs and brisket in a sprawling Western-themed room.

Famous Dave's leans into the saloon aesthetic—think reclaimed wood, neon signs, and enough Americana on the walls to fill three roadside motels—but the real draw is the smoke. The ribs come fall-off-the-bone tender, the brisket has a proper bark, and the sides (creamed corn, baked beans) don't feel like afterthoughts. It's the kind of place where families spread out across big booths and the bar stays animated on weekends.

Famous Dave's sits in Davenport, anchoring one of the area's reliable casual-dining options for folks craving legitimately smoked meat without the pretense. It's a chain, yes, but the local location delivers consistent results and a comfortable enough space to linger over a plate and a drink. If you're new to the QC or just want low-stakes barbecue that doesn't disappoint, it's worth a Friday or Saturday night.

entities.name: Fancy Nails & Spa
entities.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 7:30 PM | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 7:30 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 7:30 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 7:30 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 7:30 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Sunday: 10:30 AM – 5:00 PM
entities.description:
A steady neighborhood spot for manicures, pedicures, and a quiet afternoon out.

Fancy Nails keeps things straightforward — bright station lights, the familiar hum of files and water, the kind of place where regulars know their technicians by name. You can slip in for a quick manicure on a lunch break or sink into one of the pedicure chairs for the full treatment; the pace here is unhurried enough that you won't feel rushed. The Facebook page stays active with seasonal specials, and the Google reviews skew toward people who've been coming back for years.

Fancy Nails sits in Davenport proper, part of the neighborhood beauty-and-wellness fabric that keeps locals looking polished without the pretense. It's the kind of reliable, no-fuss spot that belongs in a real community guide — not flashy, but genuinely useful when you need an afternoon of care that doesn't require a drive across town or a big production.

entities.name: Fashion Nails
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Reliable manicures and pedicures in a calm, no-fuss Bettendorf salon.

Fashion Nails has the kind of steady reputation that comes from doing the basics really well — clean stations, attentive technicians, and a quiet room where you can actually relax. Whether you're in for a quick polish change or a full set, the work is consistent and the pace unhurried. It's the sort of place regulars return to again and again, which the Google reviews make pretty clear.

Fashion Nails sits in Bettendorf, the QC's quieter, tree-lined counterpart to Davenport — the kind of neighborhood where you notice when a business sticks around and builds real loyalty. It's the place to bookmark for when you need care that's straightforward and well-executed, without the pressure or pretense.

entities.name: Fifth Avenue Syndicate
entities.description:
A Moline mainstay where craft cocktails meet genuine neighborhood warmth.

Fifth Avenue Syndicate feels like the kind of place that knows your name by your second visit — the bartenders are sharp with a drink, the crowd leans local, and there's a real sense of ease about the whole operation. Whether you're stopping in for an early happy hour or settling in for the evening, the drinks are considered and well-made, never trying too hard. It's the rare spot that works equally well for a weeknight decompression or a proper night out.

Fifth Avenue Syndicate anchors Moline's evolving nightlife scene, sitting in a neighborhood that's quietly becoming the QC's most interesting after-dark destination. It's the kind of bar that shows up again and again in conversations about where locals actually go — not because it's trendy or new, but because it's genuinely good at what it does. Once you've been, you'll understand why it's earned its place as a community favorite.

entities.name: Figge Art Museum
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 5:00 PM
entities.description:
A serious art museum with Wright's fingerprints and work that stops you cold.

The Figge sits right on the river in a building that commands attention, and inside, the collection feels bigger than you'd expect—Frank Lloyd Wright drawings that show his thinking, international pieces that remind you art doesn't end at the Mississippi, and rotating shows that actually take risks. Thursday nights stretch the hours to 8 p.m., which means you can wander the galleries after work and end up standing in front of something that changes how you see the QC. The gift shop is genuinely curated, not the usual museum store filler.

The Figge anchors downtown Davenport's cultural spine, a short walk from the riverfront and the kind of place that justifies a whole afternoon or a quick hour depending on your mood. It's where locals bring visiting friends when they want to prove the QC has depth, and where you'll find yourself coming back—not out of obligation, but because there's always something new on the walls.

entities.name: Five Cities Brewing - Taproom
entities.hours: Monday: 3:00 – 10:00 PM | Tuesday: 3:00 – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 3:00 – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 3:00 – 10:00 PM | Friday: 3:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM
entities.description:
Local brewhouse where the beer is solid and the crowd is genuine.

Five Cities Brewing feels like the kind of place that's been part of Bettendorf longer than it probably has — wood-lined taproom, a real bar, the kind of energy that builds as the sun sets. The house beers are approachable without being forgettable; you'll find regulars working through a flight on Thursday nights and families claiming a corner table on Saturday afternoons. The space has room to breathe, which means you can actually hear the person across from you, a small luxury in the modern taproom scene.

Five Cities Brewing sits in Bettendorf, the quieter side of the river — a place where people actually live and linger, not just pass through. It's the kind of neighborhood spot that anchors a weekend, the one you bring out-of-town friends to because it's unpretentious and it works. Once you've found it, you'll keep coming back.

entities.name: Five Star Salon
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Saturday: 8:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
East Davenport's go-to for color, cuts, and unhurried attention.

Five Star has the feel of a neighborhood spot that's earned its reputation slowly—the kind of place where stylists remember what you wanted last time. Whether you're in for a refresh or a full transformation, there's no sense of rushing through; the schedule seems built around actually talking through what works for your hair. Saturday mornings fill up fast, which tells you everything about how the regulars feel.

Five Star sits in the Village of East Davenport, a quieter residential corner of the city that rewards a short drive with genuine community finds. It's the kind of salon that shows up again and again on our guides because it does one thing well—makes people feel genuinely cared for—and that matters more than any trend ever will.

entities.name: Flip's Pancake House - Utica Ridge
entities.hours: Monday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Tuesday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Thursday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Friday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Saturday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Sunday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM
entities.description:
Griddle-cooked pancakes and a booth where regulars have claimed their spots for years.

Flip's is the kind of breakfast place where the coffee arrives before you've settled in, and the pancakes come out golden and thin—cooked on an actual griddle, not a flat-top. The booths are worn comfortable, the waitstaff knows what you want before you order, and on Saturday mornings the place hums with the particular energy of people who've made this their weekend ritual. You'll see families, retirees reading the paper, and hungover college kids all sharing the same quiet gratitude for eggs done right.

Flip's sits on Utica Ridge in Davenport, a neighborhood strip of solid, unpretentious places that serve the people who actually live here—not tourists passing through. It's the kind of spot that builds loyalty through consistency rather than novelty, and those 331 Google reviews aren't from people seeking it out; they're from people who come back. Once you find it, you'll understand why it belongs on any real map of the Quad Cities.

entities.name: Fred & Ethel's 50's Antiques
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 12:00 – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 12:00 – 5:00 PM | Friday: 12:00 – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Mid-century treasures and vintage Americana packed into one Rock Island corner.

Fred & Ethel's feels like stepping into someone's carefully curated basement—the good kind, where every shelf tells a story. You'll find chrome dinette sets, vintage Coca-Cola signs, old vinyl, and the kind of kitschy ceramic pieces that somehow work perfectly in a modern kitchen. The owners clearly know their inventory; ask about anything and you'll get the real history, not a sales pitch.

Fred & Ethel's sits on Rock Island's quieter edges, worth the short drive from downtown or the riverfront if you're serious about vintage hunting. It's the kind of place that rewards a weekend afternoon—limited hours mean you plan around them, but that's part of the charm. Once you've found a mid-century treasure here, you'll be back.

entities.name: Freight House Farmers Market
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: Closed | Thursday: Closed | Friday: Closed | Saturday: 8:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Sunday: 10:00 AM – 2:00 PM
entities.description:
Saturday and Sunday mornings where local farmers sell what they actually grew.

The Freight House fills with vendors before 8 a.m. on Saturdays — farmers with dirt still under their fingernails, bakers whose bread is still warm, preserves in mason jars that taste like someone's recipe, not a formula. You'll find the regulars staking the same spots week to week, which means if you want the good tomatoes or the fresh eggs, you show up early. It's the kind of place where the person selling you something grew it themselves, which changes how the whole transaction feels.

Freight House Farmers Market sits in Downtown Davenport, a neighborhood that's learned to do community gathering well — the riverfront, the restaurants, the galleries all within walking distance. Weekend mornings here have become a ritual for people who care about where their food comes from, and once you've bought from the farmers directly, the grocery store starts to feel like a step backward. It's worth building your Saturday or Sunday around.

entities.name: Front Street Pub & Eatery
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM
entities.description:
A century-old warehouse where house beers and brick walls define downtown nightlife.

Front Street fills a real space—exposed brick, heavy wood, the kind of bones that only come with age—and it knows what it's doing with a beer list that leans into their own pours. The beer garden spills out onto the street when weather allows, turning a corner of downtown into the kind of place where you bump into people you know. Food leans comfort: solid burgers, wings that don't pretend to be anything fancy, the kind of eating that makes sense at a bar.

Front Street sits on the riverfront edge of Downtown Davenport, steps from the Mississippi and woven into the fabric of Friday and Saturday nights. It's the rare local brewery that's been doing this long enough to feel like it belongs to the neighborhood rather than the other way around—the kind of anchor that shows up in the background of a hundred QC photos and stories.

entities.name: Fusion Salon and Spa
entities.hours: Monday: 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Tuesday: 8:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Wednesday: 8:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 8:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Color, cuts, and skilled hands in a Davenport spa with real staying power.

Fusion has built a loyal following because the stylists actually listen — they're not rushing through appointments or pushing trends you didn't ask for. The space feels calm and intentional, with an emphasis on both hair and skin services, which means you can handle your whole self-care day in one place. The Google reviews speak for themselves: people come back, and they bring their friends.

Fusion sits in Davenport, where the best local businesses thrive on word-of-mouth and genuine expertise. It's the kind of place that shows up again and again on our curated guides — not because it's flashy, but because it delivers consistency and care. Once you've found your person here, you're booked months ahead.

entities.name: Fyre Lake Golf Club
entities.hours: Monday: 7:30 AM – 5:00 PM | Tuesday: 7:30 AM – 7:00 PM | Wednesday: 7:30 AM – 7:00 PM | Thursday: 7:30 AM – 7:00 PM | Friday: 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Saturday: 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Sunday: 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM
entities.description:
A well-kept 18-hole course just far enough out to feel like an escape.

Fyre Lake sits on the edge of things—far enough from town that you're genuinely away, but close enough that a Saturday morning round doesn't require a full expedition. The course has the kind of maintenance that speaks to care: fairways that hold their shape, greens that reward precision. Early birds can tee off before 7 AM most days, and by afternoon the pace slows enough to actually enjoy the walk.

Sherrard is a quieter corner of the QC, the kind of place people pass through on their way to somewhere else—which is precisely why Fyre Lake works. It's a solid local course with a real following (those 4.6 stars aren't random), the sort of place where regulars book their standing tee times and the pro shop knows what you're looking for before you ask. If you golf in the QC, you've probably heard about it by now.

entities.name: GATHERED GOODS
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Sunday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM
entities.description:
Thoughtfully curated home goods and gifts from makers you'll actually want to know.

Gathered Goods feels like walking into someone's really good taste — the kind of place where every shelf holds something with a story. The selection skews toward local and independent makers: ceramics that feel handmade, textiles with actual character, the sort of gifts that make people ask where you found them. Wednesday through Sunday afternoons are the sweet spot, when you can actually breathe and talk to the owner about what's new.

Gathered Goods sits in Davenport, where the retail landscape is shifting toward these kinds of intentional, owner-driven spaces. It's the type of shop that rewards a slow browse — the kind you end up returning to not because you needed anything in particular, but because you trust what's there. Once you've found it, it becomes part of your regular rotation.

entities.name: Geneseo Brewing Co.
entities.hours: Monday: 4:00 – 10:00 PM | Tuesday: 4:00 – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 4:00 – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 4:00 – 10:00 PM | Friday: 4:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Saturday: 12:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Sunday: 4:00 – 9:00 PM
entities.description:
A small-town brewery where the beer is good and the regulars know your name.

Geneseo Brewing Co. feels like the living room of a place that actually needed one — wood-lined, unpretentious, the kind of spot where you can hear conversations across the room. The beer list rotates enough to keep people coming back, and the staff has that rare gift of remembering what you ordered last time. Weekend afternoons draw families and dogs; Friday nights tip toward the rowdier end of friendly.

Geneseo sits just outside the main Quad Cities sprawl, which is exactly why Geneseo Brewing matters — it's the kind of destination that reminds you the QC extends far beyond the river. It's worth the short drive from downtown or the interstate for an evening that feels genuinely local, not curated for tourists.

entities.name: Genesius Guild
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: Closed | Thursday: Closed | Friday: Closed | Saturday: 8:00 – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 7:00 – 10:00 PM
entities.description:
Community theater where the real magic happens on weekend nights.

Genesius Guild operates on a different calendar than most places in the QC — they're only open Friday through Sunday evenings, and that scarcity makes their shows feel special. Walking into their KeyStone space, you're stepping into a room built by people who care about the work: intimate seating, genuine acoustics, the kind of production that reminds you why theater matters. Whether it's a classic or an original piece, there's an earnestness here that translates directly to the audience.

The Guild sits in Rock Island's KeyStone neighborhood, a pocket of the QC that rewards curiosity and wandering. This is the kind of venue that shows up in our guides not because it's flashy, but because it represents what happens when a community commits to making art on its own terms — weekend nights, limited hours, full commitment.

entities.name: German American Heritage Center & Museum
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 4:00 PM
entities.description:
Three centuries of German-American life preserved in one Downtown building.

The German American Heritage Center lives in a restored Victorian mansion on Brady Street, and walking through feels like stepping into your grandparents' attic — if your grandparents were meticulous curators. You'll find old photographs, hand-stitched textiles, brewing equipment, and letters that tell the story of why so many German families chose the Quad Cities. The rooms are intimate and unhurried; you can spend twenty minutes or two hours depending on how deep the rabbit hole goes.

The Center anchors Downtown Davenport's cultural corridor, a short walk from the riverfront and the other galleries and shops that make this neighborhood worth a Saturday afternoon. It's a place that reveals how much the QC's identity is rooted in its immigrant communities — and once you've walked through, you'll understand why German-American heritage still shapes everything from the food we eat to the festivals we celebrate.

entities.name: Ginza Hibachi and Ramen
entities.description:
Hibachi theatrics and proper ramen in the heart of Moline.

Ginza splits the difference between two kinds of Japanese cooking — sit at the counter and watch chefs work the flat-top with real skill, flames and spatulas and showmanship, or slip into a booth with a bowl of broth that's been built right. The ramen comes properly: noodles with backbone, toppings that taste like they belong there. It's the kind of place where families celebrate and where people who know what they're doing eat alone at the bar.

Ginza sits in Moline, anchoring a stretch of the QC that's quietly become more interesting over the last few years. It's easy to get to, easy to park, and the kind of dinner — whether you want performance or quiet focus — that reminds you why the Quad Cities' food scene keeps getting better. Once you've had the ramen or watched a chef work the hibachi, you'll find reasons to come back.

entities.name: Glynns Creek Golf Course
entities.hours: Monday: 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Tuesday: 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Thursday: 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Friday: 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Saturday: 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Sunday: 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM
entities.description:
Public course where locals actually want to play, morning to dusk.

Glynns Creek feels like the golf course your neighbor has been telling you about — well-maintained greens that don't require a membership fee, and a pace that stays reasonable even on weekend mornings. The layout moves you through Long Grove's quieter landscape, the kind of place where you notice the light changing through the round and remember why you started playing in the first place. Rates stay friendly, the staff knows the regulars by name, and there's a straightforward charm that beats the pretense.

Glynns Creek sits in Long Grove, just far enough outside the main QC corridor to feel like an actual escape while staying accessible — an easy drive from Bettendorf or Davenport for anyone looking to spend a morning or afternoon doing something that matters to them. It's the kind of local spot that shows up again and again on our guides because it's where the community actually shows up, week after week, and once you've played it, you'll understand why.

entities.name: Golden Royal Barbershop
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Sunday: 10:00 AM – 2:00 PM
entities.description:
A precise, no-nonsense barbershop where the clippers know what they're doing.

Golden Royal has the kind of reputation that builds quietly — 4.8 stars from people who actually care about their haircuts. The shop runs tight: you'll see the same barbers working methodically through Saturday mornings, the kind of place where regulars don't need to explain what they want. Walk in on a Friday afternoon and you're joining a steady stream of guys who've learned that a good cut here is worth the short wait.

Golden Royal sits in Moline, anchoring a part of the QC that doesn't get as much attention as downtown but absolutely deserves it. It's the kind of neighborhood spot — reliable, skilled, unpretentious — that we keep coming back to in our guides because it's exactly what a community needs and what visitors overlook. Once you've found your barber here, you'll understand why the regulars keep returning.

entities.name: Gramma's Kitchen & Checkered Flag
entities.hours: Monday: 7:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 7:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 7:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 7:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 7:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 7:00 AM – 9:00 PM
entities.description: Breakfast & classic American comfort food served in a casual restaurant with vintage decor.

entities.name: Gravastar Coffee (Formerly Coffee Hound)
entities.hours: Monday: 6:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Tuesday: 6:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Wednesday: 6:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Thursday: 6:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Friday: 6:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Saturday: 6:30 AM – 6:00 PM | Sunday: 7:00 AM – 6:00 PM
entities.description:
Specialty coffee and pastries in a Bettendorf spot that feels like a local secret.

Gravastar pulls smooth espresso and pours deliberate pour-overs in a space that's grown into a genuine neighborhood anchor since its Coffee Hound days. The pastry case rotates with real care, and there's enough seating to linger — whether you're reading the paper solo or catching up with someone over an oat milk latte. Weekend mornings pack with the kind of regulars who know the barista's name and the usual order.

Gravastar sits in Bettendorf, where good coffee spots are worth the short drive from the core neighborhoods. It's become the kind of place locals return to again and again, proof that a genuinely well-made cup and a welcoming room are enough to build something that lasts. Once you've found it, you'll understand why it keeps showing up in our guides.

entities.name: Great River Brewery
entities.hours: Monday: 2:00 – 10:00 PM | Tuesday: 2:00 – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 2:00 – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Sunday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM
entities.description:
Downtown brewery where locals gather around house-made beer and solid food.

Great River keeps the taps rotating with a lineup that favors balance over showboating — the kind of place where the IPA doesn't overshadow the lager, and there's always a seasonal pour worth trying. The patio fills up fast on weekends, especially when the weather breaks; it's where you'll find families lingering into early evening and groups of friends working through a flight. The kitchen doesn't pretend to be fine dining, but the food lands right — it's designed to keep you there longer, not to be the reason you came.

Great River anchors a corner of Downtown Davenport that's quietly becoming a real destination. It's walking distance from the riverfront, close enough to fold into an afternoon that includes the parks or a gallery visit, and it's the kind of brewpub that shows up again and again on locals' rotation. Once you've claimed a spot on the patio on a Friday evening, you'll understand why.

entities.name: Green Tree Brewery
entities.hours: Monday: 12:00 – 8:00 PM | Tuesday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM | Friday: 12:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Saturday: 12:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Sunday: 12:00 – 8:00 PM
entities.description:
Approachable craft beer and a genuinely welcoming room in Le Claire.

Green Tree feels like the kind of place that got popular by accident—locals kept showing up because the beer was good and nobody was trying too hard. The tap list leans approachable without dumbing down; you'll find solid IPAs alongside lighter stuff, and the brewers clearly know what they're doing. It's the kind of brewery that works equally well for a Friday night with friends or a quiet Tuesday afternoon with a book.

Green Tree sits in Le Claire, just far enough from downtown Davenport to feel like its own destination but close enough that it's never out of the way. It's become one of those places that locals mention when they're recommending the QC to visitors—not because it's fancy or famous, but because it's genuinely good and genuinely kind.

entities.name: Gulliver's Travel Duck Creek
entities.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
A independent travel agency where someone actually knows your name.

Gulliver's Travel in Bettendorf is the kind of place where the staff remembers you booked that river cruise last spring and asks how it went. They'll spend time mapping out your trip with a real person across a real desk — no endless clicking through websites, no chatbots. Whether you're chasing down a last-minute flight, planning a two-week European adventure, or need someone to vouch for that travel insurance, they've got the expertise and the connections.

Gulliver's sits in Bettendorf, the QC's quieter, more residential sister — a short drive from the river but worlds away from the hustle of downtown. In an age of algorithm-driven travel booking, a travel agency that listens feels rare and worth supporting. It's the kind of local business that shows up in our guides because it solves a real problem the way only a person can.

entities.name: Gypsy Highway
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 11:30 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 11:30 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 11:30 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM
entities.description:
A whiskey bar with serious depth and a room that feels like home.

Gypsy Highway pulls off the rare trick of being both ambitious and approachable — the kind of place where you can order a carefully selected pour of bourbon or just grab a beer and feel equally welcome. The room has that lived-in warmth: dim lighting, wood everywhere, the kind of bar where the bartender actually knows what they're doing and isn't performing for the crowd. Weekends stretch late (until 2 AM on Fridays and Saturdays), which means it works as a dinner destination, a nightcap spot, or the place you end up at midnight when the night's still young.

Gypsy Highway sits in Davenport proper, part of the growing cluster of bars and restaurants that's making the downtown core worth a real night out. It's the kind of establishment that builds a loyal following fast — nearly a thousand five-star reviews speak to that — and once you've settled into one of those seats, you'll understand why locals keep coming back. It belongs in our guides because it does one thing very well and does it with genuine care.

entities.name: H&R Block
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 1:00 – 7:00 PM | Wednesday: Closed | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Friday: Closed | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Tax prep that doesn't make you want to flip a table.

H&R Block on Brady Street has the kind of unhurried approach that actually works—accountants who know the Iowa tax code inside out and aren't rushing you through the door. The appointment slots are real (not overbooked), and they'll walk you through deductions you'd miss on your own. Come April, most places are swamped; here, limited hours mean they actually have time to talk.

This Davenport location sits on the practical edge of the QC's service landscape—the kind of place you don't think about until you need it, then you're relieved it's there. If you're self-employed, own property, or just have taxes complicated enough to warrant a real person looking them over, this is the spot locals keep coming back to. The 4.7 rating isn't an accident.

entities.name: Hafner's Tavern & Liquor Store
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Sunday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM
entities.description:
A Moline institution where regulars have held court for decades.

Hafner's feels like the kind of place where everybody knows your name—and they probably do. The bar has the worn-in comfort of a neighborhood living room, all dim wood and the kind of lighting that makes 10 p.m. feel like the right time to be there. The liquor selection runs deep, and the bartenders move with the easy confidence of people who've poured thousands of drinks in this same spot.

Hafner's sits in Moline, anchoring a quieter corner of the QC that rewards exploration. It's the sort of local bar that shows up on our radar because it's real—no theme, no trying too hard, just a place where the community gathers. Once you walk through that door, you understand why it's earned a devoted following.

entities.name: Hampton Inn & Suites Moline-Quad City Int'l Aprt
entities.description:
Reliable airport lodging with an indoor pool and a real breakfast spread.

The Hampton sits close enough to the airport that you won't spend twenty minutes hunting for your room after landing—but far enough away that you're not listening to jets all night. The indoor pool is the kind of detail that matters when you're traveling with kids or just need to shake off a long drive; the breakfast buffer has the eggs-and-waffle setup that actually fills you up before a day of meetings or exploring. Rooms are clean and straightforward, the Wi-Fi holds, and the staff moves without the exhausted energy you feel at some chain hotels.

The Hampton is in Moline, near the airport—the practical choice when you need a bed that won't surprise you and a location that saves you time. It's the kind of place people come back to because it does the fundamentals right, which is exactly what our guides are built around: spotting the places in the QC worth your attention, whether you're passing through or staying a while.

entities.name: Harris Pizza
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 8:30 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 8:30 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM
entities.description:
Quad City institution slinging thin-crust pizza with toppings you won't find elsewhere.

Harris has been doing Quad City-style pizza—thin, crispy, loaded—long enough that it feels like it's always been there. The toppings run creative and generous: you'll see combinations that shouldn't work but absolutely do, built on a crust that's more snap than chew. Walk in on a Saturday evening and you'll see families claiming booths, the kind of place where the order-taker knows what you're going to say before you say it.

Harris sits in Davenport proper, far enough from the riverfront tourist corridor to feel like a neighborhood spot, but accessible enough that it's become a genuine destination. Nearly 800 Google reviews speak to its staying power—people don't accumulate that kind of loyalty without doing something right, and doing it consistently. This is the kind of place that shows up again and again in our curated guides because it's earned its reputation the old way: one pizza at a time, one satisfied customer at a time.

entities.name: Harris Pizza #1
