entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 8:30 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 8:30 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 8:30 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 8:30 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM
entities.description:
Quad City institution slinging thin-crust pies with toppings you won't find anywhere else.

Harris Pizza #1 has been doing this long enough that the Quad City-style pizza feels like home cooking — thin, crispy crust with a slight char, loaded with combinations that make sense only if you've grown up eating them. The KeyStone location pulls in regulars who've been ordering the same pie for years alongside families testing out creative topping combinations. Order at the counter, grab a seat, and watch how quickly a pizza disappears when it's been made right.

Harris Pizza sits in the KeyStone neighborhood of Rock Island, a part of the QC with real roots and real appetite. With 1,388 Google reviews and a 4.5 rating, this place has earned its reputation the old way — by being consistently good and showing up for the community year after year. It's exactly the kind of local mainstay that belongs on our guides, the place you'll hear about from three different people before you ever visit.

entities.name: Hauberg Estate
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Saturday: Closed | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
A graceful 1871 mansion revealing how the QC's river barons actually lived.

The Hauberg Estate stops you cold—twenty-two rooms of pressed tin ceilings, original woodwork, and the kind of furniture that belonged to people who knew what they owned. You move through parlors and bedrooms and a library that feels like stepping into a photograph from another century. The docents know the stories: which daughter married whom, why that particular chandelier matters, what the river trade meant to families like the Haubergs.

The Estate sits in Rock Island, on a street that still feels deliberate and settled, a short drive from the riverfront that made all this wealth possible in the first place. It's one of those places that anchors your understanding of the QC—not just what we are now, but what we built when the Mississippi mattered most. Once you've stood in those rooms, you see the area differently.

entities.name: Haus of Heir
entities.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Saturday: 8:00 AM – 3:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
A beauty studio where stylists actually listen and hair transformations feel personal.

Walk into Haus of Heir and you'll notice it immediately — the stylists know what they're doing, and they're not rushing through your appointment like you're a number. Whether you're after a careful cut, color work that doesn't look like you got it done at a chain, or something bolder, there's a reason this place has earned nearly 1,500 five-star reviews. The kind of place where you book with a specific stylist and come back to them, where consultations matter more than quick turnover.

Haus of Heir sits in Davenport, anchoring the growing reputation the QC has for real craftsmanship in beauty and wellness. It's the type of local business that shows up in conversation again and again — friends recommending friends, people willing to wait weeks for an opening because the work is worth it. Once you've found your stylist here, you've found them.

entities.name: Healthy Habits
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 4:00 PM
entities.description:
A carefully stocked health food shop where locals actually shop, not just browse.

Healthy Habits feels like it's run by people who actually care about what they're selling — the kind of place where staff can tell you the difference between their supplement brands and why they stock one over another. The shelves move past the usual suspects into territory that takes real curation: quality proteins, hard-to-find grains, skincare that doesn't read like a chemistry experiment. It's small enough that you'll recognize faces on repeat visits, the kind of neighborhood shop that builds a real customer base instead of relying on foot traffic.

Healthy Habits sits in Bettendorf, the QC's quieter, more residential side — a place where community-minded shops tend to thrive because locals actually make the trip. It's the kind of destination that shows up in conversations between people who take their health seriously, and once you've found it, you'll keep coming back.

entities.name: Helzberg Diamonds
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 6:00 PM
entities.description:
Diamond expertise and watch selection in a setting that takes the guesswork out of big moments.

Helzberg has the kind of staff who actually listen — they'll walk you through cuts and carats without the pressure, and they know their watches cold. The cases are well-lit and organized in a way that makes browsing feel manageable rather than overwhelming, whether you're hunting for an engagement ring, a milestone piece, or something that just caught your eye. They're open late on weekdays and Sundays too, which means you can slip in after work or squeeze in a visit without rearranging your week.

Helzberg sits in Northpark Mall in Davenport, anchoring the kind of shopping destination where you can tackle multiple errands without leaving the building. It's the rare jeweler in the QC with genuine depth in both diamonds and watches, and locals have trusted them with their most important purchases — which shows in those 226 solid reviews. If you're serious about getting it right the first time, this is where the QC comes.

entities.name: Hero Street Monument Committee
entities.hours: Monday: Open 24 hours | Tuesday: Open 24 hours | Wednesday: Open 24 hours | Thursday: Open 24 hours | Friday: Open 24 hours | Saturday: Open 24 hours | Sunday: Open 24 hours
entities.description:
A monument honoring Silvis's eight brothers who died in World War II.

Hero Street — named for the Sandoval family and their neighbors who gave their lives in the same war — is a quiet, dignified place to understand a piece of QC history that runs deeper than most realize. The monument sits open to the sky, accessible anytime, a reflection of how seriously this community takes remembrance. Standing there, you're in the presence of a story about sacrifice that shaped an entire neighborhood.

Hero Street Monument Committee occupies a small but significant corner of Silvis, just west of the main QC corridor. It's the kind of place that rewards curiosity — not a destination you'd stumble into by accident, but absolutely worth the short drive once you know it's there. This is local history that matters, tended by people who refuse to let these stories fade.

entities.name: Horizon Movers and Climate Controlled Storage
entities.hours: Monday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Tuesday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 8:00 AM – 12:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Local movers who actually show up on time and treat your stuff like their own.

Horizon has built a reputation in Davenport on the kind of reliability that doesn't make headlines — it just makes moving suck less. Their crew knows the quirks of QC neighborhoods (those narrow Bettendorf staircases, the old Victorian charm with tight doorways), and they pack and load with the focus of people who've done this hundreds of times. The climate-controlled storage means your boxes and furniture aren't sweating through humid summers or warping in winter, which matters more than it sounds when you're storing the good stuff.

Horizon Movers sits in Davenport and has earned a near-perfect rating because they've been doing right by families and businesses moving within and out of the Quad Cities for years. They're the kind of local service that belongs on our guides — not fancy, not flashy, just competent and honest. When you're planning a move in the QC, this is the first call to make.

entities.name: Hot Glass, Inc
entities.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 3:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 3:00 PM | Wednesday: Closed | Thursday: Closed | Friday: 10:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Saturday: Closed | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
A working glass studio where you can watch art happen in real time.

Hot Glass is the real deal — a functional artist studio downtown where glassblowers are actually at the furnace, not performing for tourists. The heat is immediate and disorienting when you step in; the work is loud and precise. You'll see molten glass being shaped into vessels, jewelry, and sculpture, and the artists here are generous about explaining what's happening, even when they're mid-blow.

Hot Glass sits in Downtown Davenport, steps from the riverfront and the kind of place that reminds you the QC has genuine creative infrastructure. It's not a gift shop or a gallery pretending to be a studio — it's where the work actually gets made. Once you've felt that heat and watched glass move like liquid, you'll understand why we keep coming back.

entities.name: Hotel Blackhawk, Autograph Collection
entities.description:
A 1920s landmark with a bowling alley bar and rooms that actually feel like home.

Hotel Blackhawk carries the weight of Davenport history — all art deco bones and restored grandeur — but it's the details that stick with you. The rooms are generous and thoughtfully done, the kind of place where you actually want to spend an evening. Head downstairs to the bowling alley bar and you'll understand why locals still treat this place like theirs: it's got the casual warmth of a neighborhood spot dressed up in velvet and good lighting.

Hotel Blackhawk sits at the heart of Downtown Davenport, steps from the Figge and walkable to the riverfront. It's become the go-to reason to spend a night in the QC instead of just passing through — the kind of destination that anchors a weekend and reminds you why the city's riverfront revival matters. Once you've had a drink in that bowling alley or woken up in one of those rooms, you'll understand why it keeps appearing in our guides.

entities.name: Huckleberry's Great Pizza and Calzones
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 4:00 – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 4:00 – 8:00 PM | Friday: 4:00 – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 4:00 – 9:00 PM | Sunday: 4:00 – 8:00 PM
entities.description: Cozy storefront hub with pies, spaghetti & other Italian classics plus beer & wine amid brick walls.

entities.name: Hyatt Place East Moline / Quad Cities
entities.description:
River views and a pool in a straightforward, well-run spot on the Illinois side.

The Hyatt Place sits right where East Moline meets the river—big windows frame the water, and the rooms feel uncluttered in that way that makes a night away feel uncomplicated. There's a restaurant downstairs, an indoor pool for restless kids or early swimmers, and the kind of staff that actually knows how to handle a Friday night when the QC is full. It's not trying to be anything fancy, which is exactly why it works.

The East Moline waterfront has grown quieter and more intentional in recent years, and this hotel anchors that stretch with the kind of reliability locals trust. It's close enough to Downtown and the District for an evening out, but far enough removed that you wake up somewhere calm. When you need a night or weekend away in your own backyard, this is where the QC turns to.

entities.name: I 74 Bridge
entities.description:
The river crossing that connects the QC and defines its skyline.

The I-74 Bridge is less a destination and more a moment—that stretch of highway where the Mississippi widens beneath you and the bluffs rise on either side. Drive it at sunset and the light hits the water in ways that make you understand why people settled here in the first place. It's the spine of the Quad Cities, the route that turns driving from one side to the other into something worth noticing.

The I-74 Bridge sits between Moline and Bettendorf, connecting the Illinois and Iowa sides of the region. It's the kind of landmark that most people pass through without thinking, but it's essential to understanding the Quad Cities—how the river shapes us, how we move, what we see every time we cross. It belongs here because it's part of the daily fabric of living here, and sometimes the most important places are the ones we drive over without stopping.

entities.name: I Love Fufu Restaurant - Davenport | African
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 4:00 – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 4:00 – 8:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM
entities.description:
West African cooking that's bringing something genuinely different to downtown.

I Love Fufu does the thing most restaurants won't commit to: it specializes. The fufu is hand-pounded, the jollof rice shows up with real depth, and the stews are built on spice and patience rather than shortcuts. Walk in on a Friday night and you're surrounded by people who know what they're here for — families, regulars, folks seeking flavors that don't show up anywhere else in the QC. It's a small operation with limited hours, which means they're not trying to be everything to everyone; they're trying to do one thing well.

I Love Fufu sits in downtown Davenport, a neighborhood that's quietly becoming a destination for the curious and hungry. It's the kind of specific, earnest restaurant that signals a neighborhood worth exploring — the one that makes you realize the QC's food scene reaches further than you thought. Once you've been, you'll find yourself coming back, and recommending it to anyone asking where to eat something that matters.

entities.name: Iconic Coffee + Energy
entities.hours: Monday: 6:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Tuesday: 6:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Wednesday: 6:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Thursday: 6:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Friday: 6:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Saturday: 6:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Sunday: 6:00 AM – 7:00 PM
entities.description:
Smoothie bowls and cold brew that fuels Bettendorf mornings.

Iconic's the kind of place where the line moves fast because everyone knows what they want — acai bowls stacked with granola and fresh fruit, or a cold brew that tastes like they actually know what they're doing with coffee. The space is bright and straightforward, built for people grabbing fuel before work or squeezing in a post-workout refuel. Open early and open late, which means you can get your energy fix whether you're an early riser or an afternoon slump victim.

Iconic sits in Bettendorf, the QC's growing east-side hub where things are newer and busier than downtown. It's become a regular stop for the neighborhood crowd — the fitness-minded, the commuters, the people who treat their morning routine seriously. If you're looking for a place that knows how to make a bowl and pulls consistent coffee, this one belongs on your rotation.

entities.name: Icons Martini
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 4:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Thursday: 4:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Friday: 4:00 PM – 1:00 AM | Saturday: 4:00 PM – 1:00 AM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Craft martinis and a vintage-cool room that feels like stepping into another era.

Icons leans into the glamour—dim lighting, art deco touches, and a bartender who actually knows the difference between a gin martini and a vodka one. The drink list is serious but not pretentious; there's a reason the regulars come back on Wednesday nights when most places are dead. It's the kind of bar where you can dress up without feeling out of place, or show up casual and still belong.

Icons sits in Rock Island, a neighborhood that's quietly becoming the QC's after-work destination for people who want real cocktails and conversation. It's close enough to downtown Davenport to feel connected to the river action, but far enough away to have its own identity. Once you've found it, you'll keep coming back—and you'll start recommending it to friends who actually know what they're looking for in a cocktail bar.

entities.name: Icy Bubbles
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM
entities.description:
Bubble tea and coffee drinks that taste like someone actually cares about flavor.

Icy Bubbles takes the bubble tea trend seriously — the kind of place where they're thinking about tea quality and boba texture instead of just chasing a trend. The menu pulls in both coffee and tea drinkers, so whether you're after a milk tea with tapioca or a proper espresso drink, there's real intention behind what lands in your cup. The 4.8-star rating isn't accidental; people keep coming back because these drinks taste considered.

Icy Bubbles sits in Davenport, a neighborhood that's quietly becoming known for places that do one thing well and do it thoughtfully. It's the kind of spot that rewards exploration — closed Tuesdays but open late on weekends, which makes it perfect for an afternoon detour or an evening treat. Once you've tried it, you'll understand why it keeps showing up in conversations about where to actually get a good drink in the QC.

entities.name: Illiniwek Forest Preserve
entities.hours: Monday: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Tuesday: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Friday: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM
entities.description:
280 acres of quiet woods and water just east of the river.

Illiniwek spreads across bottomland and upland forest in Hampton—the kind of preserve where you can actually lose the sound of traffic. The main draw is the lake loop, a gentle 1.5-mile walk that threads past cattail marshes and through oak groves, with benches positioned at the best views. Early mornings feel genuinely wild; late afternoons bring families fishing from the accessible pier and runners using the paths as their own private circuit.

Illiniwek Forest Preserve sits on the quieter east side, removed from downtown's energy but close enough for a quick escape. It's become a steady fixture in our guides because it does what few places do—offer real solitude and actual wildlife (herons, deer, the occasional fox sighting) without requiring a road trip. Whether you're looking for a meditative walk, a spot to bring kids who need to burn energy, or just an hour away from screens, the preserve delivers every time.

entities.name: Insureous Health Solutions
entities.hours: Mon–Fri 9am–5pm
entities.description: Founded by longtime Quad Cities residents John and Shelia Gerstner, Insureous helps individuals, families, and employers build smarter health, dental, vision, life, and supplemental insurance solutions, supported by a custom enrollment platform and year-round guidance.

entities.name: Iowa 80 Trucking Museum
entities.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 5:00 PM
entities.description: Free-admission museum of trucking history with antique models, vintage memorabilia & a gift shop.

entities.name: Iron + Grain Coffee House
entities.hours: Monday: 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Tuesday: 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Thursday: 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Friday: 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Saturday: 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Sunday: 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM
entities.description:
Davenport's dependable third place, open early and staying late.

Iron + Grain keeps Davenport mornings on schedule — the kind of spot where you know the routine before your first visit. The coffee is straightforward and honest, the pastries rotate, and there's enough table space that you're not elbowing strangers to open your laptop. It's built for lingering, whether you've got twenty minutes or an afternoon.

Iron + Grain anchors a stretch of Davenport that's quietly become worth the trip — close enough to downtown to feel connected, but with its own neighborhood rhythm. It's the sort of place that shows up in locals' weekly rotations, not because it's flashy, but because it simply works. Once you've found it, you'll be back.

entities.name: Iron Lion Disc Golf Supply
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 3:03 PM
entities.description:
The Quad Cities' serious disc golf shop, run by people who actually play.

Iron Lion stocks everything from beginner starter sets to high-end tournament discs, and the staff knows the difference — they play the local courses and can tell you which disc flies true on the Tartan Oaks layout. The shop is small and focused, walls lined with colorful plastic in every stability rating, and they'll spend time talking flight numbers with you instead of rushing the sale. If you're picking up your first disc or hunting for that one mid-range that's been discontinued, this is where the QC's disc golfers congregate.

Iron Lion sits in Moline, a short drive from some of the region's best-maintained courses. It's the kind of specialty shop that thrives because it serves a real community — not tourists, but the dedicated players who need reliable gear and knowledgeable advice. Once you've found your disc sport, you'll keep coming back here.

entities.name: Isle Casino Hotel Bettendorf - A Caesars Rewards Destination
entities.description: Modern casino resort offering free Wi-Fi & parking, plus 3 restaurants, an indoor pool & a marina.

entities.name: Jaded Java
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Friday: 9:30 AM – 11:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Sunday: 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM
entities.description:
Coffee shop and bistro where East Moline comes to linger.

Jaded Java pulls off the rare trick of being both a serious coffee stop and a place you actually want to stay for hours. The menu reaches beyond the usual—you're looking at sandwiches and small plates alongside espresso drinks, which means a morning coffee can turn into lunch without moving. The 4.8 rating across both platforms isn't inflated; people keep coming back because the coffee is solid and the food doesn't feel like an afterthought.

Jaded Java sits in East Moline, a neighborhood that's quietly developed its own character separate from downtown Davenport's buzz. It's the kind of local spot that thrives on repeat visits and word-of-mouth—the ratings prove it—and belongs on our guides because it's exactly what a good neighborhood coffee shop should be: a place where regulars have their usual order and newcomers immediately understand why.

entities.name: Java Java Cafe
entities.hours: Monday: 5:30 AM – 12:00 PM | Tuesday: 5:30 AM – 12:00 PM | Wednesday: 5:30 AM – 12:00 PM | Thursday: 5:30 AM – 12:00 PM | Friday: 5:30 AM – 12:00 PM | Saturday: 7:00 AM – 12:00 PM | Sunday: 8:00 AM – 12:00 PM
entities.description:
Early-morning institution where Davenport lines up before dawn.

Java Java opens at 5:30 on weekdays—earlier than most of the city's awake—and regulars have their orders memorized before the register rings. The crowd shifts from night-shift workers to gym-goers to office people grabbing fuel, each wave knowing exactly what they want and why they're there. It's the kind of place where the staff recognizes you by your usual, not your name.

Java Java sits in Davenport proper, far enough from the riverfront to feel like a real neighborhood spot rather than a tourist pass-through. The consistently high ratings across platforms and those punishing early hours suggest something genuinely useful is happening here—the kind of reliable, unglamorous coffee shop that holds a community together before most places are even thinking about opening their doors.

entities.name: Jennie's Boxcar
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM
entities.description:
A dive bar institution where East Moline gathers over cheap drinks and real conversation.

Jennie's Boxcar has the kind of worn-in charm that can't be manufactured — sticky floors, neon beer signs, and a jukebox that's been playing the same songs for years. The bartenders know regulars by name and pour heavy; the crowd skews local and unpretentious, the kind of place where a Thursday night feels like Friday. It's not fancy, but it's exactly what a neighborhood bar should be.

Jennie's Boxcar sits in East Moline, where the real QC still lives — away from the polished downtown stretch and closer to where people actually spend their evenings. It's the kind of honest, unglamorous spot that reveals what a place is really about, and it's earned its loyal following for good reason. Come for a drink, stay because you've found your people.

entities.name: Jerry’s
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM
entities.description:
Le Claire's reliable dive where locals actually want to be.

Jerry's has the kind of worn-in comfort that comes from doing one thing well for a long time—cold beer, straightforward food, and a room full of people who've been coming back for years. The bar runs long, the jukebox knows what it's doing, and there's no pretense, just the easy hum of a place that doesn't need to try too hard because it's already exactly what people need it to be.

Jerry's sits in Le Claire, the quieter riverfront town that's become essential to understanding the full Quad Cities picture—close enough to Downtown and The District for a natural evening loop, but with its own rhythm entirely. It's the kind of neighborhood anchor that rarely makes it onto curated lists, but once you find it, you wonder how you missed it before.

entities.name: Jiang's Hot Pot BBQ
entities.hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM | Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM | Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM | Thursday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM | Friday: 11:30 AM – 10:30 PM | Saturday: 11:30 AM – 10:30 PM | Sunday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM
entities.description:
Tabletop hot pot where you cook, broth simmers, and the night stretches on.

Jiang's does the whole thing right — you get a burner, a bubbling broth of your choosing, and a parade of raw proteins and vegetables to cook at your own pace. It's the kind of meal that demands conversation; there's natural downtime built into every bite, which means long tables fill with groups actually talking to each other instead of scrolling. The broth stays hot, the dipping sauces are balanced, and the rhythm of cooking together turns dinner into an event.

Jiang's sits in Davenport proper, in a part of town that's quietly become a destination for adventurous eating. It's the rare restaurant in the QC that does interactive cooking well — not gimmicky, just genuine — and it's built a loyal following that keeps those tables full on weeknights. If you've been wanting to understand why hot pot has staying power as a dining experience, this is where to find out.

entities.name: John Deere Harvester Works
entities.hours: Monday: 7:30 AM – 3:00 PM | Tuesday: 7:30 AM – 3:00 PM | Wednesday: 7:30 AM – 3:00 PM | Thursday: 7:30 AM – 3:00 PM | Friday: 7:30 AM – 3:00 PM | Saturday: Closed | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Cafeteria cooking with real history, open weekday mornings only.

John Deere Harvester Works is a working factory cafeteria that opens its doors to the public — a rare thing that feels like stepping into another era. The menu is straightforward and honest: hot breakfast, lunch sandwiches, and sides that taste like someone's grandmother designed them. The crowd is a mix of long-time plant employees and locals who've learned the secret: show up before 11 AM on a weekday, grab a tray, and eat where the machinery still runs in the building next door.

East Moline's industrial heart still beats, and John Deere Harvester Works is one of the few places where you can actually feel that pulse. It's a genuinely local experience — not staged, not polished — that reminds you the Quad Cities built something real here. If you want to understand what this region is actually about, beyond the riverfront attractions, this cafeteria tells you more than most restaurants ever could.

entities.name: John Deere Pavilion
entities.hours: Monday: 1:00 – 5:00 PM | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Green machines and lever-pulling wonder for anyone who's ever loved heavy equipment.

The John Deere Pavilion is exactly what it promises — a shrine to tractors and the engineering that built the Midwest. You'll find yourself in front of gleaming vintage and current models, many of them interactive: pull a lever, watch hydraulics move, understand why farmers care about this stuff. The hands-on exhibits beat passively reading about agricultural history, and the educational films add texture to what you're seeing on the floor.

Tucked in Moline, the Pavilion lives at the heart of John Deere country — this is where the company's roots run deepest, and the museum reflects that pride without apology. It's the kind of place that works for kids burning off energy, for adults who grew up around equipment, and for anyone curious about the machines that shaped the region. Once you understand the connection between John Deere and the Quad Cities' identity, a visit here stops being a tourist checkbox and starts making sense.

entities.name: Johnny's Italian Steakhouse
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM
entities.description:
Steaks and martinis in a room that feels built for special occasions.

Johnny's has that supper club energy — dark wood, a bar that actually invites lingering, and the kind of service that remembers you ordered your martini up. The steaks arrive properly seared, the Italian dishes don't feel like an afterthought, and there's something about the lighting (low, amber, deliberate) that makes an ordinary Friday night feel like it matters. It's not trying to be trendy; it's been doing this for long enough that it doesn't have to.

Johnny's anchors Moline's upscale dining scene, the kind of place you book when you want to dress up and disappear into a booth for a few hours. It carries real weight in the QC's nightlife — the kind of establishment that shows up in anniversary plans and business dinners alike. Once you've sat at that bar or claimed one of the back tables, you'll understand why it's been pulling a crowd for this long.

entities.name: Junior's Sports Bar
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM | Sunday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM
entities.description:
A straightforward sports bar where the wings are solid and everybody knows your name.

Junior's keeps it simple — good food, cold beer, and every game on a screen somewhere. Walk in on a Friday night and you'll find the same faces who've been coming for years, the kind of regulars who have their usual order memorized by the kitchen. It's the place you take a visiting friend when you want to watch the Hawkeyes or Bears without pretension.

Junior's sits on the East Moline side, away from the downtown traffic but easy to find once you know it's there. It's one of those places that doesn't need buzz or a redesign to matter — it works because it's been doing the same thing well for long enough that it's woven into the neighborhood fabric. That kind of reliability shows up again and again in the QC spots we keep coming back to.

entities.name: K'nees Florists & Gifts
entities.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 12:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
A Moline florist where arrangements feel personal, not mass-produced.

K'nees has been doing this long enough to know the difference between a bouquet and a gesture. Walk in and you'll find serious flowers — the kind with texture and thought — arranged by people who actually talk to you about what you're celebrating (or apologizing for, or grieving). They handle the expected occasions, sure, but they're equally comfortable building something custom from whatever's fresh that week. Saturday hours are tight, so weekday mornings are your best bet for unhurried attention.

K'nees sits in Uptown Moline, a neighborhood that rewards a slower pace and a willingness to step off the main drag. It's the kind of local business that accumulates trust over time — nearly 300 Google reviews speak to that — and once you've ordered from them once, you'll keep coming back. Real florists matter in a community; this one does.

entities.name: Kick Serve Coffee
entities.hours: Monday: 6:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Tuesday: 6:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Wednesday: 6:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Thursday: 6:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Friday: 6:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Saturday: 6:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Sunday: 7:00 AM – 6:00 PM
entities.description:
Moline's tennis-themed coffee spot with serious espresso chops.

Kick Serve pulls double duty as both a working café and a love letter to tennis — the walls hint at the sport without overwhelming the coffee. The espresso is the real draw: properly extracted, balanced, the kind that regulars come back for day after day. It's open early (6 a.m. on weekdays) and stays late enough to catch the afternoon crowd, with a vibe that feels purposeful rather than rushed.

Kick Serve sits in Moline, the QC's often-overlooked west side, where good coffee shops are worth seeking out. It's become the kind of reliable third place that locals depend on — near enough to the river to work into a longer walk, distinctive enough that it sticks with you. Once you've found it, you'll understand why the ratings stay high: this is a café that takes its craft seriously.

entities.name: Kindred Styles Antiques
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Sunday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM
entities.description:
Curated vintage finds in Rock Island's quietest corner shop.

Kindred Styles has the feel of a friend's carefully edited collection — not the chaotic overflow of a typical antique mall, but intentional pieces that suggest someone actually knows what they're looking for. The inventory shifts with the seasons, which means regulars keep coming back to see what's landed. You'll find mid-century furniture alongside smaller treasures: glassware, textiles, the kind of objects that work equally well as practical buys or conversation starters.

Kindred Styles sits in Rock Island, one of the QC's quieter neighborhoods but increasingly worth the drive for places like this. It's the kind of shop that rewards browsing on a Saturday morning before heading to nearby spots or picking up lunch — the sort of discovery that makes you realize there's more to explore on this side of the river. Once you've found it, you'll understand why it keeps showing up on our radar.

entities.name: Kitchens
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 6:00 PM
entities.description:
A cooking shop where locals actually gather to learn and eat.

Kitchens operates as part retail, part classroom, part gathering spot — the kind of place where you can pick up a good knife on Tuesday and sign up for a pasta-making class on Friday. The space has that lived-in feel of a shop run by people who care about the craft, with shelves stocked thoughtfully rather than exhaustively. There's real food happening here, not just merchandise; regulars drift in knowing they'll find both what they need and people worth talking to.

Kitchens sits in Davenport, anchoring a neighborhood that rewards curiosity. It's the type of local business that thrives because it refuses to be just one thing — a shop, a school, a lunch spot — and that multiplicity is exactly what makes the QC interesting. Once you've been, you'll find reasons to come back.

entities.name: La Rancherita 2
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Sunday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM
entities.description:
Moline institution serving authentic Mexican food that keeps people coming back for decades.

La Rancherita 2 is the kind of place where the kitchen knows what it's doing—the kind where regulars order without looking at the menu and newcomers immediately understand the loyalty. The dining room has that lived-in warmth of a neighborhood spot that's earned its 900-plus reviews through consistency rather than Instagram moments. Order the chile rellenos or the carne asada, sit in a booth that's seen better days but somehow feels right, and you'll understand why this corner of Moline keeps drawing people back.

Tucked into the Wheelock/Velie area of Moline, La Rancherita 2 represents the kind of authentic neighborhood cooking that makes the QC's food landscape genuine. It's the sort of place that shows up on our guides not because it's trendy, but because it's real—a restaurant that's been doing one thing well for years, open seven days a week and ready to feed you. Once you've found it, you'll drive past the chain places and head straight here.

entities.name: La' James International College
entities.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 9:30 AM – 7:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Affordable haircuts and beauty services from students learning their craft.

La' James is where Davenport goes when budget matters but quality can't slip — a working beauty school where you're part of someone's real education. The energy is different than a typical salon: there's focus, supervision, and the kind of care that comes from students who are still proving themselves. Haircuts run half the price of the chain places, and if you're patient with the learning curve, you walk out looking sharp.

La' James sits on Davenport's east side, part of a vocational pipeline that's been training QC cosmetologists and estheticians for years. It's the kind of place that matters quietly in a community — honest work, honest prices, and a real reason to show up: you're banking on skill and getting a solid deal in return.

entities.name: Lagomarcino's Confectionery
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 5:30 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 5:30 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 5:30 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 5:30 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 5:30 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 5:30 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
A century-old candy counter where the sweets are handmade and the cases gleam.

Lagomarcino's has been doing this since 1919—hand-dipped chocolates, fresh taffy, old-fashioned hard candies made the way they used to be. Walk in and the cases stop you: rows of caramels in paper cups, chocolate-covered cherries, butter creams in flavors you didn't know you needed. The staff moves with the ease of people who've explained the difference between their fudge and everyone else's a thousand times, and they're patient about it.

Lagomarcino's sits in East Davenport, a quieter stretch of the city that rewards wandering. It's the kind of place that's been here longer than most of us have been alive—the sort of local landmark that shows up on three generations of birthday lists. Once you know it exists, you'll find reasons to go back.

entities.name: Las Salsas Dona Mary
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Authentic Moline spot where the salsas are made fresh and the wait is worth it.

Las Salsas Dona Mary does one thing exceptionally well: salsas that taste like someone's grandmother is in the kitchen (because she probably is). The dining room is modest and no-frills, but the food speaks louder than the décor — homemade enchiladas, chile rellenos that actually have weight to them, and those salsas that keep people coming back. Regulars know to arrive before the dinner rush or settle in for a wait; this place packs out for good reason.

Tucked into Moline's Overlook neighborhood, Las Salsas Dona Mary is the kind of honest, unpretentious restaurant that defines real community dining in the QC. It's not trying to be trendy or Instagram-worthy; it's just good, straightforward Mexican food made with care. Places like this are exactly what we're looking for — where the ratings are genuinely high because the food is genuinely good, and word-of-mouth has done all the marketing.

entities.name: Lavender Crest Winery
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 5:00 PM
entities.description:
Farm winery in Colona pouring wines you'll actually want to drink.

Lavender Crest sits on working land outside Colona, and you can feel the difference — the tasting room has the unhurried ease of a place that grows what it serves. The wines lean dry and balanced, made to pair with food rather than impress with fruit bombs. Come on a Saturday afternoon and you'll find locals at the bar nursing glasses, the kind of crowd that knows the owners by name and has opinions about the current vintage.

Lavender Crest is a short drive west from the river, in the quieter territory between Moline and the countryside — the kind of escape that reminds you the QC has more character beyond the downtown corridor. It's worth the trip on its own, or a natural pairing if you're already exploring the rural edge of Rock Island County. Once you've found it, you'll keep coming back.

entities.name: Le Mekong Vietnamese Restaurant
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 4:30 – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 4:30 – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 4:30 – 9:00 PM | Friday: 4:30 – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 4:30 – 9:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description: Long-running, unassuming option for Vietnamese comfort food like pho & curry dishes.

entities.name: LeClaire Park & Bandshell
entities.hours: Monday: 6:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 6:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 6:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 6:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 6:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 6:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Sunday: 6:00 AM – 9:00 PM
entities.description:
Riverfront green space where Davenport gathers to breathe and listen.

LeClaire Park is where the Mississippi actually feels close—the water trail winds past the bandshell, and on summer evenings you'll find families sprawled on the grass while live music drifts across the open air. The fountain monument anchors the space with some real character, and the park stays open early to late, which means sunrise walks before work and sunset picnics are both built into the rhythm here. It's the kind of uncluttered green that works whether you're bringing kids, a book, or just need twenty minutes away from downtown streets.

LeClaire Park sits in Downtown Davenport, steps from the river and the revitalized corridor that's become the QC's gathering point. It's reliable in a way that matters—a place that shows up for the community through every season, hosting concerts and festivals but staying quiet enough for the people who just need to sit and think. Once you know it's there, you'll find reasons to return.

entities.name: LemonGrass Cafe
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM, 4:00 – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM, 4:00 – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM, 4:00 – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM, 4:00 – 9:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM, 4:00 – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 PM, 4:00 – 9:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Thai food with real skill, served in a room that actually feels good.

LemonGrass doesn't try too hard — exposed brick, metal fixtures, the kind of industrial warmth that lets the food speak. The pad thai has heat and balance, the curries taste like someone knows what they're doing, and the cocktails aren't an afterthought. Hit it for lunch and it's quiet; come dinner and the room fills with people who've made this their spot.

LemonGrass sits in Moline, a neighborhood that's quietly become one of the QC's better places to eat. It's the kind of restaurant that pulls you back — not because it's trendy or trying to be something it's not, but because the execution is solid and the vibe is right. Once you've been, you'll understand why it keeps showing up on locals' regular rotation.

entities.name: Leon 'Bix' Beiderbecke Memorial
entities.hours: Monday: Open 24 hours | Tuesday: Open 24 hours | Wednesday: Open 24 hours | Thursday: Open 24 hours | Friday: Open 24 hours | Saturday: Open 24 hours | Sunday: Open 24 hours
entities.description:
A quiet riverfront tribute to the jazz cornet player who changed everything.

The Leon 'Bix' Beiderbecke Memorial sits along the Mississippi, a modest stone marker and plaque that honors one of the Quad Cities' most unlikely legends — a kid from Davenport who became a jazz innovator before his death at 28. It's the kind of place you can visit anytime, day or night, without crowds or ceremony. Standing there, you're aware you're touching something real: the birthplace of someone whose restless talent echoed through American music.

The memorial anchors Downtown Davenport's riverfront, a short walk from the main drag and easy to pair with a longer evening along the water. If you care about the QC's history — the stories that explain why this place matters — Bix belongs on your list. It's a quiet moment in a town with big cultural roots, the kind of stop that locals know about and visitors rarely stumble on by accident.

entities.name: Lifted Energy
entities.hours: Monday: 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Tuesday: 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Thursday: 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Friday: 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Sunday: 9:00 AM – 2:00 PM
entities.description:
Bright breakfast spot in Moline fueling the early morning crowd.

Lifted Energy opens early on weekdays — 7 a.m. sharp — and the regulars know it. The vibe is straightforward and unpretentious: people stop in before work for coffee and something quick, grab a seat if they're staying, or take their order to go. It's the kind of place that feels like it knows what it's doing, with a solid 4.7 rating built on consistency rather than flash.

Lifted Energy sits in Greater Tartan Oaks, a residential pocket of Moline that's easy to overlook if you're sticking to the riverfront corridor. But that's exactly why it matters — it's the neighborhood spot, the place locals actually go. Worth the short drive if you're looking for genuine community character and a morning routine that doesn't involve crossing the whole metro.

entities.name: Lopiez
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM
entities.description:
Brick-oven pizza that tastes like someone actually knows what they're doing.

Lopiez doesn't overthink it — the crust has char and air in equal measure, the toppings aren't fighting each other, and the room has the kind of energy that makes you want to linger over a second slice. It's the place downtown regulars circle back to on Friday nights, where the wood smoke drifts out onto the street and pulls people in. The bar's solid too, which means you're not choosing between good pizza and a good drink.

Lopiez sits in Downtown Davenport, a neighborhood that's quietly built itself into somewhere worth spending an evening. It's the kind of restaurant that anchors a neighborhood — consistent, unpretentious, and good enough that it shows up on our guides year after year. Once you've been, you'll understand why locals don't need to drive across the river to eat well.

entities.name: Los Agaves Mexican Grill
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM
entities.description:
Straightforward Mexican food, cold margaritas, and a patio that catches the evening.

Los Agaves does the basics well — enchiladas that come properly sauced, tacos with meat that tastes like it was actually seasoned, sopas that fill you up. The bar runs a strong margarita, and on weekends the patio fills with groups who've claimed the same corner table three times over. It's the kind of place where the staff knows what you're ordering before you ask.

Los Agaves sits in Moline, anchoring the casual restaurant landscape where families and coworkers slip in on weekday lunches and groups gather on Friday nights. It shows up again and again in our guides because it's the reliable spot — not trying too hard, not cutting corners — that actually improves a neighborhood by existing there.

entities.name: Lost Oasis
entities.description:
A wild animal sanctuary where rescued creatures roam actual habitat.

Lost Oasis isn't a typical zoo—it's 84 acres where animals rescued from neglect and exploitation live in environments built to match what they'd see in nature. You'll walk through prairie, woodland, and wetland zones tracking wolves, big cats, and birds; the layout forces you to move at their pace, not the other way around. There's something quieting about watching a rescued tiger just *be* in space designed for it, rather than pace behind glass.

Lost Oasis sits in Princeton, a short drive northwest of the main QC corridor—worth the trip if you're looking to venture beyond the immediate river towns. It's the kind of destination that appeals to families wanting something more thoughtful than standard attractions, and to anyone who's ever wondered what happens to animals seized from bad situations. Once you've spent an afternoon there, you understand why it shows up on our list again and again.

entities.name: Loud Thunder Forest Preserve
entities.hours: Monday: 8:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Tuesday: 8:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Wednesday: 8:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Thursday: 8:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Friday: 8:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Saturday: 8:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Sunday: 8:00 AM – 7:00 PM
entities.description:
680 acres of river bluffs, trails, and actual quiet on the Illinois side.

Loud Thunder lives up to its name only in storms — most days it's a genuine escape, with miles of hiking trails that wind through oak and hickory, past overlooks where you can actually see the Mississippi working. The preserve feels less manicured than the county parks closer to downtown; there's room to breathe here, room to disappear for an afternoon. Bring binoculars if you bird-watch. Bring a dog if you have one. The trails are solid and well-marked, and you won't fight crowds even on nice weekends.

Loud Thunder sits just across the river in Illinois City — close enough for a quick drive from Bettendorf or the Quad Cities proper, far enough to feel like you've actually left. It's the kind of place that anchors a morning or afternoon in the region's natural spaces, and it shows up in our guides because it delivers on what it promises: real forest, real river views, and the kind of quiet that's increasingly hard to find. Once you know it's there, you'll keep coming back.

entities.name: Lunardi's Restaurant | Italian
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 4:00 – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 4:00 – 9:00 PM | Friday: 4:00 – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 4:00 – 9:00 PM | Sunday: 4:00 – 8:00 PM
entities.description:
Chicago-style pizza and red-sauce classics from a Davenport institution since 1989.

Lunardi's feels like the kind of place where regulars have their own booth and the kitchen knows your order by heart. The Chicago pizza arrives with that signature thin, crispy crust and a char on the edges — less about theatrics, more about doing one thing well for thirty-some years. The pasta is straightforward and honest: marinara that tastes like it's been simmering since lunch, meatballs that hold together, garlic bread that's golden and garlicky without apology.

Tucked into Davenport's quieter corners, Lunardi's sits outside the main restaurant clusters but rewards anyone willing to find it — exactly the kind of neighborhood spot that survives by being genuinely good rather than fashionable. Open Wednesday through Sunday (closed Mondays and Tuesdays), it's built for weeknight family dinners and the kind of unhurried meals where nobody's rushing you out. Once you've been, it's the place you keep coming back to.

entities.name: Lynch Heating & Plumbing
entities.hours: Monday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Tuesday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: Closed | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
A Davenport fixture for heating, plumbing, and the advice that actually saves you money.

Lynch has been the place Quad Citizens call when the furnace dies in January or the kitchen sink backs up—and they call back because the staff knows what they're talking about. Walk in and you're not getting upsold on solutions you don't need; you're getting straight talk from people who've been doing this long enough to see every problem twice. The showroom stocks the reliable stuff: water heaters, thermostats, fixtures—nothing flashy, everything dependable.

Lynch Heating & Plumbing sits in Davenport proper, serving the neighborhood with the kind of no-nonsense competence that builds a 312-review reputation. It's a place that belongs in our guides not because it's trendy but because it's genuinely useful—the sort of local business that makes living in the Quad Cities easier and your home more comfortable. Once you've trusted them with your first repair, you'll understand why they keep showing up on the recommendations.

entities.name: Mac's Tavern
entities.hours: Monday: 4:00 PM – 2:00 AM | Tuesday: 4:00 PM – 2:00 AM | Wednesday: 4:00 PM – 2:00 AM | Thursday: 4:00 PM – 2:00 AM | Friday: 4:00 PM – 2:00 AM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Sunday: 3:00 PM – 2:00 AM
entities.description:
A 1934 institution where the bar is worn smooth and the Irish roots run deep.

Mac's has that lived-in feeling that can't be faked — dark wood, low ceilings, and a bar long enough that you'll always find a stool. The crowd shifts with the hours: lunch regulars nursing Guinness, Friday night groups spilling onto the patio, late-night conversations that wind toward closing. The kitchen keeps it simple and honest — pub food that tastes better after a beer, the kind of place where people show up the same time every week and the bartender already knows the order.

Mac's sits right in Downtown Davenport, a cornerstone of the neighborhood's character and proof that some places don't need to reinvent themselves. Nearly 90 years in, it still feels like the center of gravity for a certain kind of Quad Cities night — the kind where you lose track of time and find yourself talking to strangers who become friends. It's exactly the kind of place that belongs in our guides.

entities.name: Machine Shed Restaurant

entities.description: Easygoing farm-themed operation offering traditional American dishes & a full bar.

entities.name: Main Street Coffee
entities.hours: Monday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Tuesday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Thursday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Friday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Saturday: 8:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Sunday: 8:00 AM – 2:00 PM
entities.description:
Davenport's morning anchor—coffee, pastries, and genuine regulars.

Main Street Coffee opens early and closes by 2 p.m., which means it's built for the morning crowd: the before-work rush, the retired couples, the people who've claimed their favorite seat for years. The 4.9 rating isn't casual—it's the kind of score you earn by getting the fundamentals right every single day. Weekday mornings are tight and focused; weekends are when you can actually linger over a second cup without feeling the push behind you.

Main Street Coffee sits in Downtown Davenport, anchoring the morning rhythm of one of the QC's most walkable neighborhoods. It's the kind of small, consistent place that locals trust—no flash, no trends, just good coffee and a room full of people who know exactly why they're there. Once you find your spot here, you'll keep coming back.

entities.name: Major Art & Hobby Center
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
A cramped, treasure-filled corner for model builders, artists, and hobbyists.

Walk in and you're surrounded—shelves of paint tubes and brushes, model airplane kits stacked with train sets, bins of beads and craft supplies that seem to multiply toward the back. The owner knows what they're stocking, and regulars return for the specific stuff that bigger retailers don't bother carrying. It's the kind of place where a serious hobbyist can lose an hour just browsing, and a parent can actually find materials for their kid's project instead of settling for what's left at a big-box store.

Major Art & Hobby Center sits on a downtown Davenport block that still holds onto the old retail character of the city—the kind of street where independent shops do real work. It's close enough to the riverfront to pair with a walk or a meal, but it's the sort of destination people visit specifically because they know what they're after. Once you've found it, you'll keep coming back.

entities.name: Makenna Koffee Company
entities.hours: Monday: 6:30 AM – 8:00 PM | Tuesday: 6:30 AM – 8:00 PM | Wednesday: 6:30 AM – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 6:30 AM – 8:00 PM | Friday: 6:30 AM – 8:00 PM | Saturday: 6:30 AM – 8:00 PM | Sunday: 6:30 AM – 8:00 PM
entities.description:
Specialty coffee and all-day breakfast in a room built for lingering.

Makenna's the kind of place where the barista knows what you ordered last week, and the breakfast menu—eggs, pastries, sandwiches—gives you real reasons to stay past your first cup. The space feels unhurried, with enough natural light and comfortable corners that you'll find people working on laptops at 10 AM and still there at noon, just refueling. The coffee itself is treated seriously: they're pulling shots with care and steaming milk like it matters.

Makenna Koffee Company sits in Davenport, where it's become a reliable anchor for both the early-morning crowd and the mid-morning crowd that trickles in after errands. It's the kind of neighborhood spot—open seven days, long hours, consistently good—that deserves a place in your regular rotation, and once you find it, you'll understand why the ratings reflect what locals already know.

entities.name: Malibu Jack's Quad Cities
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 8:00 PM
entities.description:
Indoor mini golf and arcade games in a neon-lit Bettendorf barn.

Malibu Jack's is the kind of place that works just as well for a rainy Tuesday afternoon as it does for a birthday party on Saturday night. The mini golf courses wind through themed rooms with enough personality to keep you grinning between putts — and the arcade section hums with the particular energy of skee-ball, racing games, and those claw machines that somehow always feel winnable. It's bright, it's loud in the best way, and there's enough here to kill a few hours without feeling like you're just killing time.

Malibu Jack's sits in Bettendorf, the QC's reliably solid eastern anchor, and it's the kind of indoor attraction that actually earns its spot on a rainy weekend or a school day off. It fills a real gap in the area — a place where kids and adults alike can burn some energy and have a genuine good time together. Once you've spent an afternoon here, you'll remember it the next time you're looking for something to do.

entities.name: Mantra Indian Cuisine & Spirits
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 2:30 PM, 5:00 – 9:30 PM | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 2:30 PM, 5:00 – 9:30 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 2:30 PM, 5:00 – 9:30 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 2:30 PM, 5:00 – 9:30 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 2:30 PM, 5:00 – 9:30 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 2:30 PM, 5:00 – 9:30 PM
entities.description:
Serious curries and a lunch buffet that keeps downtown workers coming back.

Mantra doesn't dress itself up—it's a straightforward room where the food does the talking. The lunch buffet hits hard on weekdays, a reliable rotation of curries, breads, and rice that changes enough to keep regulars interested. Evenings shift toward the menu: order the saag paneer if you want comfort, or push into the spiced meat curries where the kitchen shows its range. The cocktails are better than you'd expect, especially if you're pairing them with something rich.

Mantra sits in Downtown Davenport, steps from the riverfront and the cluster of places worth your time. It's been a quiet fixture here for years—the kind of restaurant that builds its reputation on consistency and word-of-mouth rather than Instagram moments. Once you've found it, you'll notice it's always got a mix of regulars, families, and people stopping in between river walks.

entities.name: Massage Envy
entities.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Sunday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM
entities.description:
Professional massage therapy with extended hours and a steady stream of regulars.

Massage Envy on Elmore Avenue keeps the door open late — until 9 p.m. most weeknights — which means you can book a deep tissue or Swedish massage after work without rearranging your whole week. The therapists here know what they're doing; the high Google ratings reflect a consistent experience, and people come back. It's the kind of place where the front desk remembers your preferences and the schedule fills up weeks ahead.

Massage Envy sits on Davenport's east side, convenient to the neighborhoods that need it most. It's practical wellness — not boutique, not precious, just reliable care that fits into real life. We include it because the QC deserves good recommendations for the unglamorous necessities, and this one delivers.

entities.name: McManus Park
entities.hours: Monday: 12:00 AM – 10:30 PM | Tuesday: 12:00 AM – 10:30 PM | Wednesday: 12:00 AM – 10:30 PM | Thursday: 12:00 AM – 10:30 PM | Friday: 12:00 AM – 10:30 PM | Saturday: 12:00 AM – 10:30 PM | Sunday: 12:00 AM – 10:30 PM
entities.description:
Bettendorf's green anchor—ball fields, trails, and room to breathe.

McManus Park sprawls across Bettendorf like the town's living room, with baseball diamonds that host everything from little league to summer tournaments, and trails that wind through actual quiet. Families stake out picnic tables on weekends; kids disappear into the open grass while parents watch from the bleachers. It's the kind of place where you can show up without a plan and find exactly what you came for.

McManus Park anchors Bettendorf's park system and shows up constantly in conversations with locals about where to spend a Saturday afternoon. It's the sort of destination that works whether you're raising kids in the area, visiting family, or just looking for a green space that feels genuinely lived-in. Once you know it's there, you'll find yourself back.

entities.name: Me & Billy
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Seasonal cocktails and craft beer in downtown's most convivial modern room.

Me & Billy has the kind of bar energy that makes you want to stay longer than you planned — the kind where bartenders remember what you ordered last time and the crowd leans toward people actually talking to each other. The cocktails shift with the seasons (which means coming back to see what's new), and the elevated bar fare is the kind you don't feel guilty ordering at 9 p.m. on a Thursday. It's modern without feeling cold, busy without feeling rushed.

Me & Billy sits in Downtown Davenport, anchoring one of the QC's most vital neighborhoods — minutes from the riverfront and surrounded by galleries, restaurants, and other reasons to wander. With nearly 1,500 reviews and a 4.6 rating, it's earned its place as a reliable favorite, the kind of spot that shows up on people's "where should we go tonight?" rotation again and again.

entities.name: Meli's Pancake House
entities.hours: Monday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Tuesday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Thursday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Friday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Saturday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Sunday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM
entities.description:
Fluffy pancakes and strong coffee in a Moline breakfast institution.

Meli's opens early and stays slammed until close — the kind of place where regulars have their own mugs hanging behind the counter and the waitstaff knows your order before you sit down. The pancakes arrive golden and substantial, the kind that actually taste like something, and the coffee cups never empty. It's unpretentious, a bit worn in the best way, and the noise level tells you everything you need to know about whether the food is worth eating.

Meli's sits in Moline, a neighborhood that rewards the kind of local knowledge our guides exist to share. It's been doing the same thing well for long enough that it's become a fixture — the place you take visiting family for a proper breakfast, the spot that appears in conversation when people talk about where they actually eat. Once you've been, you'll understand why it keeps showing up.

entities.name: Middle Park
entities.hours: Monday: 8:00 AM – 10:30 PM | Tuesday: 8:00 AM – 10:30 PM | Wednesday: 8:00 AM – 10:30 PM | Thursday: 8:00 AM – 10:30 PM | Friday: 8:00 AM – 10:30 PM | Saturday: 8:00 AM – 10:30 PM | Sunday: 8:00 AM – 10:30 PM
entities.description:
Bettendorf's centerpiece park—open dawn to late evening, year-round.

Middle Park is the kind of sprawling green space that anchors a town, with room to breathe and actual things to do. Whether you're pushing a stroller along the paths, letting kids loose on the playground, or finding a quiet bench to watch the seasons change, the park feels genuinely used—not manicured into sterility. The extended hours (open until 10:30 p.m. most nights) mean you can catch it at golden hour or even swing by for an evening walk without rushing.

Middle Park sits in Bettendorf proper, just minutes from the river and the wider QC landscape. It's the kind of reliable anchor that locals return to again and again—a place that deserves a spot in our guides not for being showy, but for being genuinely good at what it does: giving the community a place to gather, move, and simply exist outdoors.

entities.name: Midwest Ale Works
entities.hours: Monday: 2:00 – 10:00 PM | Tuesday: 2:00 – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 2:00 – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 2:00 – 10:00 PM | Friday: 12:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Saturday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 9:00 PM
entities.description:
A brewery and kitchen where the beer and food both deserve your attention.

Midwest Ale Works does the hard thing—it takes brewing seriously and cooking seriously, without one overshadowing the other. The kitchen turns out honest pub fare that tastes like someone cares (the kind of place where a burger or sandwich lands with real character), and the beer list reflects actual thought: house-made IPAs and lagers that pair as well with the food as they do with conversation. Friday and Saturday nights pull a genuine crowd, but weekday afternoons are calm enough to sit with a pour-over and a notebook if that's what you need.

Midwest Ale Works sits in East Moline, a neighborhood that's becoming quietly essential to the QC's food and drink scene. It's the kind of spot that shows up on repeat visits—where locals bring out-of-town guests not to impress them with novelty, but because the beer is good, the food is real, and the room feels like a place built for staying awhile. Once you've been, it stays in rotation.

entities.name: Milltown Coffee
entities.hours: Monday: 6:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Tuesday: 6:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 6:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 6:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 6:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 6:00 AM – 3:00 PM | Sunday: 7:00 AM – 3:00 PM
entities.description:
Moline's reliable third place, serious about coffee and built for staying.

Milltown pulls consistent shots and pours thoughtful drinks in a space that feels purposefully calm—the kind of room where regulars have their usual orders memorized and newcomers immediately understand why. The early hours (6 a.m. on weekdays) make it a natural stop for the pre-work crowd, but the real draw is the unhurried atmosphere; this is where Moline comes to linger, not rush. Whether it's the coffee itself or just the weight of 699 good reviews, something here works.

Milltown Coffee sits in Moline, a neighborhood that's quietly becoming the Quad Cities' most thoughtful coffee destination. It's the kind of reliable, well-executed spot that doesn't need fanfare to fill its tables—just consistent quality and a room built for real hours, not just transactions. Once you've found it, you'll keep coming back.
