entities.description: Relaxed space with exposed-brick walls & a menu featuring Thai & Vietnamese recipes.

entities.name: Mississippi Cottage Antiques
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Sunday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM
entities.description:
Carefully curated antiques in a cozy Le Claire storefront that rewards slow browsing.

Mississippi Cottage Antiques feels like wandering through someone's thoughtfully edited collection rather than a typical picker's warehouse. The shop is intimate enough that you'll notice the detail — vintage glassware arranged by color, furniture pieces that actually look like someone restored them rather than left them to rot. You'll spend longer than you planned, turning over a bread box or reading the back of an old postcard, and leave with something you didn't know you needed.

Le Claire's main strip has quietly become one of the QC's best kept shopping neighborhoods, and Mississippi Cottage fits right into that texture — the kind of place locals drift into on weekend afternoons. It's close enough to the riverfront for an easy loop, and the kind of spot that makes you want to explore what else is hiding in the same few blocks. Once you've been, you'll find yourself back.

entities.name: Mississippi Marketplace
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 6:00 PM
entities.description:
Local vendors and handmade goods under one Davenport roof.

Mississippi Marketplace is where the QC's makers and small sellers gather — the kind of place where you'll find vintage jewelry next to fresh-baked goods, local art beside handcrafted home goods. The vibe is unhurried and genuinely collaborative; owners rotate shifts, and there's a real sense that these vendors know each other. You could spend an afternoon browsing, or pop in for one specific maker and leave with three unexpected finds.

Located in Davenport, Mississippi Marketplace sits in the growing network of independent retail that's quietly reshaping the city's shopping landscape. It's the kind of place that rewards repeat visits — the inventory shifts as vendors rotate their stock, so there's always something new to discover. Once you've been, you'll understand why it keeps pulling locals back.

entities.name: Mississippi River Distilling Company - Downtown Lounge
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 4:00 – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 4:00 – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 4:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Friday: 4:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Saturday: 12:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Sunday: 1:00 – 8:00 PM
entities.description:
House-made spirits in a downtown room with real character and a strong pour.

Mississippi River Distilling Company's lounge feels like the kind of place locals actually choose on a Friday night — wood-heavy, unhurried, the kind of bar where the bartender knows what they're doing. The spirits are made right here in Davenport, which means you're tasting something with actual roots in the QC, not something shipped in from three states over. Order a cocktail built around their bourbon or rye, settle into the warm light, and you'll understand why people keep coming back.

The lounge sits in Downtown Davenport, steps from the river and part of a neighborhood that's quietly becoming a real reason to spend an evening on this side of the Mississippi. It's the kind of place that anchors a night out — the spot you go before or after dinner, or the reason you end up downtown at all. Once you've been, it stays on your rotation.

entities.name: Mississippi Spoon Gallery
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 5:00 PM
entities.description:
A gallery where local art and handmade spoons share the same reverent wall space.

Mississippi Spoon Gallery is exactly the kind of specific, uncompromising place that makes you stop mid-walk and wonder how you'd never noticed it before. The collection spans carved wooden spoons, ceramic vessels, and rotating work from regional artists — each piece feels chosen rather than curated by algorithm. The owners know every maker by name, and they'll spend twenty minutes talking about the grain in a particular piece of walnut if you let them.

Tucked into Davenport's emerging gallery district, Mississippi Spoon sits within walking distance of other independent art spaces and the riverfront. It's a room that rewards the kind of browsing locals do on Saturday mornings — the type of place that appears on our guides because it's genuinely singular, and because once you've found it, you'll keep coming back.

entities.name: Mississippi Valley Fair
entities.hours: Monday: 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Tuesday: 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Wednesday: 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Thursday: 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Friday: 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Saturday: Closed | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
A century-old county fair that still feels like genuine community tradition.

The Mississippi Valley Fair arrives each August with the kind of earnest midway energy that hasn't been smoothed away by corporate polish — corn dogs, livestock competitions, and the Ferris wheel against the river bluffs. The crowds are real Quad Citians, families who've been coming back for years, kids in 4-H shirts leading their animals through the barns, neighbors bumping into neighbors at the beer garden. It's the kind of place where the fair is still actually *about* the fair — not themed experiences or Instagram moments, but prize ribbons and funnel cakes and the satisfaction of a well-raised steer.

The fair happens on the grounds right along the Mississippi River in Davenport, making it one of those QC institutions that feels woven into the region's DNA. If you want to understand how the Quad Cities actually gathers and celebrates together — away from polished downtown attractions — this is where to look. It's messy and sprawling and genuine, which is exactly why it keeps drawing people back.

entities.name: Mo Brady's Steakhouse
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 4:00 – 8:30 PM | Wednesday: 4:00 – 8:30 PM | Thursday: 4:00 – 8:30 PM | Friday: 4:00 – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 4:00 – 9:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Family-owned steakhouse where regulars have claimed the same booth for decades.

Mo Brady's feels like the kind of place your parents took you for special occasions — dark wood, honest cocktails, steaks that don't need much fussing. The room has that lived-in warmth that only happens when a place has been doing the same thing well for a long time, and the kitchen clearly respects the basics: good beef, proper seasoning, sides that show up on time. It's quieter than you'd expect for a Friday night, which means there's actually space to talk and the bartender remembers what you ordered last time.

Mo Brady's sits in Davenport's dining landscape as a reminder that not every good meal needs to be trendy or new. It's the kind of establishment that anchors a neighborhood — reliable, unpretentious, and worth the reservation. Once you understand what Mo Brady's is, you'll find yourself returning to it the same way locals do, the way a steakhouse should be.

entities.name: Modern Woodmen Park
entities.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: Closed | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Historic ballpark where summer nights and river views meet minor-league baseball.

Modern Woodmen Park sits right on the Mississippi, and you feel it — the river breeze cutting through the stands, the late-day light hitting the water during evening games. It's a genuine minor-league experience: the Quad Cities River Bandits take the field with real skill and grit, and the crowd is invested in a way that reminds you why baseball matters in small cities. Between innings, families drift toward the Sky Deck or the kids' play area, but the real magic is watching a close game with the river as your backdrop.

Modern Woodmen Park anchors downtown Davenport's riverfront, just steps from the Figge Art Museum and the walking paths that define the area. It's one of those places that gives the Quad Cities its character — a working ballpark that's been part of the community for decades, where locals actually spend their summer evenings. Once you've caught a River Bandits game here, it becomes part of your seasonal rhythm.

entities.name: Moline Centre Main Street
entities.description:
Main Street revitalized — walkable blocks with local shops and real community backbone.

Moline Centre Main Street is what happens when a downtown actually listens to what people want: storefronts that matter, sidewalks wide enough to linger on, the kind of blocks where you run into neighbors. There's a deliberate feel here — not forced, but intentional — where independent businesses have room to breathe and the street itself becomes a reason to show up, not just pass through.

Moline Centre sits in the Overlook area, one of the QC's ongoing revival stories. It's the kind of place that matters most when you're looking for what actually anchors a community — not just the attractions that draw tourists, but the streets where locals spend their weekends and know the owners by name. Once you've walked it, you understand why it keeps showing up in conversations about the QC's future.

entities.name: Moments Tux Shop
entities.hours: Monday: 12:00 – 7:00 PM | Tuesday: 12:00 – 7:00 PM | Wednesday: 12:00 – 7:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 3:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
The Quad Cities' go-to for tuxedos, from prom night to forever.

Moments Tux Shop handles the details that matter—proper fit, honest alterations, the kind of service where they remember your measurements. Whether you're renting for a wedding or buying your first formal suit, the staff knows the difference between a quick fix and getting it right. They've dressed thousands of QC moments, and it shows in how they work.

Moments sits in Bettendorf, a short drive from the riverfront and easy to reach before or after other errands. It's the kind of specialist shop that disappears into the background until you need it—and then you realize how rare it is to find someone who actually knows tuxedos. Once you've been, you'll trust them with your next formal occasion.

entities.name: Monarch -Kitchen & Bar
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Davenport's most reliable dinner — careful cooking, a real bar, no pretense.

Monarch doesn't try to be anything other than what it is: a solid neighborhood restaurant where the kitchen takes care with seasonal ingredients and the bartender knows your drink by your third visit. The room is bright and unpretentious, with enough space that you're not listening to the table next to you, and the kind of hum that means locals have claimed it as their own. It's the place you go when you want a good meal without the fuss — and you'll get one.

Monarch sits in Davenport proper, far enough from the riverfront to feel like a real neighborhood spot rather than a tourist destination, which is exactly why it works. It's the kind of restaurant that builds its reputation slowly, through word-of-mouth and repeat visits, and once you're in the rotation, you understand why so many people keep coming back.

entities.name: Monson Full Service Towing
entities.hours: Monday: Open 24 hours | Tuesday: Open 24 hours | Wednesday: Open 24 hours | Thursday: Open 24 hours | Friday: Open 24 hours | Saturday: Open 24 hours | Sunday: Open 24 hours
entities.description:
The tow truck that actually picks up when you call at 2 a.m.

Monson's been the QC's reliable answer to roadside panic for years — the kind of outfit where dispatchers know the highways by heart and drivers show up without the attitude. Whether you're stranded on I-80 with a dead battery, need a quick hookup after a fender-bender, or just need someone who won't gouge you on a long haul, they're open every hour of every day and genuinely seem glad to help. That 4.9 rating isn't accident — it's the result of people remembering who came through when they needed it most.

Monson operates out of West Davenport, positioned exactly where you'd want them to be if your night suddenly went sideways. They're the kind of essential local business that doesn't make glamorous guides, but absolutely belongs in ours — because a great community isn't just about where to eat or drink. It's about knowing who to call when things fall apart, and knowing they'll answer.

entities.name: Nahant Marsh
entities.description: Marshland with a nature center, hiking trails, wildlife viewing & a boardwalk into the water.

entities.name: Navarro Canoe Co.
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: Closed | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Local canoe shop stocked with gear for serious paddlers and curious newcomers alike.

Walk into Navarro and you're in the hands of people who actually paddle the Rock River — they stock what works here, not what looks good in a catalog. The shop is compact but dense with canoes, paddles, and the kind of specific advice that comes from season after season on the water. Whether you're outfitting for a weekend float or hunting down a replacement blade, the crew knows the difference between what sounds good and what performs.

Navarro sits in Rock Island, a few minutes from the riverfront they know intimately. It's the kind of place that shows up again and again in conversations with serious local paddlers — the spot you call when you need to know what actually works on the QC's water. Once you've been, you'll see why.

entities.name: Necker's Jewelers
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 4:00 PM
entities.description:
A third-generation jeweler where the staff actually knows your name.

Necker's has been in Davenport long enough that regulars come back for anniversaries, engagements, and repairs — and the team remembers. The cases are thoughtfully arranged, not overwhelming, and the staff will spend time with you whether you're browsing or hunting for something specific. It's the kind of place where a simple cleaning doesn't feel transactional.

Necker's sits in Downtown Davenport, anchoring a block that rewards a slower pace and attention to detail. It's the rare local jewelry store that's survived and thrived, which says something about both the place and the people who run it — exactly the kind of business we return to again and again in our guides.

entities.name: Niabi Zoo
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Sunday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM
entities.description: 40+ acres opened in 1959 with over 140 species of exotic & native animals, a carousel & concessions.

entities.name: NorthPark Mall
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 6:00 PM
entities.description:
The QC's reliable anchor mall with real stores and a food court that surprises.

NorthPark has the bones of a classic midwest mall — wide corridors, decent natural light, a working fountain — plus a lineup that actually reflects what people shop for. The food court leans beyond the expected chains; grab lunch from one of the local vendors and claim a table while families mill through. Saturday afternoons pull the whole Davenport crowd, but if you slip in on a Tuesday morning, it's calm enough to actually browse without the noise.

Anchored in north Davenport, NorthPark is the kind of place that works when you need several things at once — a new pair of shoes, a phone screen replaced, dinner before a movie. It's not designed to be precious or Instagram-worthy; it's built to serve its neighborhood, and it does that work well. The kind of reliable spot that deserves a spot in our guides not because it's trendy, but because it's genuinely useful and holds up to repeated visits.

entities.name: Northwest Plumbing, Heating & AC
entities.hours: Monday: Open 24 hours | Tuesday: Open 24 hours | Wednesday: Open 24 hours | Thursday: Open 24 hours | Friday: Open 24 hours | Saturday: Open 24 hours | Sunday: Open 24 hours
entities.description:
A 24-hour plumbing lifeline that shows up when you need it most.

When your water heater fails at midnight or your kitchen sink backs up on a Sunday, Northwest Plumbing answers—and they've been doing it for long enough that they know every old house quirk in Davenport. The technicians arrive in marked trucks, come prepared, and explain what's wrong without the sales pitch. Whether it's a burst pipe in January or AC that won't kick on in August, they're the number you'll find yourself calling again.

Northwest Plumbing is the kind of essential local business that doesn't make the tourism guides—but absolutely should be in your phone. It's the place your neighbors recommend without hesitation, the one that's earned nearly 1,400 five-star reviews by showing up on time and doing the job right. In a community, these are the businesses that matter most.

entities.name: Obsidian and Sage LLC
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Thoughtfully curated gifts and home goods with real character.

Obsidian and Sage feels like walking into someone's carefully edited home — the kind of person who knows the difference between clutter and intentional collection. The inventory shifts with intention: you'll find candles that actually smell like something, ceramics with personality, the sort of gifts that land because they say something true about the person giving them. It's the rare shop where browsing doesn't feel like work, where staff remember what you were looking for last month.

Tucked into Moline, Obsidian and Sage has built a loyal following — nearly 95 five-star reviews don't happen by accident. It's the kind of local spot that makes gift-giving feel less like an obligation and more like an opportunity, which is exactly why it belongs in our guides. Once you know it's there, you'll find yourself returning between seasons, watching what's new.

entities.name: Odd Bettie’s
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: Closed | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 3:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 3:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 3:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Vintage finds and oddities curated with real taste in small-town Aledo.

Odd Bettie's opens just three days a week—Thursday through Saturday, 10 to 3—which means the inventory feels genuinely curated, not picked over. The shop stocks vintage clothing that actually fits, mid-century pieces that don't need rescue, and the kind of oddball treasures (taxidermy, old signage, forgotten collectibles) that make you linger longer than you planned. It's the rare thrift experience where you leave with something you actually wanted, not just something you could justify.

Aledo sits a short drive west of the main Quad Cities corridor, and Odd Bettie's is exactly the kind of destination that rewards leaving your usual route. The limited hours and careful curation suggest the owner knows what she's doing—this isn't a warehouse, it's a curated collection. It's the kind of place that pulls people back repeatedly, each visit revealing something new.

entities.name: Olathea Creek Vineyard and Winery
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: Closed | Thursday: Closed | Friday: 12:00 – 7:00 PM | Saturday: 12:00 – 7:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 6:00 PM
entities.description:
Vineyard views and house wines in quiet Le Claire.

Olathea Creek sits on the edge of Le Claire with vines visible from the tasting room — a low-key spot where the owners pour their own wines and actually remember your name. Weekend afternoons draw a mixed crowd: couples splitting a bottle on the patio, locals who've been coming since opening, the occasional group that wandered over from the antique shops downtown. The wine list skews toward what grows well in the Midwest climate, and the staff won't oversell what they're pouring.

Le Claire has quietly become a destination for the QC's slower afternoons — antique hunting, riverside walks, spots like this that reward showing up without a plan. Olathea Creek belongs in that rotation, the kind of place that anchors a half-day wandering the village. It's open weekends only, which somehow makes it feel more intentional, like you're in on something the weekday crowds miss.

entities.name: Old Oaks Winery
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: Closed | Thursday: Closed | Friday: 12:00 – 7:00 PM | Saturday: 12:00 – 7:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 5:00 PM
entities.description:
Local winemaker's tasting room with honest pours and no pretension.

Old Oaks fills a small Milan storefront with the kind of relaxed energy that comes from pouring your own wines — the owner's likely to be there, talking through what's in your glass without the sommelier act. Weekend afternoons draw a steady mix of regulars and curious newcomers, all of them leaving with something they actually want to drink. The tasting lineup shifts with what's ready, but there's always a reason to come back.

Old Oaks sits in Milan, a quiet corner of the Quad Cities that rewards the five-minute drive east. It's the kind of small, locally-driven spot that defines what we look for — a place where the person running it actually cares what you think of it, and where an afternoon visit becomes the kind of random discovery you tell people about.

entities.name: Olde Towne Bakery
entities.hours: Monday: 6:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Tuesday: 6:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 6:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 6:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 6:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 6:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description: Local sweet shop crafting specialty cakes for all occasions & a classic array of cookies & pastries.

entities.name: Ollie's Bargain Outlet
entities.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Sunday: 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM
entities.description:
Chaotic treasure hunt where you actually find deals worth the dig.

Ollie's is organized chaos in the best way — bins overflow with closeout kitchen gadgets, seasonal goods stacked three deep, name-brand pantry items at prices that make you double-check the tags. You'll spend an hour wandering aisles that shift stock weekly, stumbling onto things you didn't know you needed. The thrill isn't in knowing what you'll find; it's in what you might.

Ollie's sits in Moline, just far enough off the main drag to feel like a local secret but easy enough to swing by on a Saturday afternoon. It's the kind of place that rewards patience and curiosity — the shopper who loves the hunt more than the list will leave happier than most. Once you've found that inexplicable deal (a case of good olive oil for $8? A set of Le Creuset on clearance?), you'll be back.

entities.name: Osaka steakhouse
entities.hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 11:30 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM | Saturday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 9:00 PM
entities.description:
Hibachi theater and sushi that keeps Moline's dinner table buzzing.

Osaka brings the full performance—chef knife-work, flames, the rhythmic scrape of steel on hot iron—and it lands. The hibachi counter is where the energy lives, especially on weekends when the place fills with families and groups leaning in to watch their dinner cook in front of them. Between the theatrical tables, there's solid sushi and a quieter dining room if you want the food without the show.

Osaka sits in Moline, anchoring the casual dining scene with something that actually draws a crowd. It's the kind of place that works for a birthday, a date night, or just wanting to eat something good that feels like an occasion—and the 866 Google reviews speak to how often the QC returns. Once you've watched a chef build an onion volcano at the hibachi, you'll understand why it keeps people coming back.

entities.name: Palmer Family and Chiropractic History Museum
entities.description:
Where chiropractic history lives in one surprising Davenport mansion.

The Palmer Museum occupies a grand old house that feels less like a typical museum and more like stepping into a very specific corner of American wellness history. You'll find original treatment tables, vintage promotional materials, and the earnest scientific ambitions of early 1900s alternative medicine laid out room by room — it's the kind of hyperlocal history that makes you realize how much of what we take for granted today was once radical and controversial. The whole experience moves quickly; it's intimate enough that you can actually read the handwritten notes and examine the equipment up close.

The Palmer Museum sits in Davenport, the birthplace of chiropractic itself — D.D. Palmer founded the practice here in 1895, and that foundation still shapes the city's identity. It's an essential stop for anyone curious about regional history or how a single idea can reshape an entire profession and a place. The kind of specific, purposeful visit that reminds you why the Quad Cities has stories worth knowing.

entities.name: Palmer Hills Golf Course
entities.description:
A well-kept municipal course where locals actually want to play.

Palmer Hills sits on the eastern edge of Bettendorf with the kind of manicured fairways and honest greens that keep the same faces coming back. It's not fancy—no pretense, no initiation fees—just solid turf, reasonable rates, and a clubhouse where people know each other's handicaps. The back nine opens up nicely, and on weekend mornings you'll see the regulars working through their usual four-somes while the coffee stays hot inside.

Palmer Hills represents what the Quad Cities does well: accessible recreation that actually gets used. It's the kind of course that anchors a neighborhood, where membership means showing up on Thursday evenings or Saturday mornings, not paying dues to a private club. For anyone looking to understand how Bettendorf residents spend their time—and where to find them on a clear day—Palmer Hills belongs in the picture.

entities.name: Parkside Grill & Lounge
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 1:00 AM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 1:00 AM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 1:00 AM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 1:00 AM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 1:00 AM
entities.description:
Sand, burgers, and late-night drinks in Moline's most reliably good backyard.

Parkside feels like the bar your friend's older sibling would throw a party at — sprawling enough that you can actually move around, with a sand volleyball court that gets competitive on summer nights and a patio where the real conversations happen. The burger menu is straightforward and honest (the kind of place that knows what it does well), and they're open late enough that it becomes a natural second or third stop after elsewhere hasn't quite satisfied.

Parkside anchors Park Hill, Moline's residential pocket with real bones — the kind of neighborhood that draws locals instead of just passing traffic. It's become the kind of place that shows up reliably on our guides because it works: unpretentious, practical, and genuinely built for hanging around.

entities.name: Pedal Pub Quad Cities
entities.description:
Pedal-powered bar crawl that turns downtown into a moving party.

Eight of you climb aboard a bike-built-for-many, grab a beer from the on-board cooler, and start pedaling through downtown Davenport's streets. It's ridiculous and social in the best way — part bar, part fitness, part excuse to laugh at how uncoordinated a group of friends can be. The pedal pub guides you between stops, and strangers become instant teammates when everyone's pushing the same pedals.

Pedal Pub Quad Cities operates out of Downtown Davenport, right where the QC's energy concentrates on weekends and summer nights. It's the kind of place that gets people out of their usual patterns — not just visiting a bar, but moving through the neighborhood together, turning the streets themselves into the experience. Once you've done it, you'll spot other Pedal Pubs rolling past and remember why you booked your own.

entities.name: Pee Wees restaurant.
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Sunday: 11:30 AM – 5:00 PM
entities.description:
A Rock Island institution serving comfort food that hasn't changed in decades.

Pee Wees feels like stepping into a time capsule — the kind of place where the same families have ordered the same meals for generations, and the staff remembers. The menu sticks to what it does: fried chicken, burgers, and sides that taste like someone's grandmother decided to open a restaurant. It's unpretentious and honest, the sort of spot where you go when you want food that satisfies without fuss, and a 4.7-star rating from over 550 reviews proves the QC agrees.

Pee Wees sits in Rock Island, just across the river from Davenport and minutes from the Quad City waterfront. It's the kind of neighborhood institution that anchors a community — the place people mention when they're telling you about where they grew up, or where they take their own kids now. Once you've been, you'll understand why it keeps showing up in conversations about where to actually eat in the QC.

entities.name: PERFECTA Beauty
entities.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 3:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Full-service beauty studio where the details matter and the results show.

Perfecta has built a loyal following across the QC for a reason — they listen, they're precise, and they remember what you asked for last time. Whether you're in for threading, waxing, nails, or a full beauty refresh, the team moves with intention. The five-star reviews aren't accident; they're the result of showing up consistently and treating each appointment like it matters.

Perfecta sits in East Moline, a neighborhood that's becoming quietly essential for everyday services done right. It's the kind of place that builds trust slowly through word-of-mouth and keeps customers coming back because the care is genuine. Once you've found your person here, you stop looking elsewhere.

entities.name: Petco
entities.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Sunday: 10:00 AM – 8:00 PM
entities.description:
One-stop pet supply shop where locals stock up before the weekend rush.

Petco in Moline is the kind of place you stop into without planning to, then realize you've been there forty minutes comparing dog bed fills. The aisles are well-organized enough that you can find what you need quickly — prescription pet medications, toys that actually last, those small things you forgot existed until your cat knocked over the water bowl again. Saturday mornings draw the whole neighborhood: folks with new puppies, seasoned pet owners restocking, people just browsing because their dog is home sick and they want to do something.

Petco sits in Moline, right where convenience matters — easy access, long hours, parking that doesn't make you circle twice. It's a practical stop that earns its place in our guides not because it's precious or rare, but because it's genuinely useful when you need it, and the QC pet owners we know keep coming back.

entities.name: Planet Fitness
entities.hours: Monday: 5:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Tuesday: Open 24 hours | Wednesday: Open 24 hours | Thursday: Open 24 hours | Friday: 12:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Sunday: 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM
entities.description:
Round-the-clock lifting and cardio for the early risers and night owls.

Planet Fitness keeps the lights on when most of Davenport is sleeping — open 24 hours Tuesday through Thursday, early mornings starting at 5 AM the rest of the week. The equipment is standard and well-maintained, the purple and yellow branding unmistakable, and the crowd is genuinely mixed: serious lifters, people recovering from injuries, folks just trying to move their bodies before work. It's straightforward fitness without the pretension, the kind of place where you show up to do the work and leave.

Located in Davenport, Planet Fitness fits into the QC's practical side — reliable, accessible, and always there when you need it. If you're new to the area or looking for a straightforward gym membership that doesn't demand a long-term commitment (their pricing is famously flexible), this is the kind of place that earns its high Google rating from locals who appreciate consistency and availability over frills.

entities.name: Playcrafters Barn Theatre
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: Closed | Thursday: Closed | Friday: 5:30 – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 5:30 – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 1:30 – 6:00 PM
entities.description:
Community theater that's been staging shows in a real barn since 1946.

Playcrafters does something rare: it fills an actual barn with live theater, which means creaky floors, exposed beams, and an intimacy that can't be faked. The Friday and Saturday nights draw crowds who come for everything from musicals to comedies — the kind of productions where you recognize half the cast from around town. Sunday matinees feel like a neighborhood gathering, the sort of thing that brings three generations together without feeling obligatory.

Playcrafters sits in Moline, anchoring a corner of the QC that doesn't always get attention but absolutely deserves it. It's exactly the kind of local institution — earnest, unpretentious, doing one thing well for decades — that makes a community feel like a real place. Once you've spent an evening watching neighbors perform in a hundred-year-old barn, you understand why the Quad Cities stays.

entities.name: Pour Bros. Craft Taproom Moline
entities.hours: Monday: 5:00 – 10:00 PM | Tuesday: 5:00 – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 3:00 – 11:00 PM | Thursday: 3:00 – 11:00 PM | Friday: 3:00 PM – 1:00 AM | Saturday: 12:00 PM – 1:00 AM | Sunday: 12:00 – 7:00 PM
entities.description:
A serious craft beer bar where the tap list changes faster than you can finish a pour.

Pour Bros. has that rare quality of feeling both ambitious and unpretentious—the kind of place where the bartender can walk you through a rotating selection of local and harder-to-find brews without making you feel small for not knowing the difference. The space itself is built for lingering: high-top tables that encourage conversation, enough seating to actually breathe, and a setup that makes browsing the taps feel like an event rather than a transaction. Weekend afternoons draw a crowd, but weeknight visits reward you with room to settle in and actually talk.

Pour Bros. sits in Moline, just across the river from downtown, and has become the kind of spot that keeps craft beer enthusiasts coming back with genuine anticipation about what's pouring this week. It's the centerpiece of a growing nightlife stretch that's worth exploring on foot—easy to pair with dinner nearby or a longer evening if the night's going well. Once you've been, you'll find yourself checking their rotation before heading over.

entities.name: Public House
entities.hours: Monday: 12:00 PM – 2:00 AM | Tuesday: 12:00 PM – 2:00 AM | Wednesday: 12:00 PM – 2:00 AM | Thursday: 12:00 PM – 2:00 AM | Friday: 12:00 PM – 2:00 AM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM
entities.description:
A no-nonsense dive where Davenport comes to unwind and stay late.

Public House has the kind of worn-in comfort that only comes from being a real neighborhood gathering spot — the kind of place where the bartender knows what you drink and the crowd shifts from after-work unwinders to late-night regulars as the sun drops. The beer list is straightforward, the bourbon pours are honest, and the jukebox leans toward the songs people actually want to hear. It's open until 2 a.m. most nights, which in Davenport means it's often the last reasonable option when the evening refuses to end.

Public House sits in Davenport proper, away from the polished riverfront corridor — it's the kind of place that exists for locals first and serves as a reliable benchmark for what a good bar should be. Once you've found it, you'll keep coming back, and you'll start bringing friends who get it.

entities.name: Purgatory's Pub
entities.description:
A dive bar with pool tables, cheap wings, and a neighborhood crowd that actually talks to strangers.

Purgatory's has the kind of worn-in charm that only comes from years of being a regular spot — neon signs casting a warm glow over scarred wood, the crack of pool balls echoing from the back room, wings arriving hot and saucy without pretense. The bartenders know their customers by name and drink, and there's always a game going if you want in. It's the place you end up after work or a game, where the TV's on but not screaming, and conversation actually matters.

Purgatory's sits in Bettendorf, just far enough from Downtown to feel like a true neighborhood anchor rather than a destination bar — the kind of spot that holds the QC together. It's been here long enough that multiple generations have bent elbows at the same bar, and that loyalty shows. Once you've found it, you'll keep coming back.

entities.name: Putnam Museum and Science Center
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 5:00 PM
entities.description:
Natural history and hands-on science under one historic roof.

The Putnam sits on Davenport's riverfront like a repository of curiosities—and it actually is one. Inside, you'll find taxidermied animals from regional hunts, Indigenous artifacts that anchor the QC's actual history, and science exhibits that work equally well for restless eight-year-olds and adults who've forgotten how pulleys function. The planetarium shows run on weekends, the building itself (a brick mansion-turned-museum) feels appropriately serious about its work, and the gift shop lingers in your mind longer than you'd expect.

The Putnam Museum sits on Davenport's riverfront, a ten-minute walk from downtown and an easy anchor for a full afternoon—pair it with lunch in the District or a walk along the Mississippi. It's the kind of place that belongs in our guides because it's genuinely rooted in the region's identity, not a chain or a franchise, and because locals keep returning to it across generations. Once you've spent an hour here, you'll understand why the QC takes its natural history seriously.

entities.name: QC Family Entertainment

entities.description:
Bowling, arcade games, and late-night energy in Moline.

QC Family Entertainment is the kind of place that doesn't need to reinvent itself — it does one thing well and shows up for it. Lanes are busy most nights, the arcade hums with the sound of games that actually work, and Friday and Saturday nights stretch until 11 p.m., which matters if you're the type who bowls better after dark. It's unpretentious and well-worn, the kind of spot where regulars have their favorite lanes and the staff knows what you're ordering before you ask.

QC Family Entertainment sits in Moline, a neighborhood with its own rhythm separate from downtown's careful curation — this is where people go to actually do something, not perform. It belongs in our guides because it's exactly the kind of reliable, low-stakes gathering spot that holds the QC together, the place you text a friend from at 9:45 on a Friday night when the evening needs one more thing.

entities.name: Quad Cities Community Foundation
entities.hours: Monday: 8:00 AM – 4:30 PM | Tuesday: 8:00 AM – 4:30 PM | Wednesday: 8:00 AM – 4:30 PM | Thursday: 8:00 AM – 4:30 PM | Friday: 8:00 AM – 12:00 PM | Saturday: Closed | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Where local philanthropy gets a face and a mission you can believe in.

The Quad Cities Community Foundation isn't a place you stumble into by accident — it's where you go when you want to understand how giving actually works in this region. The staff knows the nonprofit landscape like locals know the river, and they'll walk you through funding opportunities, grant processes, and the stories behind the organizations that matter most to your neighbors. It's the kind of conversation that happens in a real office, with real people who've been doing this work for years and actually care whether your money lands where it's supposed to.

Planted in Bettendorf, the Foundation sits at the quiet heart of the QC's philanthropic infrastructure — the kind of essential institution that doesn't always make the highlight reel but absolutely shapes community life. Whether you're looking to start a fund, explore where to give, or just understand how the region takes care of itself, this is the place that knows. It belongs in our guides because the Quad Cities runs on people who care enough to organize that caring, and this is where that organization happens.

entities.name: Quad Cities International Airport
entities.hours: Monday: 3:30 AM – 12:00 AM | Tuesday: 3:30 AM – 12:00 AM | Wednesday: 3:30 AM – 12:00 AM | Thursday: 3:30 AM – 12:00 AM | Friday: 3:30 AM – 12:00 AM | Saturday: 3:30 AM – 12:00 AM | Sunday: 3:30 AM – 12:00 AM
entities.description:
A regional hub where the QC connects to everywhere else.

The Quad Cities International Airport moves quietly — no chaos, no endless corridors, just a straightforward path from car to gate. The terminal has the charm of a place that's been here since the early 1900s, updated enough to function smoothly but still recognizably local. There's actual art on the walls (rotating local work), and you won't spend 20 minutes looking for your airline counter or wondering which security line to join.

MLT sits in Moline, a few minutes off I-74, and it's the practical heart of how the QC connects outward — whether you're catching a morning flight to Chicago or heading somewhere farther. It's not a destination in the traditional sense, but it's exactly the kind of efficient, human-scaled infrastructure that makes living here easier than it might be elsewhere. Once you've flown out and back through it, you understand why the airport matters to the region's identity.

entities.name: Quad Cities River Bandits
entities.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: Closed | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Minor league baseball where the Mississippi River is part of the view.

Modern Woodmen Park sits right on the water, and summer nights here feel like the QC at its most relaxed — families spread across the bleachers, the smell of ballpark food mixing with river breeze, and if you're in the right seat, you can actually see the water beyond the outfield. The River Bandits play the game at a pace that lets you talk, eat, and actually watch without feeling rushed. There's a cornfield in center field (yes, really), a Sky Deck if you want to float above the crowd, and enough going on for kids that parents can actually enjoy an inning or two in peace.

The River Bandits are downtown Davenport's seasonal heartbeat — the kind of place that anchors summer weekends and brings the neighborhood alive from May through September. It's a short walk or drive from the riverfront, and a night at Modern Woodmen Park is one of those QC experiences that feels both small-town and genuinely good, the kind of evening you'll find yourself planning again next year.

entities.name: Quad City Arts
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
The QC's creative hub, showcasing local artists and regional talent year-round.

Quad City Arts operates as both gallery and gathering space—rotating exhibitions fill the walls, but it's the feeling of the place that sticks with you. You'll find emerging painters alongside established names, installations that make you stop and stare, and a staff that actually talks to you about the work instead of hovering silently. The Friday night openings draw a genuine crowd, the kind where conversations about art spill into the parking lot.

Located in Rock Island's arts corridor, Quad City Arts sits within walking distance of other galleries and creative spaces that have quietly transformed this neighborhood into the QC's most vital cultural stretch. It's where the region's artists show their work, where locals discover what's possible when people commit to making space for art. If you care about what the Quad Cities is becoming—not just what it was—this is the place to keep returning to.

entities.name: Quad City Music Guild
entities.description:
A teaching studio where serious musicians build craft and community.

Walk into the Quad City Music Guild and you'll hear it immediately — the sound of someone getting better. Private lesson rooms line the halls, each one humming with focused work: a student wrestling with a Bach prelude, another nailing the rhythm section on a jazz standard. The instructors here aren't just qualified; they're working musicians who actually care about their students' progress. Whether you're seven years old picking up violin for the first time or an adult returning to piano after decades, there's a real sense that you're part of something that takes music seriously.

Planted in Moline, the Music Guild has built the kind of reputation that reaches across the whole QC — the place families call when they want real instruction, not just babysitting with an instrument. The 4.8 rating isn't accident; it reflects years of consistent teaching and the kind of loyalty that comes from actually changing how people relate to music. It's the backbone of the QC's music life, the kind of anchor institution that doesn't need a storefront location to draw people in.

entities.name: Quad City Steamwheelers
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: Closed | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Semi-pro football with heart, played right here on the river's edge.

The Quad City Steamwheelers are proof that football doesn't need a multi-million dollar stadium to matter. This is community sport at its most genuine — players who work day jobs, coaches who've been around the QC forever, fans who actually know the guys on the field. Gameday is unpretentious and loud, the kind of place where a kid can run along the sideline and nobody minds, where the concessions are simple and the conversation is real.

Based in Moline and deeply woven into the QC's sports fabric, the Steamwheelers represent the kind of hyperlocal institution that makes a region feel like home. They're worth a visit if you care about authentic community gathering — the sort of thing you'll remember not for flash or polish, but for the unmistakable feeling of belonging somewhere that matters to the people around you.

entities.name: Quad City Storm
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Wednesday: Closed | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Friday: Closed | Saturday: Closed | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Minor league hockey with real heart and neighborhood grit.

The Storm play in a converted arena that feels intimate in the best way — close enough to the ice that you can hear the crack of sticks and see the sweat. The crowd is die-hard regulars, families who've made this a Tuesday or Thursday night ritual, and kids pressed against the glass. It's honest, unpretentious hockey where the players are hungry and the concessions won't bankrupt you.

Quad City Storm sits in Moline, part of the working backbone of the QC that doesn't always make the curated lists but absolutely should. This is where locals go to feel connected to their community in the most direct way — cheering for neighbors, showing up season after season, building something real. Once you've caught a game here, you understand why the Storm matters to this place.

entities.name: Quad City Symphony
entities.description:
The QC's orchestra, live and in the Adler—worth the ticket every time.

The Quad City Symphony fills the Adler Theatre with the kind of sound that justifies a night out—strings tight, brass bright, the whole room humming with it. Whether you're catching a classical subscription series or a pops concert, there's a real sense of investment here; this is a working orchestra that knows its audience and takes the repertoire seriously. Evenings at the Adler are unhurried: you can arrive early, walk the lobby, settle in, and let the music do what it does.

The QCSO sits at the heart of Downtown Davenport's cultural life, anchored in the restored Adler Theatre just steps from the riverfront. It's a cornerstone institution that shows up in our guides because it represents what the QC does well—maintaining something real and excellent without pretense. Once you've been, you'll find yourself checking the season schedule.

entities.name: Quad City Tennis Club
entities.hours: Monday: 8:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Tuesday: 8:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Wednesday: 8:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Thursday: 8:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Friday: 8:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Saturday: 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Sunday: 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM
entities.description:
Indoor courts and patient instruction for everyone from beginners to competitive players.

The Quad City Tennis Club has the feel of a place built by people who actually play tennis—the kind of facility where you'll find serious players drilling serves alongside kids just learning how to hold a racket. The indoor courts mean you're playing year-round, no weather excuses, and the instruction leans toward real skill-building rather than just hitting balls around. Whether you're signing up for group lessons or booking court time with a friend, the staff knows the game well enough to offer the kind of casual advice that actually sticks.

Located in Moline, the Tennis Club sits in a part of the QC that's become a real hub for sports and recreation. It's the kind of straightforward, well-run place that locals return to season after season—the sort of spot that builds community through consistency and genuine expertise, not flash. If you're looking to get serious about tennis or just want a reliable place to play, this one belongs in the regular rotation.

entities.name: Quad-Cities Waterfront Convention Center
entities.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: Closed | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
The riverfront's anchor—where the QC gathers for conferences, weddings, and big-tent events.

The Quad-Cities Waterfront Convention Center sits right on the water in Bettendorf, with floor-to-ceiling windows that don't waste the view. Whether you're here for a regional conference, a wedding reception, or a local trade show, the space feels intentional—built for the river and the people crossing the I-74 bridge to get here. The bones of the building are clean and modern; the real draw is that you can literally watch barges move past while you network or dance.

The Waterfront Convention Center anchors Bettendorf's riverfront district, positioning itself as the serious venue for the QC's larger gatherings—the kind of events that draw people from across the region. It's the kind of infrastructure that matters quietly: the place where the Quad Cities presents itself, where business happens, where people mark occasions. If you're planning something that needs real space and river views, this is where the QC goes.

entities.name: Quartz Botanicals
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 10:30 AM – 5:30 PM | Thursday: 10:30 AM – 5:30 PM | Friday: 10:30 AM – 5:30 PM | Saturday: 10:30 AM – 7:00 PM | Sunday: 10:30 AM – 5:30 PM
entities.description:
A plant shop where the collection actually surprises you week to week.

Quartz Botanicals stocks the usual suspects—pothos, snake plants, monsteras—but the rotating selection is where it gets interesting. The owners clearly spend time hunting for uncommon varieties and propagations you won't find at the big box stores. Mid-week afternoons are quiet enough to actually browse and ask questions; Saturday afternoons draw the serious plant people and the curious newcomers alike.

Quartz Botanicals sits in East Moline, just far enough from downtown to feel like a genuine neighborhood discovery rather than a planned stop. It's the kind of place that makes you reconsider a corner of your home—or start a whole new obsession—and worth the drive if you're already out that direction or looking for a specific plant that's hard to track down locally.

entities.name: R. Richard Bittner Downtown YMCA
entities.hours: Monday: 4:30 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 4:30 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 4:30 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 4:30 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 4:30 AM – 8:00 PM | Saturday: 6:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Sunday: 7:00 AM – 4:00 PM
entities.description:
Downtown's serious gym with roots that run deep into Davenport.

The Bittner opens at 4:30 AM on weekdays—early enough to catch the pre-work crowd hitting the weights before the day gets loud. The equipment is well-maintained and thoughtfully arranged, the kind of gym where you can actually find a free barbell at 6 AM, and the staff knows regulars by name. It's the kind of place that feels less like a corporate fitness chain and more like infrastructure—the place Davenport has relied on for decades to stay strong.

The R. Richard Bittner Downtown YMCA sits right in the heart of Downtown Davenport, steps from the riverfront and the growing cluster of shops and restaurants that make this neighborhood worth knowing. It's the rare gym that earns perfect reviews on Yelp and near-perfect on Google—not through hype, but through consistency and genuine community investment. Once you're part of the morning rotation here, you understand why it keeps showing up on our list.

entities.name: Raccoon Motel
entities.hours: Monday: 4:00 – 10:00 PM | Tuesday: 4:00 – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 4:00 – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 4:00 – 10:00 PM | Friday: 4:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Saturday: 12:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
A dive bar with cocktails that punch above its weight and zero pretension.

Raccoon Motel feels like the kind of place that's been in Downtown Davenport longer than it probably has — all worn wood, low light, and bartenders who know what they're doing. The drinks are the real draw: classic cocktails made with care, not the watered-down kind you'd expect from a spot this unpretentious. It's the rare bar that gets the balance right — skilled enough to respect what you're ordering, casual enough that you don't feel like you have to dress up to get in.

Raccoon Motel sits on the Downtown strip where the QC's best nightlife clusters, a short walk from the riverfront and the kind of place that anchors a good evening. It's become a reliable favorite for locals who want a real drink and real company without the theater — the kind of spot that shows up again and again once you know it's there.

entities.name: Radicle Effect Brewerks
entities.hours: Monday: 2:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Tuesday: 2:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Wednesday: 2:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Thursday: 2:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Friday: 2:00 PM – 1:00 AM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 1:00 AM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM
entities.description:
Local craft brewery where Rock Island gathers over quality beer and real conversation.

Radicle Effect pulls off the hardest trick in breweries — it feels genuinely lived-in rather than designed. The space has the ease of a place where regulars know the bartenders' names and newcomers get brought into the fold without trying. They're serious about their beer (the ratings speak for themselves), but the vibe stays approachable; you're just as welcome nursing a single pour as you are settling in for the evening.

Radicle Effect Brewerks anchors Rock Island's growing nightlife scene, sitting in a neighborhood that's quietly becoming the QC's most interesting corner. It's the kind of place that shows up again and again on our curated guides — the sort of brewery that matters not because it's shiny and new, but because it's become woven into how people actually spend their time here.

entities.name: Ragged Records
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Vinyl, vintage finds, and the hum of record collectors' conversations.

Ragged Records pulls you in with the kind of organized chaos that only a serious record shop can pull off — bins deep enough to lose an afternoon in, prices that won't hollow out your wallet, and staff who actually know what they're talking about. The listening station hums constantly; regulars flip through new arrivals on Saturday mornings like it's ritual. Whether you're hunting a specific pressing or just browsing what vinyl the week brought in, there's real curation happening here, not just inventory.

Ragged Records anchors the Downtown Davenport stretch where the bones of the QC's music history still run strong. It's the kind of place that matters to a community — a gathering spot for collectors, a resource for people who still believe in the album as an object, a reminder that some corners of downtown are built on passion rather than footfall. Once you've been, you'll understand why it keeps showing up on our radar.

entities.name: Railroading Heritage of Midwest America
entities.hours: Monday: 8:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Tuesday: 8:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Wednesday: 8:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Thursday: 8:00 AM – 12:00 AM | Friday: 8:00 AM – 4:30 AM | Saturday: Closed | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
A railroad museum where vintage locomotives tell the Midwest's industrial story.

Walk the grounds at Railroading Heritage of Midwest America and you're standing in the shadow of actual working history — locomotives that hauled freight and passengers across the region, now preserved and displayed so you can climb up, peer into the cab, and feel the weight of what it took to build this country. The collection spans different eras and engine types, each one a different chapter. It's the kind of place where a five-year-old gets genuinely thrilled and an adult finds themselves lost in the details of steam valve mechanics.

Silvis has always been tied to the rails — the town was literally built around them — and this museum is rooted in that identity. It's a short drive from downtown Davenport and the riverfront, but far enough out to feel like a genuine detour worth taking. If you're curious about what actually made the Quad Cities matter in the American story, this is where that answer is on display.

entities.name: Rascals Live Music Venue
entities.description:
Live music venue where the QC's best acts actually want to play.

Rascals has the kind of bones that matter—a real stage, decent sound, and a bar that knows how to pour a drink while you're watching a band. The crowd skews local and loyal; you'll recognize faces from other shows, other venues. Thursday through Saturday nights fill up fast, and the calendar reads like a who's who of regional touring acts and homegrown talent that's outgrown smaller rooms.

Rascals sits in Uptown Moline, a neighborhood that's quietly become the QC's live music anchor. It's the kind of venue that shows up again and again on our curated guides because it actually matters to the local music scene—not a side hustle, but a real reason to head across the river and stay out late.

entities.name: Ray'z Barber Shop

entities.description:
A Davenport barbershop where the cuts are sharp and the wait is honest.

Ray'z has been the kind of place where you know what you're getting — clean lines, straightforward work, barbers who've been doing this long enough to know the difference between a fade that'll last two weeks and one that'll last three. The chairs fill up Saturday mornings and after work on Thursdays, which tells you something. Walk in without an appointment and you're looking at a real wait, but the regulars don't seem to mind; it's part of the rhythm.

Ray'z sits in Davenport proper, the kind of neighborhood barbershop that's become harder to find as chains move in. It's exactly the sort of place we profile in our guides — not because it's trendy or Instagram-ready, but because it actually serves the community that walks through its door, day after day, year after year.

entities.name: RAYGUN
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Sunday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM
entities.description:
Vintage-style tees and prints that feel like inside jokes with Iowa.

RAYGUN fills a corner of downtown with the kind of graphic tees that make you actually want to wear them — Iowa-specific designs that hit different if you're from here, clever enough that visitors want to take one home. The shop itself is compact and bright, the kind of place where you can flip through the whole selection in ten minutes but find yourself staying longer because the designs are genuinely funny. It's the rare merchandise store that doesn't feel like a tourist trap; these are things people actually buy for themselves.

Right On Time lives up to its name — they answer at 2 a.m. on a Sunday when your basement's flooding, and they show up ready to work. River Action Inc is where people who care about the Mississippi show up to do something about it. They're the outfit running cleanups, leading paddle trips, and fighting for better access to the water — the kind of organization that knows every sandbar and current eddy, and invites you to know them too. Stop by their Davenport office during weekday hours and you'll find folks plotting the next restoration project or prepping gear for a guided float.

River Action sits in Davenport, at the heart of what makes the Quad Cities singular — a real relationship with a working river. They're the connective tissue between the riverfront's natural life and the people who live here, the reason the water feels less like a boundary and more like a shared responsibility. The bar itself feels lived-in, the kind of place where regulars have their spot and the bartender remembers what you drink. Weekends stretch late with a solid tap list and the low hum of people actually talking to each other instead of their phones.

Planted on Rock Island's main stretch, the Ale House sits in an area that's quietly become the QC's most genuine neighborhood gathering spot — no pretense, just good food and better company. It's the kind of reliable anchor that shows up on our guides because it's where locals actually spend their Friday nights, not where they think they should be spending them.

entities.description:
A living museum where American military history walks right up to the riverbank.

The Rock Island Arsenal spreads across an island in the Mississippi, and the Welcome Center is your entry point to centuries of arms-making, engineering, and strategy. You'll see the grounds themselves—the red-brick buildings, the river views, the weight of what happened here—before stepping inside to understand it. The rangers know the stories, and the place rewards a slow walk; this isn't a quick photo stop.

Rock Island Arsenal sits on its own island in Moline, connected by bridge and separated from the everyday QC by water and gates—which somehow makes it feel both central and removed. It's the kind of destination that locals often pass by without stopping, which is exactly why it belongs here. If you've ever wondered what built this region, or you're visiting with someone interested in American industrial and military history, the Arsenal is the answer.

The Rock Island Downtown Alliance sits at the heart of Rock Island's riverfront neighborhood, the kind of organization that exists because locals decided their downtown mattered. Salon R5 feels intentional—small enough that your stylist actually remembers you, professional enough that you trust them with the cut that matters. The ratings speak for themselves: people come back, and they bring their friends. Whether it's color work, a fresh cut, or something more involved, there's a quiet confidence in how they approach the chair.

Salon R5 sits in Davenport, where word-of-mouth recommendations still carry real weight. It's the kind of place that shows up again and again in conversations among locals who care about where they spend their money—and their time. Once you've found a good stylist, you protect that information like it's gold.

entities.name: Saukie Golf Course

entities.description:
Municipal course where locals actually play, with views of the Rock River.

Saukie's the kind of place where you'll see the same faces on weekend mornings — regulars who know every slope and where the wind plays tricks. The course sits right there along the Rock River, so you get those occasional glimpses of water between the fairways, and the greens have real character, the kind that reward knowing where to aim. It's public, it's walkable if you want it to be, and the green fees won't wreck your week.

Saukie Golf Course sits in Rock Island, part of the QC's working landscape — a genuine community gathering spot rather than a destination resort course. It's the kind of place that shows up again and again on our curated guides because it's where the Quad Cities actually plays, and once you've walked those fairways, you'll understand why.

entities.name: Schebler Heating and Air
entities.hours: Monday: Open 24 hours | Tuesday: Open 24 hours | Wednesday: Open 24 hours | Thursday: Open 24 hours | Friday: Open 24 hours | Saturday: Open 24 hours | Sunday: Open 24 hours
entities.description:
The HVAC shop Quad Citians trust when the heat dies in January.

Schebler's been keeping the QC comfortable for long enough that they answer the phone at 2 a.m. on a Sunday without hesitation. The team knows the difference between a quick fix and a system that needs replacing — and they'll tell you which one you actually need, not the expensive option. Their 4.7 rating on Google isn't accident; it's built on thirty years of midnight emergency calls and honest diagnoses.

Schebler operates out of Bettendorf, where they've become the default choice for homeowners who've learned that HVAC emergencies don't follow business hours. They belong in our guides because they're the kind of essential local business that quietly makes the Quad Cities livable — the sort of place you hope you never need, but are grateful exists when you do.

entities.name: Schwiebert Riverfront Park
entities.hours: Monday: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Tuesday: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Friday: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM
entities.description:
Where Rock Island gathers to watch the river and let the kids loose.

Schwiebert is the kind of park that gets busy on purpose — families staking out picnic tables, kids running between the playground and splash pad, locals walking the river path at dusk. The water views aren't an afterthought here; they're the whole point. There's a real outdoor music area that hosts events, turning summer evenings into the kind of casual gathering that defines a neighborhood.

Schwiebert sits on Rock Island's riverfront, one of the QC's most underrated stretches of the Mississippi. It's the kind of place that works year-round — a quick stop for playground energy on a weekday afternoon, or a destination on a summer weekend when the concerts start. Once you've spent an evening here, you understand why Rock Island residents keep coming back.

entities.name: Scott County Park

entities.description: Over 1,000 acres of multi-use trails plus playground, swimming pool, equestrian & camping areas.

entities.name: Serenity Salon & Day Spa
entities.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Moline's quiet corner for facials, massage, and actual relaxation.

Serenity lives up to its name — the kind of place where you exhale the moment you walk in. The therapists know their craft (the Google reviews keep coming back to skill), and they're not rushing you through a schedule. Whether you're there for a deep tissue massage to unknot your shoulders or a facial that actually addresses what your skin needs, the experience feels unhurried and intentional.

Serenity Salon & Day Spa sits in Moline, a neighborhood with its own steady rhythm away from downtown's bustle. It's the rare local spa that builds real loyalty — 175 Google reviews and a 4.6 rating don't happen by accident. Once you find it, it becomes the kind of place you return to when you need to disappear for an hour.

entities.name: Sippis American Grill & Craft Beer
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Brick walls, craft beer lineup, and a fireplace that makes you want to stay longer.

Sippis feels like the kind of place that's been downtown forever — exposed brick, a real wood-burning fireplace, and a beer list that actually rewards paying attention. The kitchen handles American comfort food without pretension: burgers that land, wings that disappear, the kind of food that pairs naturally with a rotating roster of local and regional craft beers. Come for dinner on a Friday and you'll find the bar crowded with people who work nearby; come on a quieter weeknight and you might actually claim one of those fireplace-side tables.

Sippis sits in Downtown Davenport, anchoring the street-level restaurant scene with an unpretentious ease that matches the neighborhood's character. It's the kind of place that regulars return to again and again — not because it's fancy or trendy, but because it's genuinely good at what it does, and because the fireplace and the beer selection and the lack of performance make it feel like it's always been waiting for you.

entities.name: Skate City QCA
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 1:00 – 4:00 PM, 5:00 – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 1:00 – 4:00 PM, 6:00 – 8:00 PM | Friday: 1:00 – 4:00 PM, 6:00 – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 12:00 – 8:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 5:00 PM
entities.description:
A wood-floor rink where the QC's skaters have spun for decades.

Skate City QCA is the kind of place that smells like floor wax and sounds like wheels on wood — a proper skating rink with the worn-in comfort of somewhere that's been doing this forever. The lights dim, the music thumps, and whether you're eight or eighty, there's something about gliding around that oval that feels exactly right. Wednesday nights draw the school crowd; weekends bring families and the occasional group that just wants to remember what it felt like to move that way.

Skate City sits in East Moline, a short drive across the river from Downtown and Bettendorf — the kind of neighborhood spot that's easy to overlook unless you know to look. It's the place QC families return to year after year, the one where kids learn to balance and adults rediscover a simple joy. Once you've been, you'll understand why it keeps showing up on our guides.

entities.name: Skeleton Key Art and Antiques
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 12:00 – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 12:00 – 5:00 PM | Friday: 12:00 – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 4:00 PM
entities.description:
A curated rabbit hole of vintage finds, art, and objects with actual stories.

Walking into Skeleton Key feels like browsing through someone's thoughtfully edited personal collection—the kind of place where you go in for one thing and leave with three. The carefully arranged shelves hold everything from mid-century glass to hand-bound journals, antique keys (naturally), and local art that actually stops you. The owners clearly know their inventory and what they're sourcing; there's no clutter, just intention.

Skeleton Key anchors a quieter stretch of Rock Island, away from the main drag but worth the detour. It's the kind of spot that rewards slow looking and conversation—perfect for a weekend afternoon when you're not rushing, or the rare weekday when you manage to catch their limited Wednesday-Friday hours.

entities.name: Skellington Manor Escape Experience
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 1:00 – 7:00 PM | Wednesday: 1:00 – 7:00 PM | Thursday: 1:00 – 7:00 PM | Friday: 1:00 – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 1:00 – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 1:00 – 7:00 PM
entities.description:
Puzzle rooms that feel less like games and more like getting trapped somewhere real.

Skellington Manor takes the escape room formula and leans hard into atmosphere — dimly lit rooms with actual props you can touch, puzzles that unfold naturally rather than arbitrarily, and the kind of detail work that makes you forget you're on a timer. Whether you're a first-timer or you've done a dozen of these, there's something unsettling (in the best way) about the Manor's aesthetic; it doesn't feel like a theme park. Groups tend to get quiet once the door locks, focused, occasionally laughing when someone spots a clue everyone else missed.

Skellington Manor sits in Rock Island, just far enough from the Davenport-Bettendorf corridor to feel like a destination rather than a pit stop — the kind of place you plan a Friday or Saturday night around. It's perfect for a friend group looking for something more interactive than dinner, a date night with actual stakes, or a family wanting to work together on something that isn't screen-based. Once you've been, you'll understand why the ratings stay high and the same faces keep coming back.

entities.name: SmartStyle

entities.description:
Reliable haircuts and color in a bright, no-fuss Davenport salon.

SmartStyle does what it does well — clean cuts, honest pricing, and stylists who listen. The Elmore Avenue location is bright and straightforward, no pretense, with a steady stream of regulars who've found their person and stick with them. Walk-ins are welcome, but booking ahead means you're not watching the clock.

SmartStyle sits on the practical side of Davenport, easy to reach and easy to fit into a weekday errand run. It's the kind of place that works because it doesn't try to be anything other than what it is — a solid neighborhood salon where people actually get good haircuts, week after week.

entities.description: Lessons are available at this small ski area with 15 downhill trails, tubing lanes & a terrain park.

entities.name: Soar N Bounce Trampoline & Adventure Park - Davenport
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM
entities.description:
Wall-to-wall trampolines where kids burn energy and parents reclaim their sanity.

Soar N Bounce is controlled chaos in the best way — a sprawling warehouse of interconnected trampolines, foam pits, and dodgeball courts that somehow keeps even the most restless kids occupied for hours. The sound is pure childhood (squeaking sneakers, delighted screaming, the rhythmic bounce of springs), and the energy is contagious. It's the kind of place where your kid crashes hard on the drive home and actually sleeps through dinner.

Located in Davenport, Soar N Bounce fills a real gap for rainy-day adventures and birthday parties — the type of venue that becomes a repeat destination for local families. Open late on weekends and most weeknights, it slots easily into after-school schedules or weekend plans. Once you've discovered it, you'll find yourself recommending it to every parent friend who asks what to do with restless kids.
