entities.description:
Coffee and sandwiches in Moline that actually make you want to linger.

The Java Lab feels like the kind of place built by someone who gets it — good coffee paired with real food, and enough counter seating to watch the day happen. The sandwiches come thoughtfully built, the kind where you can tell someone cares about the ratio of filling to bread. Weekday mornings draw the regulars who know the drill; afternoons shift quieter, which is when you notice the light coming through and realize you've been here longer than you planned.

The Java Lab sits in Moline, across the river from the main downtown corridor but far enough in that it serves its neighborhood first — the kind of spot that builds its reputation one regular at a time. It's the place worth crossing the bridge for when you want coffee and lunch that both deserve your attention, and exactly the kind of small, run-well café that anchors our guides.

entities.name: The Landing Ice Rink
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 6:00 – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 6:00 – 9:00 PM | Friday: Closed | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM
entities.description:
Community ice rink where weekends fill with wobbling kids and their patient parents.

The Landing is where Bettendorf families go when they need to burn off winter energy — Saturday mornings are pure controlled chaos, the kind where you're watching your kid's ankles decide whether to cooperate while you grip the boards. Wednesday and Thursday nights skew quieter, a handful of regulars and date-night couples gliding past the handful of spectators. It's not fancy; it's functional in the best way, the kind of place that serves its neighborhood without pretense.

The Landing sits in Bettendorf, just east of downtown, and operates on a schedule built around school calendars and winter breaks — which means you'll want to check ahead before you go. It's the rare indoor rink in the QC that's actually accessible and affordable, making it a reliable pick for parents hunting a rainy-day plan or anyone who wants to remember what it feels like to lace up skates.

entities.name: The Last Picture House
entities.hours: Monday: 3:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Tuesday: 3:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Wednesday: 3:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Thursday: 3:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Friday: 1:00 PM – 1:00 AM | Saturday: 10:30 AM – 1:00 AM | Sunday: 10:30 AM – 12:00 AM
entities.description:
A speakeasy-style bar hiding behind a vintage movie theater façade.

The Last Picture House doesn't announce itself—you walk past the old marquee and through heavy doors into a dim, moody space that feels pulled from another era. The bartenders know their classics, and the crowd is the kind that lingers over cocktails on Friday nights, not rushing through. There's an earnestness to the place that rewards staying longer than you planned.

Downtown Davenport has quietly become a destination for nights out, and The Last Picture House sits at the heart of that shift—a bar that takes itself seriously without being pretentious. It's the kind of spot that shows up on our guides because it's genuinely good at what it does, and once you've found it, you'll keep coming back.

entities.name: The Logger House
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 1:00 AM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 1:00 AM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 1:00 AM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 1:00 AM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 1:00 AM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 1:00 AM | Sunday: 10:00 AM – 1:00 AM
entities.description:
A wood-paneled dive where Hampton comes to unwind and stay late.

The Logger House has the kind of worn-in comfort that only comes from decades of regulars and their stories soaked into the walls. The bar runs long, the beer list is straightforward, and the crowd on a Friday night spans everyone from the early-shift crew stopping by at 10 a.m. to the late-night set nursing whiskeys well past midnight. It's the kind of place where the bartender knows your name by your third visit and doesn't need you to order.

The Logger House sits in Hampton, a quieter corner of the QC that rewards the folks willing to venture slightly outside the main cluster. It's exactly the kind of neighborhood spot — genuine, unhurried, built on repetition — that makes the Quad Cities feel like a place where people actually live, not just visit. Once you find it, you'll understand why it keeps showing up on the route home.

entities.name: The Phoenix Restaurant & Martini Bar
entities.hours: Monday: 4:00 – 11:00 PM | Tuesday: 4:00 – 11:00 PM | Wednesday: 4:00 – 11:00 PM | Thursday: 4:00 – 11:00 PM | Friday: 3:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Saturday: 3:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Upscale cocktails and live piano in a multilevel downtown spot that feels like an occasion.

The Phoenix earns its name—a downtown anchor that's survived and thrived by doing the fancier things well. The martini list is taken seriously (order one and taste the difference), and when the pianist starts up in the evenings, the room fills with the kind of ambient energy that makes a Friday night feel intentional. The menu swings from classic to eclectic, the kind of place where you can trust the kitchen.

The Phoenix sits in Downtown Davenport, right in the heart of the QC's most walkable stretch. It's the rare restaurant-bar hybrid that works as both a date-night destination and a standing-reservation spot for locals who know when to show up. Once you've spent an evening there, you'll understand why it keeps showing up on our guide—it's one of the things downtown does best.

entities.name: The Renwick Mansion
entities.description:
A Victorian mansion where Davenport's gilded past comes alive in rooms full of period detail.

The Renwick Mansion stops you the moment you walk through the door — high ceilings, carved woodwork, and the kind of craftsmanship that feels impossible to replicate now. Tours move through rooms that actually tell stories: the parlor where you can imagine the social weight of a single evening, the kitchen with its old iron stove, the bedrooms with their original fixtures still catching light. It's the kind of place that makes you slow down and actually look at the architecture around you.

The Renwick sits in Davenport's residential core, a few minutes from downtown but far enough to feel like stepping into another era. It's exactly the kind of local landmark that gets overlooked — no hype, just genuine history — and once you've seen it, you understand why the QC's character runs deeper than the riverfront alone.

entities.name: The Ridge Social Drinkery
entities.hours: Monday: 12:00 PM – 2:00 AM | Tuesday: 12:00 PM – 2:00 AM | Wednesday: 12:00 PM – 2:00 AM | Thursday: 12:00 PM – 2:00 AM | Friday: 12:00 PM – 2:00 AM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM
entities.description:
Craft cocktails and live music in a space that actually feels like a neighborhood gathering spot.

The Ridge pulls off something harder than it looks: a bar that's serious about drinks without taking itself too seriously. The cocktail list leans thoughtful — house-made syrups, proper spirit selections, drinks that taste like someone cared — but you can also just order a beer and feel completely at home. On nights with live music, the whole room shifts into something warmer, the kind of crowd that actually listens instead of just talking over the band.

The Ridge sits in Davenport proper, anchoring the kind of neighborhood where people actually want to spend an evening. It's open early enough for an afternoon drink and late enough for the last call crowd, which means it works whether you're planning your night or ending it. This is exactly the place that keeps showing up in conversations about where the QC's nightlife is actually good — worth seeking out, worth coming back to.

entities.name: The Rust Belt
entities.description:
Industrial-chic bar where East Moline gathers over craft drinks and loud good times.

The Rust Belt wears its name honestly — exposed brick, reclaimed wood, and a vibe that feels pulled straight from a repurposed warehouse. The crowd is younger, louder, and here for the drinks and the energy; order a cocktail that actually tastes like someone cared, grab a spot near the front where you can watch the street, and settle in. It's the kind of place where you'll run into people you know, or wish you did.

The Rust Belt sits in East Moline, a neighborhood that's quietly becoming its own destination beyond the bigger QC names. It's far enough from the riverfront to feel like its own world, but close enough to reach in five minutes — and it's exactly the kind of bar that deserves a spot in our guides because it's where locals actually choose to spend their Friday nights, not where tourists are told to go.

entities.name: The Shameless Chocoholic
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Handmade chocolates and confections from a Bettendorf chocolatier obsessed with craft.

The Shameless Chocoholic is exactly what the name promises — a small, focused shop where the owner treats chocolate like it matters. You'll find handmade truffles, caramels, and seasonal confections that actually taste like something, not the mass-produced stuff. The place has that quiet intensity of a maker's studio; limited hours (closed Sundays and Mondays) mean they're not trying to be everywhere, just good.

Located in Bettendorf, The Shameless Chocoholic sits in a neighborhood of real, independent businesses — the kind of corner that rewards exploration. It's the sort of place you stumble onto and then tell people about, especially if you're looking for a gift that doesn't feel like an afterthought. Once you've tasted the difference, you'll understand why the ratings are so high.

entities.name: The Spotlight Theatre
entities.description:
A community theater where locals perform, and the whole QC shows up.

The Spotlight Theatre is where you'll find your neighbor's kid nailing a solo, a retired accountant stealing scenes, and the kind of earnest, unpolished performances that somehow hit harder than anything on a bigger stage. The intimate room means you're close enough to see the sweat and hear every line — there's no hiding, which is exactly the point. Whether it's a musical or a straight play, there's a warmth to productions here that comes from people doing this because they love it, not because it's their job.

The Spotlight Theatre sits in Moline, part of the QC's performing arts ecosystem and the kind of place that builds real community bonds. It's where you discover new sides of people you've known for years, and where a Thursday night out becomes a genuinely memorable evening. Once you've been, you'll start seeing cast members around town differently — and you'll keep coming back.

entities.name: The Tangled Wood
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM
entities.description: Lively hub in a sprawling site serving classic pub fare, plus an amphitheater, games & a rustic bar.

entities.name: The Treasure Chest
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Vintage finds and local oddities stacked floor to ceiling on Rock Island's quieter streets.

The Treasure Chest lives up to its name—walk in and you're immediately overwhelmed by the sheer density of stuff: vintage glassware, old vinyl records, furniture that's either a steal or a project depending on your perspective, and the kind of tchotchkes that feel like they have actual stories. The owners clearly curate rather than hoard, which means you're hunting through real treasures, not junk. Saturday mornings draw the serious thrifters; weekday afternoons are quieter and better for browsing without an audience.

The Treasure Chest sits on Rock Island's quieter blocks, away from the main commercial drag—the kind of place you stumble onto once and then keep returning to. It's become a reliable stop on the QC thrifting circuit, the sort of hole-in-the-wall that regulars protect because they know good vintage spots are disappearing everywhere. Once you've found it, you'll understand why.

entities.name: The UPS Store
entities.hours: Monday: 8:30 AM – 7:00 PM | Tuesday: 8:30 AM – 7:00 PM | Wednesday: 8:30 AM – 7:00 PM | Thursday: 8:30 AM – 7:00 PM | Friday: 8:30 AM – 7:00 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 3:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Reliable shipping and printing when you need it done right, no shortcuts.

The UPS Store on Avenue of the Cities handles the kind of tasks that keep small businesses and busy households running — precise box sealing, custom printing, certified shipping. The staff moves with the efficiency of people who've processed ten thousand packages, and they'll ask the right questions before you hand over your fragile goods. It's the place you go when a FedEx label won't cut it and you need someone who actually cares about getting it there intact.

The UPS Store sits on East Moline's main commercial strip, convenient to residential areas and easy to swing by during a supply run or lunch break. It's the kind of practical, unglamorous service that makes the QC function — not a destination in itself, but indispensable once you need it, and worth remembering when you do.

entities.name: The Vault Coffee & Cafe
entities.hours: Monday: 6:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Tuesday: 6:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Wednesday: 6:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Thursday: 6:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Friday: 6:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Saturday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Eldridge's morning anchor — coffee that tastes like someone actually cares.

The Vault opens early and closes by 2 p.m., which tells you everything: this is a place built for the breakfast and lunch crowd, not Instagram scrollers camping out till dark. The rating speaks for itself — 4.8 stars from people who know the difference between decent coffee and the real thing. Walk in expecting genuine hospitality and a menu that goes beyond the basics; you'll leave wondering why you haven't been making the drive to Eldridge sooner.

The Vault sits in Eldridge, a quieter corner of the QC where locals actually live their lives instead of perform them. It's the kind of neighborhood spot that builds its reputation slowly, one regular at a time, and doesn't need flashy marketing because word-of-mouth does the work. If you're looking for coffee with character and a room full of people who belong there, this is worth the short drive.

entities.name: The Vault Med Spa + Body Sculpting
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 3:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Aesthetic treatments and body sculpting in a Downtown space that actually feels like a retreat.

The Vault operates with the quiet confidence of a place that knows what it does well — injectables, laser work, non-invasive body contouring, the kind of treatments that don't require downtime but deliver real results. The staff clearly takes time with consultations; you're not rushed through a menu of services. It's the sort of spot where regulars book their next appointment before they leave, and where the 4.8 rating across 140 reviews isn't luck — it's consistency.

The Vault sits in Downtown Davenport, a neighborhood that's quietly becoming the QC's wellness hub. It's the kind of place that deserves a spot in our guides because it fills a real need without the sterile clinical feel you'd expect — just good work, good care, and people who show up for their appointments. Once you've been, you'll likely be back.

entities.name: Theo & Co.
entities.description:
Curated goods and vintage finds in a Downtown room that rewards browsing.

Theo & Co. feels like shopping at a friend's carefully edited closet—the kind of friend with good taste and an eye for both new and old. The space mixes contemporary pieces with genuine vintage, and there's real thought in how things are arranged; you'll find yourself lingering over details you didn't know you were looking for. It's the kind of shop where you come in for one thing and leave with something better.

Theo & Co. sits in Downtown Davenport, a neighborhood that's quietly become the QC's most interesting shopping district. It's walking distance from the riverfront and fits naturally into an afternoon of exploring the block—grab coffee nearby, spend an hour here, then keep moving. Once you've been, it shows up on your regular rotation.

entities.name: TMI - Total Maintenance, Inc. Heating, Air Conditioning, Plumbing
entities.hours: Monday: Open 24 hours | Tuesday: Open 24 hours | Wednesday: Open 24 hours | Thursday: Open 24 hours | Friday: Open 24 hours | Saturday: Open 24 hours | Sunday: Open 24 hours
entities.description:
The HVAC and plumbing crew the QC actually calls at 2 a.m.

TMI answers the phone 24/7—literally every day of the year—which means when your furnace dies in January or a pipe bursts on a Sunday, there's a real person on the line who can help within hours. Their Bettendorf base covers the whole region, and the reviews speak louder than any tagline: nearly 3,100 five-star ratings across Google and Yelp from people who've been grateful enough to come back and write about it.

Most of us don't think about HVAC and plumbing until something breaks, and that's exactly why TMI deserves a spot in our guides. They're the kind of essential local business that proves you don't need to be a restaurant or attraction to matter to the community—you just need to show up, do the work right, and be there when people need you most.

entities.name: Tony's Chicago Style Pizzeria
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM
entities.description: Cozy local parlor doling out deep dish pies, subs & Chicago-style hot dogs in modest digs.

entities.name: TPC Deere Run
entities.hours: Monday: 7:00 AM – 6:30 PM | Tuesday: 7:00 AM – 6:30 PM | Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 6:30 PM | Thursday: 7:00 AM – 6:30 PM | Friday: 7:00 AM – 6:30 PM | Saturday: 7:00 AM – 6:30 PM | Sunday: 7:00 AM – 6:30 PM
entities.description:
Golf course dining with views that stretch as far as your drive.

TPC Deere Run's restaurant catches the best light in late afternoon, when the fairways turn golden and you can actually see why people spend four hours out here. The menu leans into the clubhouse playbook—solid sandwiches, salads you don't feel guilty about, the kind of food that tastes better when you've already walked nine holes. Whether you're a member celebrating a good round or a non-golfer grabbing lunch with a view, the space feels unhurried in a way that's rare in the QC.

Out in Silvis, TPC Deere Run sits on some genuinely beautiful land—the kind of manicured green space that makes you remember this region has more to offer than the riverfront alone. It's the place that shows up on the calendar for tournaments and club events, but it's also approachable for a casual meal. Once you've sat on that patio, watched golfers work their way across the course, and eaten something better than you expected, you'll understand why it keeps pulling people back.

entities.name: Trade Mart
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 6:00 PM
entities.description:
A sprawling game shop where tabletop players actually gather and trade.

Trade Mart is the kind of place where you can spend an hour just browsing—rows of board games, card sets, miniatures, and dice in every color imaginable stacked floor to ceiling. The regulars know the staff by name, and there's almost always a game happening in the back corner, which tells you everything about the vibe here. Friday and Saturday nights stretch to 8 p.m., and that's when the real action happens.

Trade Mart sits in Moline, a short drive from the river and within reach of the rest of the QC's entertainment scene. It's become the go-to spot for the local tabletop gaming community—the kind of place that builds loyalty through consistency and real knowledge, not just inventory. Once you find it, you'll understand why it keeps showing up in conversations about where to spend a rainy afternoon or a weekend night.

entities.name: Trash Can Annie | Vintage, Styling & Photography
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Curated vintage finds and a stylist's eye for the unexpected.

Trash Can Annie doesn't feel like a typical thrift store — it's more like wandering through a stylist's personal collection, where every rack has been edited down to pieces that actually work. The front windows change seasonally, styled with the kind of care you'd see in a magazine spread, and inside you'll find everything from 90s denim to mid-century home goods, all chosen with obvious intention. The owner knows her inventory well enough to help you build a look, not just sell you a shirt.

Trash Can Annie anchors a stretch of Downtown Davenport that's quietly become the place to spend a Saturday afternoon — close enough to the riverfront for a full day out, but with real character that chain stores can't touch. It's the kind of shop that rewards browsing, and the kind of place locals point friends toward when they want to find something nobody else will have. Once you've picked through the racks, you'll be back.

entities.name: Trattoria Tiramisu
entities.hours: Monday: 5:00 – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 5:00 – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 5:00 – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 5:00 – 9:00 PM | Friday: 5:00 – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 5:00 – 9:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Red-checkered tables and handmade pasta that tastes like someone's nonna is in the kitchen.

Trattoria Tiramisu feels like stepping into a corner of Italy that's been transplanted to Bettendorf—all warm lighting, simple wooden chairs, and the kind of straightforward Italian cooking that doesn't need to apologize. The pasta is the draw here; order the lasagna or any of their house specials and you'll taste the difference between careful preparation and assembly-line cooking. The bar runs the length of one wall, and regulars know to linger over a Chianti or an Aperol Spritz while the kitchen takes its time.

Trattoria Tiramisu sits in Bettendorf, the QC's quieter, more residential side—a neighborhood where good restaurants tend to stay good because people keep coming back. With nearly 800 five-star reviews, this is the kind of place that shows up on our guides because it's earned its reputation through consistency, not flash. If you're looking for honest Italian food where the effort shows, this is the table to book.

entities.name: Treehouse Pub & Eatery
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM
entities.description:
Craft beer and wood-fired pizza in a sprawling Bettendorf space that feels like home.

Treehouse sprawls across reclaimed wood and open air—the kind of place where you can disappear into a corner booth or claim a spot on the deck and watch the evening unfold. The pizza arrives properly charred, the beer list leans local and thoughtful, and the kitchen treats American comfort food like it actually matters. Weekends pack the place, but there's enough room that you never feel squeezed.

Treehouse sits in Bettendorf, the QC's most polished suburb, positioned perfectly for a Friday night out that doesn't require a river crossing. It's the rare spot that works equally well for a casual dinner, a long beer-fueled evening with friends, or a place to land after catching something nearby. Once you've been, you'll keep coming back.

entities.name: Tuggers
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: Closed | Thursday: 4:00 – 9:00 PM | Friday: 4:00 – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 8:00 PM
entities.description:
A dive bar anchor where Port Byron gathers over wings and cheap beer.

Tuggers has the worn-in comfort of a place that's been doing the same thing for decades—wood-paneled walls, a solid bar, the kind of lighting that doesn't ask too many questions. The wings are legitimately good, crispy and sauced without pretension, and the beer list skews toward what people actually want to drink. Thursday through Sunday it fills with regulars who know the bartenders by name and the pool table in back by memory.

Port Byron doesn't get much attention in QC conversation, but Tuggers is exactly why it should—a neighborhood bar that serves its community without apology or theme nights. It's a short drive from the river towns and worth the trip if you want to feel like a local, not a tourist passing through. This is the kind of place that anchors a town.

entities.name: Twin Oaks Shopping Center
entities.hours: Monday: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Tuesday: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Friday: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM
entities.description:
A straightforward strip center where Silvis comes to get things done.

Twin Oaks doesn't fuss with atmosphere — it's a no-nonsense lineup of everyday stops arranged around a practical parking lot. The mix is honest: grocery runs, quick errands, and the kind of places you visit because you need them, not because you're looking for an experience. Open early and late (6 AM to 10 PM every day), it's built for people with actual schedules.

Twin Oaks sits in Silvis, the working part of the QC where neighbors know what they want and expect it to be accessible. It's the kind of local anchor that doesn't make our guides for ambiance or discovery — it makes them because it's genuinely useful, and because the QC is as much about reliable everyday spaces as it is about destination spots.

entities.name: Twin Span Brewing
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM
entities.description:
Bettendorf's brewery where the beer is solid and the crowd is local.

Twin Span fills that rare middle ground — polished enough to feel like a destination, comfortable enough that regulars rotate through on weeknights without fanfare. The taps rotate with thought, the space is open and bright, and there's room to actually have a conversation, which matters. Friday and Saturday nights pull in the broader crowd; weekday afternoons are quieter, better for settling in with a pint and figuring out what they've got on.

Twin Span sits in Bettendorf, the QC's quieter, more residential east side — far enough from downtown bustle to feel like a neighborhood gathering spot, close enough that it's only a few minutes across the river from Davenport proper. It's the kind of place that builds a loyal following without needing hype, and that's exactly why it matters on our guides: it's where real people go to drink good beer on an ordinary Thursday.

entities.name: Twisted Paddle Brewery
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 3:00 – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 3:00 – 10:00 PM | Friday: 3:00 – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 3:00 – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 3:00 – 9:00 PM
entities.description:
Craft beer and kitchen food in a converted DeWitt garage space.

Twisted Paddle feels like it started in someone's dream and actually got built — a small-batch brewery operation that doesn't pretend to be bigger than it is. The space has that honest garage energy: exposed bones, good beer brewed steps away from where you're sitting, and a kitchen that takes its food seriously enough to complement the pour, not just fill the void. Wednesday through Saturday nights draw a steady crowd of people who know the difference between a brewery that's trying and one that's got something to prove.

DeWitt's quietly becoming a destination for people willing to drive a few minutes outside the core QC for quality — and Twisted Paddle is exactly why. It's far enough from downtown Davenport to feel like its own thing, but close enough that it slots easily into an evening of real eating and drinking. The kind of place that shows up on our guides because it's doing something genuine.

entities.name: Two Men and a Truck Moving and Storage
entities.hours: Monday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Tuesday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 8:00 AM – 1:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Local movers who actually show up on time and treat your stuff like their own.

Two Men and a Truck has built serious goodwill in Davenport by doing the fundamentals right — the crew arrives when they say they will, wraps your furniture carefully, and doesn't charge surprise fees at the end. Whether you're moving across town or across the river to Illinois, they handle apartment-sized jobs and full houses with the same professionalism. Saturday morning appointments fill up fast, which tells you something about the reputation.

Moving is one of those necessary tasks that can either stress you out or feel handled — and in the Quad Cities, Two Men and a Truck is the name that comes up repeatedly when neighbors ask neighbors for a recommendation. They're the kind of reliable local business that makes the practical side of living here easier, which is why they belong in our guides.

entities.name: TYCOGA Winery & Distillery
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 7:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 AM | Sunday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM
entities.description:
Wine and spirits made in-house, poured in a quiet corner of DeWitt.

TYCOGA feels like a discovery — the kind of place you stumble onto and immediately wonder why you haven't been sooner. The tasting room is unhurried, built around their own wines and distilled spirits, with the kind of care that comes from actually making what you're drinking. Weekends draw a steady crowd, but there's still room to linger over a pour and ask real questions about what goes into their bottles.

DeWitt sits just outside the immediate Quad Cities sprawl, making TYCOGA a worthy detour for anyone looking to leave the main drag behind. It's the kind of neighborhood spot that rewards curiosity — a place where locals know to go when they want something more intentional than a standard night out. Once you've found it, you'll find yourself steering friends and visitors this direction.

entities.name: Ultimate Fitness
entities.hours: Monday: Open 24 hours | Tuesday: Open 24 hours | Wednesday: Open 24 hours | Thursday: Open 24 hours | Friday: Open 24 hours | Saturday: Open 24 hours | Sunday: Open 24 hours
entities.description:
A 24-hour Bettendorf gym that actually feels like your gym.

Ultimate Fitness opens at midnight and never closes—which means 5 a.m. lifters share the floor with night-shift workers squeezing in a session at 2 a.m. The place has the kind of equipment spread that doesn't force you to wait, and the regulars nod. You'll find serious weightlifters, people training for something specific, and folks just trying to move their body before work.

Ultimate Fitness sits in Bettendorf, the QC's quieter, residential east side—close enough to I-74 to be convenient, far enough from downtown to feel like a neighborhood spot. It's the kind of gym that earns repeat visitors and high ratings because it does the fundamentals right: equipment that works, hours that bend to your life, and a community that doesn't make you feel like you're in the way.

entities.name: UP Skybar & Lounge
entities.hours: Monday: 4:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Tuesday: 4:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Wednesday: 4:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Thursday: 4:00 PM – 12:00 AM | Friday: 4:00 PM – 2:00 AM | Saturday: 2:00 PM – 2:00 AM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Rooftop views and craft cocktails in the heart of downtown Davenport.

UP Skybar sits several stories above the street, and that elevation matters — you're looking out over the Mississippi, the skyline, the whole QC spread below you. The cocktails are the draw: built with care, not shortcuts, and the bartenders know their riffs on the classics. Weekend nights pack in a mix of locals and visitors, all of them angled toward those windows and the view that justifies the climb.

UP Skybar is downtown Davenport's best-kept vantage point, the kind of place that reminds you the river city has always had its eyes on the horizon. It anchors the evening scene — close to the District, walkable from the riverfront, and the rare spot where you can nurse a drink and actually see what you're celebrating. Once you find it, you'll keep coming back, especially on nights when the light is good and the whole river glows.

entities.name: Vander Veer Botanical Park
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM | Sunday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM
entities.description:
140 years of roses, greenhouses, and quiet afternoons in Davenport.

Vander Veer feels like the kind of place that's been breathing the same air since 1885 — because it has. The rose garden is the real draw, especially in bloom season when the smell hits you before you round the corner. Inside the greenhouse, humid air and the smell of soil remind you why people used to spend entire afternoons just walking around looking at things growing.

Vander Veer sits on the northwest side of Davenport, far enough from downtown to feel like genuine escape but close enough that it's never a production to get there. It's the kind of destination — old, free or nearly free, genuinely beautiful — that shows up on every list of "things locals actually do" because locals actually do go there, season after season, year after year.

entities.name: VanDerGinst Law - Personal Injury Lawyers
entities.hours: Monday: Open 24 hours | Tuesday: Open 24 hours | Wednesday: Open 24 hours | Thursday: Open 24 hours | Friday: Open 24 hours | Saturday: Open 24 hours | Sunday: Open 24 hours
entities.description:
Personal injury lawyers who actually answer the phone at 2 a.m.

VanDerGinst handles the calls nobody else wants to take — and they take them around the clock. After a car wreck on I-80 or a slip at work, you need someone who understands QC streets and won't make you wait until Monday morning. Their Moline office has handled hundreds of cases, and the 4.7-star rating reflects what happens when a firm treats late-night emergencies like they matter.

VanDerGinst sits in Moline, accessible and reliable when you need it most. This is the kind of place our guide recommends not because it's flashy, but because locals trust it — and in personal injury law, trust is everything.

entities.name: Vibrant Arena at The MARK
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description: 12,000-seat venue showcasing local sports teams, live concerts, musicals & other events.

entities.name: Vibrant Coffeehouse + Kitchen
entities.hours: Monday: 7:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Tuesday: 7:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Thursday: 7:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Friday: 7:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 7:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Sunday: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM
entities.description:
Moline's bright-corner spot for coffee, breakfast, and a real seat at the counter.

Vibrant lives up to its name — the space is clean-lined and welcoming, the kind of place where you can actually hear your friend across the table. The coffee is straightforward and reliable, the pastries are fresh, and the kitchen does breakfast right: eggs cooked the way you order them, toast that doesn't apologize. Regulars camp out with laptops in the morning; by mid-morning families drift in for weekend breakfast.

Vibrant sits in Moline, just across the river from the District and within easy reach of the Rock Island waterfront. It's become the kind of steady, unpretentious neighborhood spot that makes a QC community feel like home — the place you return to because the coffee's good, the people behind the counter remember you, and there's always a table when you need one.

entities.name: Vicki's Rocks, Readings & Books
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 4:00 PM
entities.description:
Crystals, tarot, and shelves of books in a Moline shop that feels like home.

Vicki's is the kind of place where you walk in looking for one thing and leave with three. The front windows glow with amethyst geodes and polished stones—some small enough to slip in your pocket, others substantial enough to anchor a shelf. The books section runs deep: tarot decks, spirituality, occult history, and the kind of worn paperbacks that smell like someone's actual life. It's unhurried in a way that matters; Vicki's regulars come back because they're treated like they belong there.

Vicki's Rocks, Readings & Books sits in Moline, a part of the QC that's quietly building its own character away from the riverfront bustle. It's the kind of specialty shop that anchors a neighborhood—the place locals know about and visitors discover by word of mouth. Once you've stepped inside, you'll understand why it shows up again and again on our guides: it's genuine, it's welcoming, and there's nothing else quite like it in the Quad Cities.

entities.name: Village Inn
entities.hours: Monday: 7:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Tuesday: 7:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 7:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Friday: 7:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Saturday: 7:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Sunday: 7:00 AM – 8:00 PM
entities.description:
Homestyle comfort food that's been feeding Moline families for decades.

Village Inn is the kind of place where the waitstaff remembers regulars' names and the chicken-fried steak arrives golden and substantial. The dining room has that warm, lived-in feeling of a restaurant that's earned its loyal following — booths worn smooth from years of Sunday dinners, pie cases that actually move, coffee that stays hot. It's unpretentious in the best way: you come here because the food is honest and the portions don't apologize.

Village Inn sits on 53rd Street in Moline, a straightforward neighborhood spot without any attempt at trendiness or reinvention. It's the kind of reliable anchor that shows up on best-of lists because locals keep coming back — not for Instagram moments, but for food that tastes like home. If you're looking for what the Quad Cities actually eat when nobody's watching, this is it.

entities.name: Village of East Davenport
entities.description:
A quiet east-side neighborhood where tree-lined streets feel genuinely removed from the city.

The Village of East Davenport is where Davenport slows down — brick homes set back from wide boulevards, mature oaks creating actual shade, the kind of neighborhood where you notice the details of front porches and garden gates. It's residential and deliberately unhurried, a place to walk without purpose or bike toward the river on a Sunday morning. There's a realness here that feels intentional; this isn't somewhere trying to be discovered so much as somewhere people choose to stay.

East Davenport sits on the quieter side of the city, close enough to Downtown and the riverfront to matter but far enough to feel like its own thing. It's the kind of neighborhood we come back to because it's where actual Quad Citizens live — not a destination in the typical sense, but a necessary counterpoint to the busier pockets of the QC. If you're looking to understand what makes the area home to people who've chosen to stay, this is where to start.

entities.name: Vintage Rose Jewelry
entities.description:
Vintage and estate jewelry with the care of someone who actually knows the stones.

Vintage Rose feels like stepping into someone's carefully curated collection — cases of estate rings, delicate gold chains, and genuine vintage pieces you won't find at the mall. The owner takes time with each person who walks in, asking what you're looking for and why, which makes the whole experience feel less transactional and more like advice from someone who's spent years learning the difference between a good stone and a great one. Whether you're hunting for an engagement ring with actual history or just a specific Art Deco bracelet you've had in mind, there's a real expertise here.

Vintage Rose sits on Rock Island's main stretch, close enough to the riverfront and walkable from some of the area's best spots. It's the kind of neighborhood shop that deserves a trip if you're serious about jewelry — the kind where the person behind the counter remembers what you were looking at last time, and where ratings like 4.7 stars aren't inflated but earned through genuine attention to detail.

entities.name: Virtual Ventures
entities.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 6:00 PM
entities.description:
VR arcade where the QC's competitive streak comes out to play.

Virtual Ventures is where you shed the real world for an afternoon — slip on a headset, grab the controllers, and suddenly you're scaling impossible heights or locked in a firefight alongside friends. The space fills with the kind of energy that only happens when someone nails a perfect shot in a zombie game or discovers they're actually good at rhythm games. Whether you're here solo, with your partner trying to out-score each other, or corralling a birthday group, there's enough variety that nobody's left watching from the sidelines.

Virtual Ventures sits in Davenport and fills a real gap in the QC's entertainment landscape — it's the kind of place that keeps people from driving to Cedar Rapids or Iowa City for an afternoon. The crew running it knows their regulars, remembers your high scores, and keeps the equipment maintained and current. Once you've spent an hour here, you'll be back.

entities.name: Von Maur
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 6:00 PM
entities.description:
A Midwestern department store that actually feels like home.

Von Maur carries the brands you're looking for—J.Crew, Calvin Klein, Dooney & Bourke—but what sets it apart is the unhurried pace and the staff who seem genuinely interested in helping you find what fits. The store is bright and open, with room to breathe between racks, and there's a quiet dignity to the place that's missing from the big-box alternatives. It's the kind of shopping trip where you might actually enjoy yourself.

Von Maur anchors Davenport's retail landscape and has earned deep loyalty from locals who appreciate its no-pressure approach and consistent quality. It's the kind of familiar, reliable destination that weaves into the fabric of how people actually shop in the Quad Cities—not a tourist draw, but a place that belongs here.

entities.name: Wake Brewing
entities.hours: Monday: 3:00 – 10:00 PM | Tuesday: 3:00 – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 3:00 – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 3:00 – 10:00 PM | Friday: 3:00 – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 8:00 PM
entities.description:
Rock Island's brewpub where the beer list actually rewards curiosity.

Wake Brewing pulls off the harder trick—good beer *and* good food, no compromise on either end. The space has that lived-in brewpub warmth: wood, brass, the kind of place where you recognize faces by your third visit. Their house beers rotate with the seasons, but it's the depth of the list that keeps people coming back; you can spend months working through their offerings without hitting a dud.

Wake sits on Rock Island's slowly-reviving stretch, the kind of neighborhood spot that becomes a destination once word gets out. It's the anchor tenant for a quieter corner of the QC—less obvious than Downtown Davenport, more rewarding if you're willing to wander. This is exactly the place our guides exist to find: solid, unpretentious, worth the trip.

entities.name: Wallace's Garden Center
entities.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Thursday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Friday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Saturday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM | Sunday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM
entities.description:
The go-to for serious gardeners and casual plant parents alike.

Wallace's has the kind of depth that makes you linger — rows of perennials organized by season, a greenhouse section that actually feels like walking into humidity and green, and staff who can tell you exactly which hostas handle shade. Whether you're after a single houseplant or rebuilding a whole bed, the selection beats the big-box runs, and the prices reflect that they're not trying to undercut anyone. Saturdays draw the neighborhood gardeners who've been coming back for years, the kind of regulars who know which section just got restocked.

Wallace's anchors the Bettendorf shopping landscape — close enough to the river neighborhoods to be a natural stop on a weekend errand run, far enough from downtown that it feels like a destination rather than an afterthought. It's the kind of place that shows up in our guides because it does one thing well and has earned the trust of the community that shops there, which is worth more than novelty.

entities.name: West End Gardens Florist
entities.hours: Monday: 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Tuesday: 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Wednesday: 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Thursday: 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Friday: 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Saturday: 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM | Sunday: 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM
entities.description:
A full-service florist where arrangements feel like they're made by someone who actually cares.

West End Gardens has the kind of depth you don't expect to find — walk in and you're surrounded by fresh stems, potted plants, and the quiet focus of florists who know their craft. They'll listen to what you're after (a wedding, an apology, just Tuesday) and build something that doesn't look like it came from a template. Open seven days a week, which means when you need flowers at 5 p.m. on a Saturday, they're still there.

Tucked into West Davenport, West End Gardens sits far enough from downtown to feel like a neighborhood spot, but it's worth the drive across town or an easy stop if you're already out that way. The kind of local business that gets trusted with the moments that matter — and keeps earning that trust with every arrangement that leaves the shop.

entities.name: Wharton Field House
entities.description:
A storied arena where the QC gathers to watch its teams compete.

Wharton Field House carries the weight of decades—the kind of venue where you can feel the history in the rafters and the roar of the crowd echoing off concrete walls. Whether it's a high school tournament, college match, or local competition, the energy here is genuine; these are your neighbors' kids playing, your community's teams fighting for wins. The bleachers fill with families, rival school sections split by invisible lines, and the smell of stadium popcorn mixing with the particular gym smell that never quite leaves.

Wharton sits in Moline's Willard neighborhood, anchoring the athletic heart of the QC. It's the kind of place that shows up on weekends and weeknights alike, a gathering point that connects you to the area's deep sports culture—easy enough to add to an evening if you're catching a game, or worth the trip on its own if you're invested in watching the QC's young athletes compete.

entities.name: Whiskey Stop
entities.hours: Monday: Closed | Tuesday: Closed | Wednesday: 3:00 PM – 1:00 AM | Thursday: 3:00 PM – 1:00 AM | Friday: 2:30 PM – 3:00 AM | Saturday: 12:00 PM – 3:00 AM | Sunday: 12:00 PM – 12:00 AM
entities.description:
A serious whiskey bar where the pours are generous and the crowd knows what they're drinking.

Whiskey Stop lives up to its name—the back wall is lined with bottles that would make a collector pause, and the bartenders actually know their stuff. Friday and Saturday nights the place fills with people who've made the drive from across the QC for a drink that's made right, not rushed. The vibe is comfortable but intentional; you can have a quiet conversation at the bar or settle into the energy of a weekend crowd that's there because they chose to be.

Whiskey Stop sits in East Moline, just far enough from the main cluster that it feels like a destination rather than a pit stop. It's the kind of place that builds a loyal following—people return because the whiskey selection is real, the bartenders remember you, and there's no pretense masking what should be a simple pleasure: a good drink, well-made. If you're serious about spirits or just want a night out where quality actually matters, it's worth the trip.

entities.name: Whitey's Ice Cream
entities.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM | Sunday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM
entities.description: Longtime chain offering ice cream & frozen yogurt, known for its upside-down shakes & malts.

entities.name: WHOLLY GRINDS Coffee Shop
entities.hours: Monday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Tuesday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Wednesday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Thursday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Friday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Saturday: 7:00 AM – 2:00 PM | Sunday: Closed
entities.description:
Morning-focused coffee shop where the regulars outnumber the tourists.

Wholly Grinds opens early and closes by 2 p.m., a rhythm that signals serious coffee people running the show. The menu skews simple — good espresso drinks, pastries, maybe a sandwich — but the real draw is consistency: the same barista remembering your order, the same hum of conversation between the same faces. It's the kind of place that doesn't need to be fancy because it's already earned trust.

Wholly Grinds sits in Rock Island, a neighborhood that rewards the kind of attention this coffee shop demands. Its limited hours mean it's built for intentional visits, not drop-ins, and the 4.8 rating across 111 reviews suggests people have made the trip enough times to know it's worth it. This is a place that belongs in our guides because it refuses to be anything but exactly what it is.

entities.name: Wide River Winery Village of East Davenport
entities.hours: Monday: 12:00 – 6:00 PM | Tuesday: 12:00 – 6:00 PM | Wednesday: 12:00 – 6:00 PM | Thursday: 12:00 – 6:00 PM | Friday: 12:00 – 8:00 PM | Saturday: 12:00 – 8:00 PM | Sunday: 12:00 – 6:00 PM
entities.description:
Wine tasting in a converted East Davenport space with real neighborhood warmth.

Wide River Winery Village feels less like a typical tasting room and more like someone's living room — if that living room happened to pour solid local and regional wines. The vibe is unhurried; people linger over glasses, chat with staff who actually know what they're pouring, and drift between tastings without the velvet-rope formality you'd expect. Friday and Saturday nights stretch to 8 p.m., which is when the place finds its rhythm: the kind of evening where you meant to stay for one glass and suddenly it's two hours later.

Wide River Winery Village sits in East Davenport, a neighborhood quietly finding its footing as a gathering spot — away from downtown's bustle but close enough to walk to dinner and drinks. It's the kind of place that shows up on our guides because it does one thing genuinely well and does it without pretense. Once you've found it, you'll keep coming back.

entities.name: Wildcat Den State Park
entities.hours: Monday: 4:00 AM – 10:30 PM | Tuesday: 4:00 AM – 10:30 PM | Wednesday: 4:00 AM – 10:30 PM | Thursday: 4:00 AM – 10:30 PM | Friday: 4:00 AM – 10:30 PM | Saturday: 4:00 AM – 10:30 PM | Sunday: 4:00 AM – 10:30 PM
entities.description: Bluff- & creek-lined hiking trails, a historic grist mill, picnic shelters & primitive campgrounds.

entities.name: WIREONE Electric - Davenport
entities.hours: Monday: Open 24 hours | Tuesday: Open 24 hours | Wednesday: Open 24 hours | Thursday: Open 24 hours | Friday: Open 24 hours | Saturday: Open 24 hours | Sunday: Open 24 hours
entities.description:
Industrial-grade electrical supply, open round-the-clock for the trades.

WireOne is the kind of place contractors know by heart — fluorescent-lit aisles stocked with wire, breakers, conduit, and everything else that keeps the QC's buildings running. The staff moves with the ease of people who've answered the same questions a thousand times and actually know the answers. Whether you're pulling permit work at 2 a.m. or restocking a job site before dawn, the lights are on and someone's behind the counter.

WireOne sits in Downtown Davenport, serving the trades and DIYers who keep the region's infrastructure humming. It's the kind of essential, unadorned place that belongs in a real community guide — not for tourists, but for the people who actually live and work here. Once you know it exists, you'll find yourself sending friends there, or stopping by yourself when a project suddenly needs parts at an odd hour.
